title: Kafka on the Shore
author: Haruki Murakami
synopsis: A fifteen-year-old runaway and an old man who talks to cats drift toward each other across a Japan thick with omens, libraries, and fish falling from the sky.

title: Special Topics in Calamity Physics
author: Marisha Pessl
synopsis: A film professor's daughter narrates her senior year — and a suspicious death — through a syllabus of the Great Books.

title: American Gods
author: Neil Gaiman
synopsis: Fresh out of prison, Shadow is hired by the enigmatic Mr. Wednesday and pulled into a brewing war between America's old gods and its new ones.

title: Napoleon: A Life
author: Andrew Roberts
synopsis: A sweeping single-volume life of Napoleon Bonaparte, drawn from his thirty-three thousand surviving letters.

title: The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-time
author: Mark Haddon
synopsis: Christopher Boone, who understands the world through logic and prime numbers, investigates the death of his neighbour's dog.

title: Creativity, Inc.
author: Ed Catmull
synopsis: Pixar's co-founder on building — and protecting — a culture where creative people can do their best work.

title: Slaughterhouse-Five
author: Kurt Vonnegut
synopsis: Billy Pilgrim has come unstuck in time, shuttling between Dresden, suburbia, and the planet Tralfamadore. So it goes.

title: The Outsiders
author: S. E. Hinton
synopsis: Ponyboy Curtis lives on the Greaser side of town, until one violent night blurs every line he thought he knew.

title: Breakfast of Champions
author: Kurt Vonnegut
synopsis: A midwestern car dealer becomes convinced that a pulp science-fiction writer's novel is addressed to him personally.

title: Between the World and Me
author: Ta-Nehisi Coates
synopsis: A letter from a father to his son about what it means to inhabit a Black body in America.

title: The Autobiography of Red
author: Anne Carson
synopsis: A novel in verse: the red-winged monster Geryon falls for Herakles, armed with a camera and Greek myth.

title: The Reluctant Fundamentalist
author: Mohsin Hamid
synopsis: Over one evening in a Lahore café, a Princeton-educated Pakistani tells an American stranger the story of his disillusionment.

title: Coraline
author: Neil Gaiman
synopsis: Behind a small door in her new flat, Coraline finds another mother with black-button eyes who never wants her to leave.

title: The Book Thief
author: Markus Zusak
synopsis: Narrated by Death: in Nazi Germany, Liesel Meminger steals books and shares them through the air raids.

title: Hard Boiled Wonderland and the End of the World
author: Haruki Murakami
synopsis: Two narratives — a data-encrypting Calcutec in Tokyo and a dreamreader in a walled town — converge at the end of the world.

title: Zero to One
author: Peter Thiel
synopsis: Notes on startups: why building something genuinely new — going from zero to one — beats copying what already works.

title: The Righteous Mind
author: Jonathan Haidt
synopsis: A moral psychologist on why good people are divided by politics and religion — intuition first, strategic reasoning second.

title: The Motorcycle Diaries
author: Ernesto Che Guevara
synopsis: A young medical student's road diary of an eight-month motorcycle journey up the spine of South America.

title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
author: Douglas Adams
synopsis: Seconds before Earth is demolished, Arthur Dent is rescued by a researcher for the galaxy's most remarkable book. Don't panic.

title: The Alienist
author: Caleb Carr
synopsis: In 1896 New York, a pioneering psychologist assembles a secret team to profile a serial killer.

title: Elon Musk: Tesla, SpaceX, and the Quest for a Fantastic Future
author: Ashlee Vance
synopsis: How a bullied South African kid bet two fortunes on electric cars and reusable rockets.

title: A Country Doctor's Notebook
author: Mikhail Bulgakov
synopsis: Autobiographical stories of a newly minted doctor posted to a remote Russian village.

title: Song of Solomon
author: Toni Morrison
synopsis: Milkman Dead's journey south in search of family gold becomes a search for his own name and history.

title: The Just City
author: Jo Walton
synopsis: The goddess Athene gathers thinkers from across history to build Plato's Republic for real, on an island.

title: Americanah
author: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie
synopsis: Ifemelu and Obinze leave military-era Nigeria for America and England; years later, love pulls them home.

title: The Philosopher Kings
author: Jo Walton
synopsis: Twenty years after the Just City fractured, Apollo — living as a mortal — sails in pursuit of a stolen future.

title: The Kite Runner
author: Khaled Hosseini
synopsis: Amir's betrayal of his childhood friend Hassan haunts him from Kabul to California — and back.

title: Queen Elizabeth
author: Anonymous
synopsis: A life of Queen Elizabeth I, from princess in peril to Gloriana.

title: The Autobiography of Benjamin Franklin
author: Benjamin Franklin
synopsis: Printer, inventor, founding father: Franklin's own account of his rise, written as advice to his son.

title: The Art of Public Speaking
author: Dale Carnegie & J. Berg Esenwein
synopsis: The classic training manual on delivery, confidence, and holding an audience.

title: Bushido: The Soul of Japan
author: Inazo Nitobe
synopsis: An explanation of the samurai ethical code — rectitude, courage, honour — written for Western readers.

title: How to Live on Twenty-Four Hours a Day
author: Arnold Bennett
synopsis: An Edwardian classic on reclaiming your hours for reading, thought, and living.

title: Night and Day
author: Virginia Woolf
synopsis: Katharine Hilbery, granddaughter of a great poet, weighs marriage, work, and what a woman's life may hold.

title: On Heroes, Hero-Worship, and The Heroic in History
author: Thomas Carlyle
synopsis: Six lectures arguing that history is shaped by great men — prophets, poets, and kings.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J. D. Salinger
synopsis: Expelled again, Holden Caulfield wanders New York for a weekend, allergic to phonies and mourning his brother.

title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
author: Mark Twain
synopsis: Huck fakes his death and rafts down the Mississippi with the runaway Jim, dodging feuds, con men, and his own conscience.

title: A Thousand and One Nights
author: Anonymous
synopsis: Scheherazade postpones her execution by telling the king a story every night — a frame holding hundreds more.

title: The Trial
author: Franz Kafka
synopsis: Josef K. is arrested one morning without being told his crime, and the proceedings never really begin — or end.

title: The Metamorphosis
author: Franz Kafka
synopsis: Traveling salesman Gregor Samsa wakes transformed into a monstrous insect and becomes a burden his family slowly comes to resent. A parable of alienation, duty, and the body.

title: Cousin Basilo
author: José Maria de Eça de Queirós
synopsis: In Lisbon, a bored wife's affair with her cousin hands her maid the means for blackmail.

title: The Miner
author: Natsume Sōseki
synopsis: A drifting Tokyo youth descends into a copper mine — and into an unsparing study of his own mind.

title: The Tale of Genji
author: Murasaki Shikibu
synopsis: The shining prince Genji's loves and losses at the Heian court — often called the world's first novel.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F Scott Fitzgerald
synopsis: Nick Carraway's summer among West Egg's new money and the green light at the end of Daisy's dock.

title: Romeo and Juliet
author: William Shakespeare
synopsis: Two star-crossed lovers of Verona defy their feuding houses.

title: Hamlet
author: William Shakespeare
synopsis: The Prince of Denmark feigns madness to avenge his father's murder.

title: The Republic
author: Plato
synopsis: Socrates and his interlocutors build an ideal city to discover the nature of justice, arriving at the philosopher-king and the allegory of the cave. The cornerstone of Western political philosophy.

title: Poor Richard's Almanack
author: Benjamin Franklin
synopsis: Maxims, weather, and wit from Franklin's alter ego, Richard Saunders.

title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland
author: Lewis Carroll
synopsis: A girl follows a waistcoated rabbit down a hole into a nonsensical world of talking creatures, riddles, and a murderous queen. The masterpiece of literary nonsense.

title: Paradise Lost
author: John Milton
synopsis: Milton's epic of the Fall: Satan's rebellion and man's first disobedience.

title: Plutarch's Lives
author: Plutarch
synopsis: Paired biographies of noble Greeks and Romans.

title: The Pilgrim's Progress
author: John Bunyan
synopsis: Christian's allegorical journey from the City of Destruction to the Celestial City.

title: War and Peace
author: Leo Tolstoy
synopsis: Spanning the Napoleonic invasions of Russia, the novel follows several aristocratic families whose loves, ambitions, and spiritual searches unfold against the vast, impersonal tides of war and history.

title: Anna Karenina
author: Leo Tolstoy
synopsis: A married Petersburg aristocrat's passionate affair with Count Vronsky destroys her social standing and life, counterpointed by Levin's earnest search for meaning in love and land.

title: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
synopsis: Victor Frankenstein animates a creature from dead matter, then abandons it — and the being's lonely education and vengeance drive them both to ruin across the ice.

title: Northanger Abbey
author: Jane Austen
synopsis: Catherine Morland, her head full of Gothic novels, mistakes an ordinary English abbey for a house of dark secrets — a coming-of-age comedy about reading the world too literally.

title: Little Women
author: Louisa May Alcott
synopsis: The four March sisters — Meg, Jo, Beth, and Amy — grow up in genteel poverty during the Civil War, chasing love, art, and independence.

title: David Copperfield
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: The orphaned David makes his way from cruelty and poverty to authorship, meeting Micawber, Steerforth, Uriah Heep, and the steadfast Peggottys along the road.

title: The Moonstone
author: Wilkie Collins
synopsis: A cursed Indian diamond vanishes from a Yorkshire country house on the night of a birthday, and a chorus of narrators — a butler, a spinster, a doctor's assistant — circle the truth.

title: Jane Eyre
author: Charlotte Brontë
synopsis: An orphaned governess of fierce conscience falls for her brooding employer, Mr. Rochester — and discovers the secret locked in Thornfield's attic before she can accept him.

title: Persuasion
author: Jane Austen
synopsis: Persuaded years ago to reject the man she loved, Anne Elliot gets a second chance when Captain Wentworth returns from the sea, still wounded and still proud.

title: A Study in Scarlet
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson meet and take their first case together — a revenge killing whose roots run back to the Mormon frontier of Utah.

title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
author: Oscar Wilde
synopsis: A beautiful young man stays youthful while his portrait ages and rots with his sins — Wilde's fable of aestheticism and corruption.

title: Emma
author: Jane Austen
synopsis: Emma Woodhouse, handsome, clever, and rich, meddles in everyone's matches but her own — until she learns the cost of her cleverness.

title: Sense and Sensibility
author: Jane Austen
synopsis: Two sisters — the prudent Elinor and the romantic Marianne — navigate love and loss and learn to temper feeling with sense.

title: The Adventures of Tom Sawyer
author: Mark Twain
synopsis: A mischievous boy in a sleepy Mississippi town whitewashes fences, courts Becky Thatcher, witnesses a murder, and finds buried treasure.

title: Anne of Green Gables
author: L. M. Montgomery
synopsis: A talkative red-haired orphan is sent by mistake to elderly siblings on Prince Edward Island, and wins over a whole community.

title: Mansfield Park
author: Jane Austen
synopsis: Timid, poor Fanny Price grows up among wealthy relatives at Mansfield Park and holds to her principles as flirtation and scandal unsettle the household.

title: The Sign of the Four
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson investigate a mysterious set of pearls and a hidden treasure tied to a secret pact among four convicts.

title: The Hound of the Baskervilles
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: Holmes and Watson investigate a spectral hound said to haunt a moorland family, separating legend from a very human plot. The most famous of the Sherlock Holmes novels.

title: Dracula
author: Bram Stoker
synopsis: An epistolary novel in which a group led by Professor Van Helsing hunts the vampire Count Dracula after he preys on their circle in England.

title: The Woman in White
author: Wilkie Collins
synopsis: A sensation novel in which drawing-master Walter Hartright uncovers a conspiracy of stolen identity and fortune surrounding a mysterious woman dressed in white.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
synopsis: The Bennet sisters navigate love, class, and misjudgement in Regency England, centred on Elizabeth Bennet and Mr Darcy.

title: Great Expectations
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: The orphan Pip rises from a blacksmith's forge to gentility through a mysterious benefactor, learning hard lessons about class, love, and gratitude.

title: Oliver Twist
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: An orphan boy falls in with a gang of London thieves led by Fagin before his true parentage and a better life are revealed.

title: A Tale of Two Cities
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: Love and sacrifice among Londoners and Parisians caught in the French Revolution, ending in one man's redeeming act on the guillotine. Dickens's meditation on resurrection and the Terror.

title: The Count of Monte Cristo
author: Alexandre Dumas
synopsis: A wrongfully imprisoned man escapes, acquires a fortune, and methodically exacts revenge on those who betrayed him.

title: The Three Musketeers
author: Alexandre Dumas
synopsis: The young Gascon d'Artagnan joins the King's Musketeers and, with Athos, Porthos, and Aramis, foils the schemes of Cardinal Richelieu and Milady.

title: Treasure Island
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
synopsis: Young Jim Hawkins finds a map to buried pirate gold and sails with a crew that includes the charming, treacherous Long John Silver. The archetypal pirate adventure.

title: The Scarlet Letter
author: Nathaniel Hawthorne
synopsis: In Puritan Boston, Hester Prynne bears a child out of wedlock and must wear a scarlet 'A', while the father's hidden guilt festers. A study of sin, shame, and hypocrisy in a rigid community.

title: Vanity Fair
author: William Makepeace Thackeray
synopsis: A satirical panorama of English society following the amoral schemer Becky Sharp and the gentle Amelia Sedley through the Napoleonic era.

title: Middlemarch
author: George Eliot
synopsis: A panoramic study of provincial English life centered on idealistic Dorothea Brooke and ambitious young doctor Tertius Lydgate as their aspirations collide with a narrow society.

title: Villette
author: Charlotte Bronte
synopsis: The reserved Englishwoman Lucy Snowe teaches at a boarding school in the foreign city of Villette, where she navigates loneliness, love, and her own guarded inner life.

title: Wuthering Heights
author: Emily Bronte
synopsis: On the Yorkshire moors, the fierce, doomed passion between Heathcliff and Catherine Earnshaw drives cycles of revenge across two families and generations.

title: The Age of Innocence
author: Edith Wharton
synopsis: In 1870s upper-crust New York, lawyer Newland Archer is torn between his conventional fiancee May Welland and her unconventional, European cousin the Countess Ellen Olenska.

title: Gulliver's Travels
author: Jonathan Swift
synopsis: The satirical voyages of ship's surgeon Lemuel Gulliver to the tiny Lilliputians, the giant Brobdingnagians, the flying island of Laputa, and the rational Houyhnhnms.

title: Uncle Tom's Cabin
author: Harriet Beecher Stowe
synopsis: The pious enslaved man Tom is sold south and endures the brutality of Simon Legree, while the Harris family flees toward freedom, in Stowe's landmark anti-slavery novel.

title: The Mill on the Floss
author: George Eliot
synopsis: The story of impulsive, intelligent Maggie Tulliver and her bond with her rigid brother Tom as their family's decline and her forbidden loves drive toward tragedy on the river Floss.

title: Silas Marner
author: George Eliot
synopsis: A miserly, embittered linen-weaver, exiled and robbed of his gold, is redeemed when he adopts an orphaned golden-haired child who wanders into his cottage.

title: Adam Bede
author: George Eliot
synopsis: In rural Hayslope, upright carpenter Adam Bede loves the vain dairymaid Hetty Sorrel, whose ruin by the young squire Arthur Donnithorne brings tragedy and draws Adam toward the Methodist preacher Dinah Morris.

title: Barchester Towers
author: Anthony Trollope
synopsis: Ecclesiastical power struggles in the cathedral town of Barchester pit the oily chaplain Mr. Slope and the domineering Mrs. Proudie against the old guard as suitors circle the widowed Eleanor Bold.

title: The Warden
author: Anthony Trollope
synopsis: The first Barsetshire novel, in which the gentle clergyman Septimus Harding is drawn into a public controversy over the income he draws as warden of a charitable almshouse.

title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: Twelve short cases in which the detective Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson unravel blackmail, disguise, and murder through observation and deduction. The stories that fixed the modern detective in the popular imagination.

title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: A collection of Sherlock Holmes short stories, culminating in his fatal struggle with Professor Moriarty at the Reichenbach Falls.

title: North and South
author: Elizabeth Gaskell
synopsis: Margaret Hale, uprooted from the rural south of England to the grim industrial town of Milton, clashes with and gradually comes to understand mill-owner John Thornton amid strikes and social upheaval.

title: The Tenant of Wildfell Hall
author: Anne Bronte
synopsis: A mysterious young widow, Helen Graham, takes refuge at Wildfell Hall, and her secret diary reveals her flight from a dissolute, abusive husband.

title: Kidnapped
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
synopsis: Orphaned David Balfour is cheated of his inheritance and swept into the Jacobite Highlands alongside the dashing rebel Alan Breck. A chase across a vividly rendered 18th-century Scotland.

title: Far from the Madding Crowd
author: Thomas Hardy
synopsis: The independent farmer Bathsheba Everdene is courted by the steadfast shepherd Gabriel Oak, the obsessive Farmer Boldwood, and the reckless Sergeant Troy in the rural world of Weatherbury.

title: Jude the Obscure
author: Thomas Hardy
synopsis: The stonemason Jude Fawley yearns for the university at Christminster but is thwarted by class, an ill-made marriage, and his tragic love for his cousin Sue Bridehead.

title: The Mayor of Casterbridge
author: Thomas Hardy
synopsis: Michael Henchard, who in a drunken auction sells his wife and child, rises to become Mayor of Casterbridge before his past and his own temper bring him down.

title: Kim
author: Rudyard Kipling
synopsis: An orphaned boy in British India travels the Grand Trunk Road as disciple to a Tibetan lama while being drawn into the espionage of the Great Game.

title: A Room with a View
author: E. M. Forster
synopsis: Young Lucy Honeychurch, awakened by a passionate encounter in Florence, must choose between the freethinking George Emerson and her conventional English fiancé Cecil Vyse.

title: My Ántonia
author: Willa Cather
synopsis: Narrator Jim Burden recalls his boyhood on the Nebraska prairie and his lifelong bond with Ántonia Shimerda, the spirited daughter of Bohemian immigrant homesteaders.

title: The House of Mirth
author: Edith Wharton
synopsis: Beautiful but impoverished socialite Lily Bart drifts through turn-of-the-century New York high society, her search for a wealthy marriage undone by debt, scandal, and her own scruples.

title: The Red Badge of Courage
author: Stephen Crane
synopsis: A young Union recruit flees his first Civil War battle, then wrestles with cowardice and the wish to prove himself. An impressionistic account of fear and courage under fire.

title: Heart of Darkness
author: Joseph Conrad
synopsis: The sailor Marlow recounts his journey up the Congo River to retrieve the enigmatic ivory agent Kurtz, confronting the brutality and moral emptiness of colonialism. A dark voyage into what men become far from restraint.

title: Ivanhoe
author: Walter Scott
synopsis: A disinherited Saxon knight returns from the Crusades to a England divided between Normans and Saxons, amid tournaments, Robin Hood, and Richard the Lionheart. A founding historical romance.

title: Tristram Shandy
author: Laurence Sterne
synopsis: A digressive comic novel in which the narrator's attempt to tell his own life story is endlessly derailed by opinions, tangents and mishaps.

title: Bleak House
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: A sprawling novel centred on the interminable Chancery suit Jarndyce and Jarndyce, weaving together mystery, satire of the law, and the secret of Lady Dedlock.

title: Our Mutual Friend
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: Dickens's last completed novel, in which a fortune tied to Thames dust-heaps and a supposed drowning set off intertwined stories of greed, disguise and redemption.

title: Daniel Deronda
author: George Eliot
synopsis: The self-searching Gwendolen Harleth's disastrous marriage is intertwined with the idealist Daniel Deronda's discovery of his Jewish heritage and vocation.

title: Wives and Daughters
author: Elizabeth Gaskell
synopsis: In a small English town, the doctor's daughter Molly Gibson navigates a new stepmother, a captivating stepsister, and quiet love amid the intertwined fortunes of the Hamley and Cumnor families.

title: The Return of the Native
author: Thomas Hardy
synopsis: On the brooding expanse of Egdon Heath, the restless Eustacia Vye marries the returned diamond-merchant Clym Yeobright, but their clashing ambitions and a web of misunderstandings drive them toward tragedy.

title: Lord Jim
author: Joseph Conrad
synopsis: A young officer abandons a sinking ship full of pilgrims and spends the rest of his life trying to outrun the shame of that one act. Marlow narrates his search for redemption in a remote Malay settlement.

title: The Way We Live Now
author: Anthony Trollope
synopsis: The mysterious financier Augustus Melmotte ascends to the heights of London society on a fortune of speculation and fraud, while the impoverished, scheming Carbury family and a corrupt literary world orbit his rise and fall.

title: Ethan Frome
author: Edith Wharton
synopsis: A poor, taciturn New England farmer trapped in a joyless marriage to his hypochondriac wife Zeena falls in love with her cousin Mattie, and their doomed passion ends in a shattering act of despair.

title: Shirley
author: Charlotte Bronte
synopsis: Set amid Luddite unrest in Yorkshire, the fortunes of mill-owner Robert Moore entwine with those of the gentle Caroline Helstone and the spirited heiress Shirley Keeldar.

title: The Professor
author: Charlotte Bronte
synopsis: The orphaned Englishman William Crimsworth makes his own way as a teacher in Brussels, where he falls in love with the poor but resolute pupil-teacher Frances Henri.

title: A Connecticut Yankee in King Arthur's Court
author: Mark Twain
synopsis: A 19th-century Yankee is transported to King Arthur's Britain and tries to modernize it.

title: The Prince and the Pauper
author: Mark Twain
synopsis: In Tudor England a pauper boy and Prince Edward swap places and each experiences the other's life.

title: Three Men in a Boat
author: Jerome K. Jerome
synopsis: Three friends and a dog take a comic rowing holiday up the Thames from Kingston to Oxford.

title: Madame Bovary
author: Gustave Flaubert
synopsis: A provincial doctor's romantic wife pursues adulterous affairs and luxurious fantasies to escape bourgeois boredom, ruining herself with debt and despair.

title: Sons and Lovers
author: D. H. Lawrence
synopsis: A Nottinghamshire miner's son, bound by an intense attachment to his refined mother, struggles to form independent romantic and adult identity through his loves for Miriam and Clara.

title: Dombey and Son
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: The story of the proud shipping merchant Paul Dombey, whose obsession with a male heir blinds him to the devotion of his daughter Florence.

title: The Awakening
author: Kate Chopin
synopsis: Edna Pontellier, a wife and mother in Creole Louisiana, awakens to her own desires and refuses the roles society assigns her. A frank early portrait of a woman's sexual and artistic self-discovery.

title: The War of the Worlds
author: H. G. Wells
synopsis: Martians land in England and lay waste to the countryside with heat-rays and tripods, until an unexpected foe halts them. The template for alien-invasion stories.

title: Robinson Crusoe
author: Daniel Defoe
synopsis: Shipwrecked alone on a Caribbean island, a castaway spends decades building a life from wreckage and wit before meeting the man he calls Friday. Often called the first English novel.

title: The Vicar of Wakefield
author: Oliver Goldsmith
synopsis: A gentle country parson watches misfortune strip away his family's fortune, home, and honor before providence relents.

title: Don Quixote
author: Miguel de Cervantes
synopsis: An aging gentleman, his wits addled by chivalric romances, rides out as a knight-errant with the peasant Sancho Panza, tilting at windmills and misreading the world. The founding comic novel of the Western canon.

title: Macbeth
author: William Shakespeare
synopsis: Spurred by witches' prophecy and his wife's ambition, a Scottish general murders his king and is consumed by the crown he steals.

title: Rasselas
author: Samuel Johnson
synopsis: A prince escapes the Happy Valley to search the world for a way of life that truly satisfies.

title: Divine Comedy
author: Dante Alighieri
synopsis: Guided by Virgil and Beatrice, the poet descends through Hell and climbs Purgatory and Paradise toward the vision of God.

title: Julius Caesar
author: William Shakespeare
synopsis: Fearing tyranny, Brutus and his fellow conspirators assassinate Rome's rising dictator and unleash civil war.

title: Othello
author: William Shakespeare
synopsis: A Moorish general, poisoned by his ensign's whispers, strangles the innocent wife he loves.

title: The Sorrows of Young Werther
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
synopsis: A sensitive young man's hopeless love for a woman already betrothed drives him to despair and suicide.

title: Tom Jones
author: Henry Fielding
synopsis: A good-hearted foundling stumbles through scrapes, seductions, and the open road on his way to winning his beloved Sophia.

title: Idylls of the King
author: Alfred Tennyson
synopsis: Arthur's Round Table rises in glory and crumbles through Guinevere's betrayal, told in twelve linked verse tales.

title: Iliad
author: Homer
synopsis: The wrath of Achilles turns the tide of the Trojan War through slaughter, grief, and the fall of Hector.

title: King Lear
author: William Shakespeare
synopsis: An aging king divides his realm by his daughters' flattery and is driven mad by their cruelty.

title: Marmion
author: Walter Scott
synopsis: A tale of forgery, betrayed love, and knightly deceit that ends on the disastrous field of Flodden.

title: Symposium
author: Plato
synopsis: At a drinking party, guests take turns praising love until Socrates reframes it as the soul's ascent toward beauty itself.

title: The Lady of the Lake
author: Walter Scott
synopsis: Love, honor, and clan feud entangle a Highland maiden and a disguised king around the shores of Loch Katrine.

title: An Essay Concerning Human Understanding
author: John Locke
synopsis: The mind begins as a blank slate, and all our knowledge is written on it by experience.

title: Faust
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
synopsis: A restless scholar wagers his soul with Mephistopheles for boundless knowledge and earthly experience.

title: Henry V
author: William Shakespeare
synopsis: A reformed young king rallies his outnumbered English army to improbable victory at Agincourt.

title: Holy Living and Holy Dying
author: Jeremy Taylor
synopsis: A devotional handbook on ordering one's days in virtue and meeting death with a prepared soul.

title: Humphry Clinker
author: Tobias Smollett
synopsis: A crotchety squire and his household tour Britain, their clashing letters mapping the comedy of the road.

title: Paul and Virginia
author: Bernardin de Saint-Pierre
synopsis: Two children raised in island innocence grow into a love that the wider world destroys.

title: Phaedrus
author: Plato
synopsis: Strolling beyond the city walls, Socrates and Phaedrus debate love, the soul, and the true art of rhetoric.

title: Richard III
author: William Shakespeare
synopsis: A deformed, silver-tongued duke murders his way to the English throne and reigns until conscience and Bosworth undo him.

title: Roderick Random
author: Tobias Smollett
synopsis: A quick-tempered young Scot endures press-gangs, shipwrecks, and betrayals as he chases his fortune across land and sea.

title: Rubáiyát of Omar Khayyám
author: Omar Khayyám
synopsis: A Persian astronomer's quatrains urge us to seize the wine and the passing hour before the grave claims us.

title: Sartor Resartus
author: Thomas Carlyle
synopsis: A fictional German professor's 'philosophy of clothes' becomes a meditation on how all creeds and forms are garments the spirit wears.

title: Tales of the Genii
author: James Ridley
synopsis: Oriental fables, passed off as translations from the Persian, in which genii test mortals and reward virtue over vice.

title: The Aeneid
author: Virgil
synopsis: A Trojan refugee, driven by fate and the gods, sails to Italy to found the race that will become Rome.

title: The Christian Year
author: John Keble
synopsis: Devotional poems for every Sunday and holy day of the Anglican calendar, turning the church's seasons into quiet meditation.

title: The History of the Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire
author: Edward Gibbon
synopsis: The vast, ironic chronicle of Rome's collapse across thirteen centuries, tracing the empire's ruin to barbarism and religion.

title: The Italian
author: Ann Radcliffe
synopsis: In the shadow of the Inquisition, two lovers are torn apart by a scheming monk with a guilty past.

title: The Koran
author: Anonymous
synopsis: The revelations Muslims hold that Muhammad received from God, calling humankind to submission, judgment, and mercy.

title: The Lord of the Rings
author: J R R Tolkien
synopsis: A hobbit inherits a ring of terrible power and carries it across Middle-earth to unmake it in the fire that forged it.

title: The Raven
author: Edgar Allan Poe
synopsis: A grieving lover, visited at midnight by a black bird that answers only 'Nevermore,' sinks toward madness.

title: The Task
author: William Cowper
synopsis: A poem that begins in jest with a sofa and wanders into praise of rural quiet, gardens, and the country over the town.

title: Timaeus
author: Plato
synopsis: Plato's speaker recounts how a divine craftsman shaped the ordered cosmos, the world-soul, and time itself from eternal forms.

title: A Bend in the River
author: V. S. Naipaul
synopsis: An Indian trader builds a life at a town deep in a newly independent African country as it slides toward chaos.

title: A Child's Garden of Verses
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
synopsis: Poems that see the world through a child's eyes — bedtime, shadows, journeys, and the small kingdoms of play.

title: A Dictionary of the English Language
author: Samuel Johnson
synopsis: One man's monumental attempt to fix and define the whole English tongue, his wit and prejudice included.

title: A Dissertation on Liberty and Necessity, Pleasure and Pain
author: Benjamin Franklin
synopsis: A precocious pamphlet arguing that in a world wholly governed by God there can be no free will, and so no vice or virtue.

title: A Farewell to Arms
author: Ernest Hemingway
synopsis: An American ambulance driver on the Italian front falls in love with a nurse and deserts a war that spares no one.

title: A Gossip on Romance
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
synopsis: An essay defending the deep pleasures of storytelling — the vivid scenes and incidents that seize a reader and never let go.

title: A History of British Birds
author: Thomas Bewick
synopsis: A naturalist's finely engraved catalogue of the land and water birds of Britain, each cut from wood by his own hand.

title: A New Voyage Round the World
author: William Dampier
synopsis: A buccaneer-naturalist's account of circling the globe, mixing piracy with careful notes on winds, coasts, and strange creatures.

title: A Shropshire Lad
author: A. E. Housman
synopsis: Spare, aching poems of young men, lost love, and early death in a half-imagined Shropshire countryside.

title: A Tale of a Tub
author: Jonathan Swift
synopsis: A riotous satire in which three brothers mangle their father's will, mocking the corruptions of the Christian churches.

title: A Theory of Justice
author: John Rawls
synopsis: A thought experiment behind a 'veil of ignorance' derives the principles a fair society would choose for itself.

title: A Treatise of Human Nature
author: David Hume
synopsis: Hume grounds all human knowledge in experience and passion, arguing that reason is the slave of the passions.

title: Acres of Diamond
author: Russel H. Conwell
synopsis: A farmer sells his land to chase diamonds abroad, never knowing the richest mine lies in his own backyard.

title: Adaptation and Natural Selection
author: George C Williams
synopsis: Natural selection acts on genes and individuals, not groups — a rigorous case against loose adaptationist thinking.

title: Aerothermodynamics of Gas Turbine and Rocket Propulsion
author: Gordon C Oates
synopsis: A graduate text on the thermodynamics and gas dynamics governing jet engines and rocket motors.

title: Aesop's Fables
author: Aesop
synopsis: Talking animals enact brief moral lessons, from the tortoise outracing the hare to the fox spurning sour grapes.

title: Agamemnon
author: Aeschylus
synopsis: Returning victorious from Troy, the king is murdered in his bath by the wife who never forgave their daughter's sacrifice.

title: American Grace: How Religion Divides and Unites Us
author: David E. Campbell and Robert D. Putnam
synopsis: A sweeping survey of how American religion fosters both tolerance and division across a diverse democracy.

title: An Essay on Man
author: Alexander Pope
synopsis: A philosophical poem defending providence and man's place in the ordered chain of being — whatever is, is right.

title: An Essay on Projects
author: Daniel Defoe
synopsis: A young Defoe proposes banks, insurance, pensions, and academies to remake the commerce and welfare of England.

title: An Iceland Fisherman
author: Pierre Loti
synopsis: A Breton woman waits ashore for the fisherman she loves, whose heart belongs to the perilous northern sea.

title: Anne of Geierstein
author: Walter Scott
synopsis: An exiled English nobleman is drawn into Swiss intrigue and the doomed ambition of Charles the Bold of Burgundy.

title: Apologia Pro Vita Sua
author: John Henry Newman
synopsis: Newman traces the history of his religious opinions to justify his conversion from Anglicanism to Rome.

title: Apology
author: Plato
synopsis: Socrates defends his life of questioning before an Athenian jury, refusing to beg for his acquittal.

title: Aurora Leigh
author: Elizabeth Barrett Browning
synopsis: A verse novel following an orphaned woman's struggle to become a poet and reconcile art with love.

title: Average is Over
author: Tyler Cowen
synopsis: An economist forecasts a hyper-meritocracy where those who work well with machines thrive and the rest stagnate.

title: Belinda
author: Maria Edgeworth
synopsis: A sensible young woman navigates fashionable London society and the perils of courtship and reputation.

title: Ben-Hur
author: Lew Wallace
synopsis: A Jewish prince betrayed into slavery seeks revenge on his Roman friend and crosses paths with Christ.

title: Beowulf
author: Anonymous
synopsis: A Geatish hero slays the monster Grendel and its mother, then falls decades later battling a dragon.

title: Book of Imaginary Beings
author: Jorge Luis Borges
synopsis: A wry bestiary cataloguing the fantastic creatures dreamed up across the world's myths and literatures.

title: Bowling Alone
author: Robert Putnam
synopsis: Americans grow ever more disconnected from family, neighbors, and civic life — the quiet collapse of social capital.

title: Breakfast at Tiffany's
author: Truman Capote
synopsis: A writer remembers Holly Golightly, a New York café-society drifter chasing rich men and a sense of belonging that stays out of reach.

title: Breaking the Spell: Religion as a Natural Phenomenon
author: Daniel Dennett
synopsis: A philosopher argues that religion should be studied scientifically, as a natural phenomenon shaped by evolution and human need rather than shielded from inquiry.

title: Camilla
author: Frances Burney
synopsis: A spirited young woman's path to marrying the man she loves is tangled by debts, misunderstandings, and her own impulsive heart.

title: Carmen Saeculare
author: Horace
synopsis: A choral hymn to Apollo and Diana, commissioned by Augustus and sung by children to bless the future of Rome.

title: Castle of Wolfenbach
author: Eliza Parsons
synopsis: Fleeing a predatory uncle, a young woman takes refuge in a haunted castle whose locked rooms hide a wronged countess's secret.

title: Cecilia
author: Frances Burney
synopsis: An heiress may keep her fortune only if her husband takes her surname, a condition that torments her love for a proud family's son.

title: Chaos: Making a New Science
author: James Gleick
synopsis: A narrative history of chaos theory, following the scientists who found strange order in turbulence, weather, and the unpredictable.

title: Chastelard
author: Algernon Charles Swinburne
synopsis: A French poet at the Scottish court loves Mary, Queen of Scots, so recklessly that he courts his own execution.

title: Chinese Ghosts
author: Lafcadio Hearn
synopsis: Six retold Chinese legends of great bells, temple spirits, and doomed love, rendered in lush, incantatory prose.

title: Christus
author: Henry Wadsworth Longfellow
synopsis: A dramatic trilogy tracing Christianity from the Nativity through medieval faith to the persecutions of Puritan New England.

title: Chrysal, or the Adventures of a Guinea
author: Charles Johnstone
synopsis: A gold guinea narrates its passage through the hands of misers, harlots, and hypocrites, exposing the vices of Georgian society.

title: Civil and Moral Discourse
author: Francis Bacon
synopsis: Terse, worldly essays on ambition, friendship, truth, and study, offering hard counsel for getting on in public and private life.

title: Clermont
author: Regina Maria Roche
synopsis: A girl raised in rural seclusion with her father is drawn into a web of aristocratic secrets and Gothic peril when the past intrudes.

title: Clisson et Eugene
author: Napoleon
synopsis: A brilliant soldier withdraws into idyllic love with Eugénie, then is recalled to war and undone by betrayal — Napoleon's autobiographical romance.

title: Cocker's Arithmetick
author: Edward Cocker
synopsis: A practical arithmetic textbook so authoritative that 'according to Cocker' came to mean precisely correct.

title: Code of Hummarabi
author: Hummarabi
synopsis: A Babylonian king's engraved code of laws, prescribing an eye for an eye across matters of trade, family, and property.

title: Collected Verses of Walt Whitman
author: Walt Whitman
synopsis: Free-verse hymns to democracy, the body, and the American self, gathered from a lifetime of expanding Leaves of Grass.

title: Colomba
author: Prosper Merimee
synopsis: A Corsican woman goads her reluctant, Europeanized brother into carrying out the blood vendetta owed for their father's murder.

title: Confessions of an English Opium Eater
author: Thomas De Quincey
synopsis: A writer chronicles the visionary pleasures and slow torments of his addiction to opium.

title: Conservatism
author: Jerry Muller
synopsis: An anthology tracing conservative social and political thought from David Hume to the present day.

title: Consolations in Travels; or, The Last Days of a Philosopher
author: Humphry Davy
synopsis: Dialogues on science, nature, and the soul, composed by a dying chemist on his final journeys.

title: Contes Drolatiques
author: Honore de Balzac
synopsis: Bawdy, Rabelaisian tales of old Touraine spun in mock-medieval French.

title: Corinne
author: Madame de Stael
synopsis: An improvising Italian poetess loves a melancholy Scottish nobleman who cannot free himself from northern duty.

title: Coriolanus
author: William Shakespeare
synopsis: A proud Roman general, banished by the city he saved, turns his sword against Rome.

title: Critias
author: Plato
synopsis: An unfinished dialogue recounting the ancient war between primeval Athens and the island empire of Atlantis.

title: Cryptonomicon
author: Neal Stephenson
synopsis: Second World War cryptographers and their grandchildren chase buried gold and a data haven across two eras.

title: Curfew Must Not Ring To-Night
author: Rose Hartwick Thorpe
synopsis: To save her condemned lover, a young woman climbs the belfry and clings to the great bell's clapper to silence the curfew.

title: DIANA: Her True Story
author: Andrew Morton
synopsis: The secret account of a princess's unhappy royal marriage, told with her own covert cooperation.

title: Dancing in the Streets: A History of Collective Joy
author: Barbara Ehrenreich
synopsis: A history of communal ecstasy — festival, carnival, and dance — and the forces that worked to suppress it.

title: Darwin's Cathedrals
author: David Sloan Wilson
synopsis: An argument that religion evolved as an adaptation binding human groups into cooperative organisms.

title: De Officiis
author: Marcus Tullius Cicero
synopsis: A statesman's letter to his son on duty, honor, and reconciling expediency with virtue.

title: Delivering Happiness
author: Tony Hsieh
synopsis: The Zappos founder recounts building a company on customer service, culture, and the pursuit of happiness.

title: Descartes' Error
author: Antonio Damasio
synopsis: A neuroscientist argues that emotion, far from clouding reason, is indispensable to sound judgment.

title: Destruction of Black Civilization
author: Chancellor Williams
synopsis: A sweeping history arguing that Africa's great civilizations were deliberately undermined across six thousand years.

title: Digest of International Law
author: J. B. Moore
synopsis: A monumental compendium of the precedents and practice of international law drawn from American diplomacy.

title: Don Juan
author: George Gordon Byron
synopsis: A satirical epic recasting the legendary seducer as an innocent swept through the follies of Europe.

title: Dreams of My Father
author: Barack Obama
synopsis: A future president's memoir of race, identity, and the search for a father he barely knew.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
synopsis: On a desert planet prized for a mind-expanding spice, a betrayed heir rises as a messiah among its people.

title: Elective Affinities
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
synopsis: A contented married couple invite two guests, and chemical attraction quietly and tragically rearranges all four hearts.

title: Electra
author: Sophocles
synopsis: Consumed by grief, Electra waits for her exiled brother to return and avenge their father's murder by their mother.

title: Elegant Extracts
author: Vicesimus Knox
synopsis: A schoolmaster's popular anthology of instructive poetry and prose, gathered to improve the taste and morals of the young.

title: Eloquence
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
synopsis: Emerson anatomizes the orator's power to seize a crowd and bend it toward the truth of the moment.

title: Encyclopaedia Britannica
author: Anonymous
synopsis: A comprehensive reference work marshaling the whole of human knowledge — science, art, history — into alphabetically ordered articles.

title: Epipsychidion
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley
synopsis: A rapturous verse hymn to a young woman who embodies the poet's vision of ideal, boundless spiritual love.

title: Essays of Elia
author: Charles Lamb
synopsis: Whimsical, tender personal essays in which 'Elia' muses on old actors, roast pig, chimney-sweeps, and vanished friends.

title: Essays to do Good
author: Cotton Mather
synopsis: A Puritan minister's practical exhortation that every person, in every station, seize daily occasions to do good.

title: Essays: Circles
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
synopsis: Emerson finds in the ever-widening circle a figure for a self and universe that endlessly outgrow every limit.

title: Euphorion
author: Vernon Lee
synopsis: Impressionistic essays tracing how the antique and medieval worlds mingle and transform within the art of the Renaissance.

title: Excelsior
author: Henry Wadsworth Longfellow
synopsis: A youth bearing a banner marked 'Excelsior' climbs ever higher through an Alpine village toward a frozen death.

title: Exotics and Retrospectives
author: Lafcadio Hearn
synopsis: Delicate essays on Japanese nature and memory, from the singing of insects to intimations of former lives.

title: Extraordinary Popular Delusions and the Madness of Crowds
author: Charles Mackay, LL. D
synopsis: A rollicking history of mass folly — tulip mania, alchemy, witch-hunts, and speculative bubbles that swept whole nations.

title: Family Instructor
author: Daniel Defoe
synopsis: Didactic dialogues in which parents, children, and servants wrestle over religious duty within the Christian household.

title: Fordyce's Sermons
author: James Fordyce
synopsis: Earnest sermons instructing young women in modesty, piety, and the domestic virtues thought becoming to their sex.

title: Fundamentals of Astrodynamics
author: Roger R. Bate, Donald D. Mueller, Jerry E. White
synopsis: A rigorous introduction to orbital mechanics — two-body motion, orbit determination, and interplanetary transfer — for aspiring space engineers.

title: Garden of Cyrus
author: Thomas Browne
synopsis: Browne traces the quincunx, the five-point pattern, through gardens, art, and nature into the mystical design of the cosmos.

title: Gargantua and Pantagruel
author: Francois Rabelais
synopsis: The bawdy, riotous adventures of two giant kings, brimming with satire, learning, and boundless appetite for life.

title: Gerusalemme Liberata
author: Torquato Tasso
synopsis: An epic of the First Crusade, where Christian knights besiege Jerusalem amid sorcery, love, and holy war.

title: Gil Blas
author: Alain-René Lesage
synopsis: A picaresque chronicle of a Spanish youth's rise and fall through servants' halls, robbers' dens, and royal courts.

title: God is Not Great: How Religion Poisons Everything
author: Christopher Hitchins
synopsis: A blistering polemic arguing that religion is man-made, violent, and a poison at the root of human cruelty.

title: Gone with the Wind
author: Margaret Mitchell
synopsis: Willful Scarlett O'Hara claws her way through the ruin of the Civil War South, loving the wrong man.

title: Good natured: The Origins of Right and Wrong in Humans and Other Animals
author: Frans B. M. de Waal
synopsis: A primatologist finds the roots of human morality in the empathy, reciprocity, and fairness already at work among apes and monkeys.

title: Grendel
author: John Gardner
synopsis: Beowulf's monster tells his own story, a lonely, philosophical beast raging against the meaning humans insist on imposing.

title: Gunn’s Domestic Medicine
author: John C Gunn
synopsis: A frontier household's plain-language guide to treating disease and injury when no doctor can be reached.

title: Heimskringla
author: Snorri Sturluson
synopsis: The saga chronicle of the kings of Norway, from mythic Odin-descended origins down to the twelfth century.

title: Helen
author: Isocrates
synopsis: A showpiece of oratory that turns to praise of Helen of Troy for the beauty that stirred all Greece.

title: Hierarchy in the Forest
author: Christopher Boem
synopsis: Egalitarian societies persist because the rank and file band together to suppress anyone who reaches for dominance.

title: Hints from Horace
author: George Gordon Byron
synopsis: Byron transplants Horace's Art of Poetry to Regency London and lashes its literary fashions in couplets.

title: Histories
author: Herodotus
synopsis: Herodotus inquires into the great war between Greeks and Persians, gathering the customs and marvels of the known world.

title: History of European Morals
author: William Edward Hartpole Lecky
synopsis: Lecky traces the slow evolution of European moral feeling from the age of Augustus to Charlemagne.

title: Holy Fair
author: Robert Burns
synopsis: Burns satirizes a Scottish communion gathering where sermon and flirtation, piety and drink mingle freely.

title: Horrid Mysteries
author: Carl Grosse
synopsis: A young nobleman is drawn into the labyrinthine intrigues and staged deaths of a sinister secret society.

title: How They Brought the Good News from Ghent to Aix
author: Robert Browning
synopsis: Three riders gallop through the night, and only one horse survives to carry the news to Aix.

title: How to Attract and Hold an Audience: A Popular Treatise on the Nature, Preparation, and Delivery of Public Discourse
author: Joseph Berg Esenwein
synopsis: A practical treatise on preparing and delivering a public speech that seizes and holds a crowd's attention.

title: How to Win Friends and Influence People
author: Dale Carnegie
synopsis: A salesman's rules for making people like you and bringing them around to your way of thinking.

title: Hydriotaphia
author: Thomas Browne
synopsis: Unearthed Roman urns prompt Browne to meditate on burial rites, oblivion, and the vanity of earthly remembrance.

title: In Memoriam
author: Alfred Tennyson
synopsis: A long elegy works through years of grief for a dead friend toward a hard-won faith.

title: In the Penal Colony
author: Franz Kafka
synopsis: A visitor watches an officer demonstrate the machine that executes prisoners by slowly carving their sentence into their flesh.

title: Introduction to the Principles of Morals and Legislation
author: Jeremy Bentham
synopsis: Bentham grounds law and morality in one principle: the greatest happiness of the greatest number.

title: Invictus
author: William Ernest Henley
synopsis: Bloodied but unbowed, the speaker declares himself master of his fate and captain of his soul.

title: Isabella, or the Pot of Basil
author: John Keats
synopsis: Isabella's brothers murder her lover, and she buries his severed head in a pot of basil she tends with her grief.

title: Killshot
author: Elmore Leonard
synopsis: An ordinary working couple witnesses a shakedown and must survive two mismatched hitmen bent on erasing them.

title: Kokoro
author: Natsume Sōseki
synopsis: A young man befriends an aloof older Sensei whose reserve hides a betrayal that has haunted him for years.

title: L'Allegro
author: John Milton
synopsis: A day in the company of Mirth — from the lark's dawn song to firelit tales — celebrating the cheerful, sociable life.

title: L'Entranger
author: Albert Camus
synopsis: Meursault, indifferent to his mother's death, kills a man on a sun-blinded beach and is condemned as much for his honesty as his crime.

title: La Guerre des Dieux
author: Evariste de Parny
synopsis: The pagan gods wage bawdy war on the newly arrived Christian trinity in a mock-heroic, anticlerical epic.

title: La Tour de Nesle
author: Alexandre Dumas
synopsis: Marguerite of Burgundy lures lovers to a riverside tower by night and has them murdered, until one proves bound to her own buried past.

title: Lady Clara Vere de Vere
author: Alfred Tennyson
synopsis: A poet rebuffs a cold, highborn beauty, reminding her that kind hearts are more than coronets.

title: Lalla Rookh
author: Thomas Moore
synopsis: A Mughal princess journeys to her wedding in Kashmir, beguiled along the way by a poet's four Eastern tales.

title: Laon and Cythna
author: Percy Bysshe Shelley
synopsis: Two lovers lead a bloodless revolution against tyranny and pay for it with martyrdom, in Shelley's visionary epic.

title: Laws
author: Plato
synopsis: In his last dialogue, an Athenian stranger designs the laws and institutions of a second-best city.

title: Lay Sermons
author: Thomas Henry Huxley
synopsis: Essays and addresses in which Huxley preaches the method and morality of science to a lay Victorian public.

title: Le Morte d'Arthur
author: Thomas Malory
synopsis: The rise and ruin of King Arthur's Round Table — Lancelot's love, the Grail quest, and the last battle in the west.

title: Lenore
author: Gottfried August Burger
synopsis: A grieving maiden curses God, and her dead lover returns at midnight to carry her on horseback to his grave.

title: Les Chouans
author: Honoré de Balzac
synopsis: A republican beauty is sent to seduce and betray a royalist rebel leader in war-torn Brittany, and falls in love instead.

title: Les Misérables
author: Victor Hugo
synopsis: Ex-convict Jean Valjean seeks redemption while the relentless Inspector Javert pursues him across decades of French upheaval. A sweeping epic of justice, mercy, and revolution.

title: Lettres a une Inconnue
author: Prosper Merimee
synopsis: Mérimée's decades of witty, tender letters to a woman he never names, published after his death.

title: Light in August
author: William Faulkner
synopsis: In a Mississippi August, an orphan haunted by rumors of black blood collides with a pregnant girl walking north to find her lover.

title: Light of Asia
author: Edwin Arnold
synopsis: The life and awakening of Prince Gautama, who leaves his palace to become the Buddha, told in flowing verse.

title: Literary Character
author: Isaac Disraeli
synopsis: A study of the temperament, habits, and sufferings of authors and men of genius.

title: Literary Character of Men of Genius
author: Isaac Disraeli
synopsis: An inquiry into the mind and character of literary genius, drawn from the lives of great authors.

title: Literary Studies
author: Walter Bagehot
synopsis: Collected essays in which Bagehot weighs English poets and writers with shrewd, worldly judgment.

title: Little Dorrit
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: Amy 'Little Dorrit,' born inside the Marshalsea debtors' prison, and the returning Arthur Clennam are swept through sudden fortune and financial ruin in a satire of bureaucracy and speculation.

title: Lives and Anecdotes of Misers
author: Frederic Somner Merryweather
synopsis: Biographical sketches chronicling the strange, grasping careers of history's most notorious misers and hoarders.

title: Logic or the Art of Thinking
author: Antoine Arnauld and Pierre Nicole
synopsis: The Port-Royal treatise on how to reason well, teaching conception, judgment, deduction, and method.

title: Lotos Time in Japan
author: Henry T. Finck
synopsis: A Western critic's leisurely travel impressions of temples, gardens, and festivals across Meiji-era Japan.

title: Love in a Village
author: Isaac Bickerstaffe
synopsis: Young lovers disguise themselves as servants to escape arranged marriages in this pastoral ballad opera.

title: Lovers' Vows
author: Elizabeth Inchbald
synopsis: A soldier discovers that the baron who seduced and abandoned his dying mother is his own father, and forces a reckoning.

title: Made in the USA
author: Vaclac Smil
synopsis: A data-driven account of the rise and retreat of American manufacturing, and why a nation that stops making things falters.

title: Manon Lescaut
author: Abbé Prévost
synopsis: A young nobleman abandons everything for the beautiful, faithless Manon, following her from Paris to ruin and exile.

title: Marco Bozzaris
author: Fitz-Greene Halleck
synopsis: A Greek chieftain leads a midnight charge against the Turks and falls in the very hour of victory.

title: Martin Chuzzlewit
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: Greed and hypocrisy swirl around old Martin Chuzzlewit while his disinherited grandson learns the cost of selfishness on both sides of the Atlantic.

title: Mary Stuart
author: Friedrich Schiller
synopsis: Imprisoned and awaiting the axe, Mary Queen of Scots faces her rival Elizabeth in a doomed contest of crowns.

title: Masnawi
author: Rumi
synopsis: A vast poem of spiritual couplets, threading fables and parables toward union with the divine.

title: Measure for Measure
author: William Shakespeare
synopsis: A duke steps aside and his puritanical deputy condemns a man to death for fornication, then propositions the man's chaste sister.

title: Mein Kampf
author: Adolph Hitler
synopsis: Hitler's autobiographical manifesto setting out his ideology of racial nationalism, antisemitism, and territorial conquest.

title: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
synopsis: Ishmael signs aboard the whaler Pequod under Captain Ahab, whose monomaniacal hunt for the white whale that maimed him drags his crew toward destruction. A vast meditation on obsession, nature, and fate wrapped in a sea adventure.

title: Moll Flanders
author: Daniel Defoe
synopsis: The candid confessions of a woman born in Newgate who becomes wife, thief, and penitent across a long, hard life.

title: Monsieur Lecoq
author: Émile Gaboriau
synopsis: A young police detective reconstructs a triple murder from a single clue, only to be baffled by the prisoner's true identity.

title: Monsieur de Camors
author: Octave Feuillet
synopsis: Heir to his dead father's creed of ambition without conscience, a count pursues power and pleasure until they destroy him.

title: Moral, Believing Animals
author: Christian Smith
synopsis: A sociologist argues that human beings are, at bottom, moral and believing creatures who live inside the stories they tell.

title: Mr Palomar
author: Italo Calvino
synopsis: A man tries to observe the world with perfect attention — a wave, a cheese counter, the stars — and meets the limits of knowing.

title: Nature
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
synopsis: A transcendentalist manifesto finding in the natural world a mirror of spirit and a path to self-reliance.

title: Neuromancer
author: William Gibson
synopsis: A burned-out hacker is hired for one last job in cyberspace and pulled into the schemes of a rogue artificial intelligence.

title: Night Thoughts
author: Edward Young
synopsis: A grieving poet meditates through the dark hours on death, immortality, and the consolations of Christian faith.

title: Notre Dame de Paris
author: Victor Hugo
synopsis: In medieval Paris, a deformed bell-ringer loves the gypsy dancer Esmeralda, coveted also by the tormented archdeacon Frollo.

title: Oedipus Rex
author: Sophocles
synopsis: A king vows to purge the plague from Thebes, not knowing he himself killed his father and wed his mother.

title: Of Human Bondage
author: Somerset Maugham
synopsis: A club-footed orphan seeks meaning through art and medicine while enslaved by his obsession with a callous waitress.

title: On History
author: Thomas Carlyle
synopsis: An essay arguing that history is the true epic, the essence of innumerable biographies no narrator can ever fully grasp.

title: On Liberty
author: John Stuart Mill
synopsis: A defense of individual freedom against social tyranny, holding that liberty may be curbed only to prevent harm to others.

title: On The Road
author: Jack Kerouac
synopsis: Sal Paradise crisscrosses postwar America with the restless, rhapsodic Dean Moriarty in a headlong search for experience.

title: Out of Africa
author: Isak Dinesen
synopsis: A Danish baroness recalls her coffee farm at the foot of the Ngong Hills in colonial Kenya.

title: Out of the House of Bondage
author: Thavolia Glymph
synopsis: A history of the plantation household as a violent workplace where white and enslaved women struggled over labor and power.

title: Outliers
author: Malcolm Gladwell
synopsis: Success is less lone genius than accident of birth, culture, and the ten thousand hours behind any mastery.

title: Pamela
author: Samuel Richardson
synopsis: A virtuous servant girl resists her master's advances through a torrent of letters until he reforms and marries her.

title: Pamela, or Virtue Rewarded
author: Samuel Richardson
synopsis: A young maidservant's letters chronicle her defense of her chastity against a predatory master who at last weds her.

title: Peregrine Pickle
author: Tobias Smollett
synopsis: A headstrong, prank-loving gentleman roams England and the Continent, squandering fortunes and playing cruel jokes before humbling reversals.

title: Phaedo
author: Plato
synopsis: On his last day, Socrates argues for the immortality of the soul, then calmly drinks the hemlock.

title: Philosophy of History
author: Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel
synopsis: History is the rational progress of Spirit toward the consciousness of freedom, unfolding through the rise and fall of nations.

title: Pleasures of Memory
author: Samuel Rogers
synopsis: A didactic poem tracing how memory preserves the past and colors the present with tender recollection.

title: Patterns, Thinking and Cognition
author: Howard Margolis
synopsis: A theory of judgment arguing that all thinking, even reasoning, rests fundamentally on pattern recognition rather than logical rules.

title: Predictably Irrational
author: Dan Ariely
synopsis: Experiments reveal the hidden, systematic forces that make people irrational in consistent and foreseeable ways.

title: Principles of Psychology
author: William James
synopsis: A sweeping foundational treatise on habit, emotion, will, and the ceaseless stream of consciousness.

title: Protagoras
author: Plato
synopsis: Socrates and the celebrated sophist debate whether virtue is a single thing and whether it can be taught.

title: Public Speaking and Debate
author: George Jacob Holyoake
synopsis: A practical manual for advocates and agitators on the arts of oratory, argument, and public debate.

title: Reflections on the Revolution in France
author: Edmund Burke
synopsis: A statesman's impassioned warning that the Revolution's abstract rights will devour tradition, order, and liberty itself.

title: Religious Courtship
author: Daniel Defoe
synopsis: Through cautionary domestic tales, Defoe argues that the pious should marry only the equally devout.

title: Rights of Man
author: Thomas Paine
synopsis: Answering Burke, Paine defends the French Revolution and every generation's natural right to govern itself.

title: Rivals
author: Jilly Cooper
synopsis: Rival television magnates, adulterous affairs, and ruthless ambition collide in the fight for a Cotswolds franchise.

title: Robin Hood
author: Anonymous
synopsis: The outlaw of Sherwood Forest robs the rich, feeds the poor, and defies the Sheriff of Nottingham.

title: Rocket Propulsion Elements
author: George P Sutton
synopsis: The standard engineering treatise on how rocket engines generate thrust, from propellant chemistry to nozzle design.

title: Rudin
author: Ivan Turgenev
synopsis: An eloquent, idealistic drifter dazzles a provincial household yet proves incapable of acting on his own words.

title: Ruins of Empires
author: Constantin-François Volney
synopsis: Contemplating the wreckage of vanished civilizations, a traveler indicts superstition and despotism as the ruin of nations.

title: Sanshiro
author: Natsume Sōseki
synopsis: A country youth arrives at Tokyo University and drifts through study, friendship, and a love he cannot grasp.

title: Shahnameh
author: Ferdowsi
synopsis: The great Persian epic recounting the mythical and heroic history of Iran's kings from creation to the Arab conquest.

title: Shenandoah
author: Bronson Howard
synopsis: A Civil War melodrama in which love and friendship are strained across the battle lines of North and South.

title: Sir Charles Grandison
author: Samuel Richardson
synopsis: An epistolary portrait of a paragon gentleman whose virtue steers him between two women who love him.

title: Sociology: The New Synthesis
author: E. O Wilson
synopsis: A sweeping argument that social behavior in animals and humans rests on a biological, evolutionary foundation.

title: Sonnets from the Portuguese
author: Elizabeth Barrett Browning
synopsis: Forty-four love sonnets tracing a poet's disbelief giving way to devotion — 'How do I love thee?'

title: Spring Torrents
author: Ivan Turgenev
synopsis: A middle-aged man recalls the springtime love of his youth, betrayed by an enchanting, imperious woman.

title: Stranger in a Strange Land
author: Robert A. Heinlein
synopsis: Raised by Martians, Valentine Michael Smith returns to Earth and becomes a messiah of radical freedom.

title: Summa Tehologica
author: Thomas Aquinas
synopsis: Aquinas's monumental synthesis of Christian theology, arguing every question of God and man through reasoned disputation.

title: Switch: How to Change Things when Change is Hard
author: Chip Heath and Dan Heath
synopsis: Two brothers explain how lasting change happens by directing the rider, motivating the elephant, and shaping the path.

title: Table Talk
author: Martin Luther
synopsis: Informal dinner-table sayings of the Reformer, recorded by students, on faith, scripture, the devil, and daily life.

title: Tales
author: George Crabbe
synopsis: Plainspoken verse narratives of English village life, tracing the moral fortunes of ordinary provincial souls.

title: Tales of Moonlight and Rain
author: Ueda Akinari
synopsis: Nine eerie Japanese tales in which ghosts, spirits, and the restless dead intrude upon the living.

title: Tales of Old Japan
author: Algernon Bertram Freeman-Mitford
synopsis: A collection of Japanese legends, folk tales, and customs, including the vendetta of the forty-seven ronin.

title: Tales of the Alhambra
author: Washington Irving
synopsis: Sketches and Moorish legends drawn from the author's residence in the crumbling Alhambra palace above Granada.

title: Tartarin de Tarascon
author: Alphonse Daudet
synopsis: A boastful Provençal burgher, hungry for glory, sets off to hunt lions in the wilds of Algeria.

title: The Advancement of Learning
author: Francis Bacon
synopsis: Bacon surveys the whole of human knowledge, diagnosing its defects and urging an empirical advance of learning.

title: The African Origin of Civilization
author: Cheikh Anta Diop
synopsis: Diop argues that ancient Egypt was a Black African civilization and the wellspring of later Mediterranean culture.

title: The Ancient History
author: Charles Rollin
synopsis: A sweeping narrative of the Egyptians, Assyrians, Babylonians, Persians, and Greeks from their rise to their fall.

title: The Art of Animation
author: Bob Thomas
synopsis: A behind-the-scenes chronicle of the Walt Disney studio and the craft that gave its cartoons life.

title: The Audacity of Hope
author: Barack Obama
synopsis: A senator's reflections on faith, politics, and the possibility of common ground in a divided America.

title: The Betrothed
author: Alessandro Manzoni
synopsis: In seventeenth-century Lombardy, a tyrant's whim tears two peasant lovers apart amid plague, famine, and war.

title: The Blank Slate: The Modern Denial of Human Nature
author: Steven Pinker
synopsis: Pinker dismantles the doctrine that the mind is a blank slate, defending an innate and universal human nature.

title: The Book of Martyrs
author: John Foxe
synopsis: A Protestant martyrology recounting the sufferings and deaths of Christians persecuted for their faith.

title: The Boscombe Valley Mystery
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: Holmes reads the mud and footprints around a pond to clear a young man accused of murdering his father.

title: The Bride of Abydos
author: Byron
synopsis: In a Turkish court, Selim and Zuleika love across a forbidden bond that ends in bloodshed.

title: The Bride of Lammermoor
author: Walter Scott
synopsis: The doomed love of Lucy Ashton and Edgar Ravenswood, divided by their families' feud over ancestral lands.

title: The Brook
author: Alfred Tennyson
synopsis: A babbling stream narrates its own winding journey to the river, outlasting the men who come and go.

title: The Brothers Karamazov
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
synopsis: The murder of a dissolute father draws his three very different sons into a searing drama of faith, doubt, and moral responsibility that culminates in a wrenching trial.

title: The Carpetbaggers
author: Harold Robbins
synopsis: A ruthless young tycoon claws through aviation, munitions, and Hollywood, wrecking lives as he builds his empire.

title: The Castle
author: Franz Kafka
synopsis: A land surveyor called K. struggles endlessly to reach the elusive authorities of the castle that rules a snowbound village.

title: The Children of the Abbey
author: Regina Maria Roche
synopsis: Amanda and Oscar Fitzalan, cheated of their inheritance, endure slander and misfortune before virtue is at last rewarded.

title: The Chrysanthemum Pledge
author: Ueda Akinari
synopsis: A samurai vows to return by the Chrysanthemum Festival, and when death prevents him, his ghost keeps the promise.

title: The Cost of Discipleship
author: Dietrich Bonhoeffer
synopsis: Bonhoeffer contrasts cheap grace with the costly grace of truly following Christ, whatever the sacrifice.

title: The Culprit Fay
author: Joseph Rodman Drake
synopsis: A fairy of the Hudson Highlands, having loved a mortal maid, must complete perilous quests to cleanse his stain.

title: The Decameron
author: Giovanni Boccaccio
synopsis: Ten young Florentines flee the plague to a country villa, passing ten days telling a hundred tales of love and wit.

title: The Deerslayer
author: Fenimore Cooper
synopsis: Young Natty Bumppo, a frontier hunter raised among the Delaware, faces his first battle and first kill in the New York wilderness.

title: The Descent of Man
author: Charles Darwin
synopsis: Darwin extends evolution to humankind, arguing that our bodies, minds, and morals descend from animal ancestors and are shaped by sexual selection.

title: The Deserted Village
author: Oliver Goldsmith
synopsis: A poet mourns Auburn, a once-thriving English village emptied by enclosure and greed, its cottagers driven into exile and poverty.

title: The Destruction of Dresden
author: David Irving
synopsis: A controversial account of the Allied firebombing that reduced the German city to ash in February 1945.

title: The Doctrine Of The Mean
author: Confucius
synopsis: A Confucian classic on cultivating equilibrium and sincerity as the constant, unswerving path to moral harmony.

title: The Doctrine of Fascism
author: Benito Mussolini and Giovanni Gentile
synopsis: Mussolini and Gentile set out fascism's creed — the total state above the individual, glorifying war, authority, and national will.

title: The End of Faith: Religion, Terror and the Future of Reason
author: Sam Harris
synopsis: Harris indicts religious faith as a dangerous relic, arguing that dogma and revelation fuel violence in an age of nuclear weapons.

title: The End of the Affair
author: Graham Greene
synopsis: In wartime London, a jealous novelist reconstructs his passionate affair with a married woman who abruptly abandoned him.

title: The English People, a Study of Their Political Psychology
author: Emile Gaston Boutmy
synopsis: A French scholar dissects the political psychology and national character of the English, tracing their institutions to temperament and history.

title: The English Rogue
author: Richard Head
synopsis: The bawdy, picaresque confessions of Meriton Latroon, a scoundrel who cheats and thieves his way across seventeenth-century England.

title: The Excursion
author: William Wordsworth
synopsis: A wandering poet, a disillusioned Solitary, and a wise Wanderer debate faith, despair, and nature across the English hills.

title: The Execution of Private Slovik
author: William Bradford Huie
synopsis: The true story of Eddie Slovik, the only American soldier since the Civil War executed for desertion.

title: The Fable of the Bees
author: Bernard Mandeville
synopsis: A verse fable and essays arguing that private vices — greed, vanity, luxury — paradoxically drive public prosperity.

title: The Faith Instinct
author: Nicholas Wade
synopsis: Wade argues that religion is woven into human nature by evolution, an instinct that once bound early societies together for survival.

title: The Fire Next Time
author: James Baldwin
synopsis: Two searing essays on race, faith, and America's coming reckoning, opening with a letter to Baldwin's young nephew.

title: The First Blast of the Trumpet Against the Monstruous Regiment of Women
author: John Knox
synopsis: Knox thunders against female monarchs, declaring that rule by women defies God, nature, and right order.

title: The Georgics
author: Virgil
synopsis: Virgil's didactic poem on farming — of crops, vines, cattle, and bees — as a meditation on labor, order, and Italy.

title: The Giaour
author: Byron
synopsis: A fragmented Byronic tale of a brooding infidel who avenges Leila, the harem slave drowned for loving him.

title: The God Delusion
author: Richard Dawkins
synopsis: Dawkins mounts a sweeping case against belief in God, arguing that religion is a delusion unsupported by evidence and often harmful.

title: The Good Earth
author: Pearl S Buck
synopsis: The Chinese farmer Wang Lung rises from poverty to wealth, bound always to the land that sustains and defines him.

title: The Great Stagnation
author: Tyler Cowen
synopsis: Cowen argues that America has consumed the easy sources of growth, leaving decades of technological and economic slowdown.

title: The Greening of America
author: Charles A Reich
synopsis: Reich hails a coming cultural revolution in which a new youthful consciousness liberates America from the corporate machine.

title: The Happiness Hypothesis
author: Jonathan Haidt
synopsis: Haidt tests ten ancient ideas about happiness against modern psychology, seeking wisdom in the divided human mind.

title: The Heart of the Matter
author: Graham Greene
synopsis: A scrupulous colonial policeman in wartime West Africa is undone by pity, adultery, and his own Catholic conscience.

title: The Heir of Redclyffe
author: Charlotte M. Yonge
synopsis: A high-spirited young heir battles his inherited temper and a family feud in a Victorian tale of Christian self-conquest.

title: The History of Henry, Earl of Moreland
author: Henry Brooke
synopsis: A nobleman's younger son is raised amid nature and benevolence to become an ideal man of feeling.

title: The History of Rome
author: Oliver Goldsmith
synopsis: Goldsmith's popular narrative of Rome from its founding through kings, republic, and empire to decline.

title: The History of Sandford and Merton
author: Thomas Day
synopsis: A spoiled merchant's son is reformed by the example of a virtuous farmer's boy and their wise tutor.

title: The History of the Devil
author: Daniel Defoe
synopsis: Defoe traces the Devil's career from his fall to his meddling in human affairs, half history and half satire.

title: The History of the Puritans
author: Daniel Neal
synopsis: A sympathetic history of the English Puritans from the Reformation through their long struggle for religious liberty.

title: The Holy War
author: John Bunyan
synopsis: Bunyan's allegory of the town of Mansoul, lost to Diabolus and reclaimed by the prince Emanuel.

title: The House of Life
author: Dante Gabriel Rossetti
synopsis: A sonnet sequence charting love, its consummation, and its loss under the shadow of death and art.

title: The Imitation of Christ
author: Thomas a Kempis
synopsis: A devotional manual counseling humility, detachment from the world, and the inward following of Christ.

title: The Importance of Being Earnest
author: Oscar Wilde
synopsis: Two young men juggle invented alter egos named Ernest to court the women they love, amid mistaken identity and a fearsome aunt. Wilde's sparkling comedy of manners and epigram.

title: The Inferno
author: Dante
synopsis: Guided by Virgil, Dante descends through the nine circles of Hell, meeting the damned at every ledge.

title: The Legend of Sleepy Hollow
author: Washington Irving
synopsis: A superstitious Yankee schoolmaster courting a Dutch heiress is chased from the valley by a headless horseman.

title: The Life of Cowper
author: Robert Southey
synopsis: Southey's biography of the gentle, melancholy poet William Cowper, tracing his faith, his madness, and his verse.

title: The London Merchant
author: George Lillo
synopsis: An apprentice, seduced by a scheming woman, robs his master and murders his uncle in this domestic tragedy.

title: The Maid of the Mill
author: Isaac Bickerstaffe
synopsis: A comic opera in which a miller's virtuous daughter is courted by a young gentleman far above her station.

title: The Maid's Tragedy
author: Francis Beaumont and John Fletcher
synopsis: Ordered to abandon his betrothed and marry the king's secret mistress, Amintor is drawn into a tragedy of revenge.

title: The Marble Faun
author: Nathaniel Hawthorne
synopsis: Four artists in Rome are bound together by a sudden murder that awakens one innocent nature to sin and sorrow.

title: The Martyrdom of Man
author: Winwood Reade
synopsis: A sweeping secular history of humanity's progress through war, religion, liberty, and intellect.

title: The Masqueraders
author: Georgette Heyer
synopsis: A Jacobite brother and sister hide in Georgian London disguised as the opposite sex, courting both danger and love.

title: The Memorable Thoughts of Socrates
author: Xenophon
synopsis: A soldier-companion's recollections of Socrates, defending his master against impiety and showing his plain wisdom in everyday talk.

title: The Merry Wives of Windsor
author: William Shakespeare
synopsis: The fat knight Falstaff woos two respectable wives for their husbands' money, and the wives conspire to humiliate him.

title: The Monk
author: Matthew Lewis
synopsis: A revered Spanish abbot yields to lust and the Devil, tumbling from sanctity into rape, murder, and damnation.

title: The Moon is a Harsh Mistress
author: Robert A Heinlein
synopsis: A lunar penal colony, aided by a self-aware computer, revolts to win its independence from Earth.

title: The Moral Essays
author: Alexander Pope
synopsis: Four verse epistles anatomizing the characters of men and women and the right and ruinous uses of riches.

title: The Mourning Bride
author: William Congreve
synopsis: A Granadan princess secretly wed to a prince she believes drowned grieves amid royal vengeance and disguise — for hell hath no fury like a woman scorned.

title: The Murder of Roger Ackroyd
author: Agatha Christie
synopsis: Poirot investigates a country squire's stabbing, narrated by the village doctor who assists him.

title: The Murders in the Rue Morgue
author: Edgar Allan Poe
synopsis: The reasoner Dupin unravels a savage double murder in a locked Paris room that baffles the police.

title: The Mysteries of Udolpho
author: Ann Radcliffe
synopsis: Orphaned Emily St. Aubert, imprisoned in a brooding Apennine castle by her aunt's sinister husband, confronts terrors that reason will explain.

title: The Nature and Necessity of a Paper Currency
author: Benjamin Franklin
synopsis: A young printer argues that issuing paper money would ease colonial Pennsylvania's scarcity of coin and quicken its trade.

title: The Odyssey
author: Homer
