synopsis: Ten years after Troy's fall, Odysseus struggles homeward past monsters and gods to the wife and son who still await him.

title: The Paypal Wars: Battles with eBay, the Media, the Mafia, and the Rest of Planet Earth
author: Eric M Jackson
synopsis: An insider's account of PayPal's scrappy fight to survive fraud, rivals, and eBay on its way to reinventing online money.

title: The Pickwick Papers
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: The genial Mr. Pickwick and his club ramble across England, tumbling through misadventures, lawsuits, and the comic loyalty of Sam Weller.

title: The Pirate
author: Walter Scott
synopsis: In the remote Shetland Isles, a shipwrecked stranger who proves to be a pirate captain unsettles the loves of two sisters.

title: The Political Brain
author: Drew Westen
synopsis: A psychologist argues that elections are won through emotion, not reason, and faults Democrats for appealing to the head over the heart.

title: The Powers That Be
author: David Halberstam
synopsis: A sweeping history of how CBS, Time, the Washington Post, and the Los Angeles Times grew into empires that shaped American politics.

title: The Prophet
author: Khalil Gibran
synopsis: On the eve of his departure, the sage Almustafa answers a town's questions with poetic counsel on love, work, sorrow, and death.

title: The Rape of the Lock
author: Alexander Pope
synopsis: A stolen lock of hair ignites a mock-epic war among the fashionable belles and beaux of England.

title: The Razor's Edge
author: W. Somerset Maugham
synopsis: A young American, scarred by the Great War, forsakes wealth and convention to wander in search of meaning.

title: The Red and the Black
author: Stendhal
synopsis: An ambitious carpenter's son schemes his way through the church and the salons of Restoration France.

title: The Religion of Nature Delineated
author: William Wollaston
synopsis: A moral philosophy grounding right and wrong in whether our actions affirm or deny the truth of things.

title: The Religions of Japan
author: William Elliot Griffis
synopsis: A survey of Shinto, Buddhism, and Confucianism as they shaped the faith and daily life of Japan.

title: The Renaissance
author: Walter Pater
synopsis: Essays on the art and poetry of the Renaissance that urge the reader to burn with a hard, gemlike flame.

title: The Romance of the Forest
author: Ann Radcliffe
synopsis: A persecuted young woman takes refuge in a ruined abbey where buried family secrets slowly close in around her.

title: The Saints' Everlasting Rest
author: Richard Baxter
synopsis: A Puritan pastor's meditation urging the faithful toward the eternal rest that awaits them in heaven.

title: The Satyricon
author: Petronius
synopsis: The disreputable misadventures of a wandering rogue and his boy lover, centered on a vulgar freedman's lavish banquet.

title: The Sea
author: Barry Cornwall
synopsis: A rolling hymn to the open sea, which the poet claims as his wild home, his joy, and his ever-free element.

title: The Seasons
author: James Thomson
synopsis: A four-part poem tracing the turning year through spring, summer, autumn, and winter across the English countryside.

title: The Shaughraun
author: Dion Boucicault
synopsis: A merry Irish vagabond schemes to rescue a Fenian exile and outwit an informer in a melodrama of old Ireland.

title: The Sketch Book of Geoffrey Crayon
author: Washington Irving
synopsis: A genial traveler's miscellany of English sketches and American tales, home to Rip Van Winkle and Sleepy Hollow.

title: The Sound and Fury
author: William Faulkner
synopsis: The Compson family's decline told in fractured voices, beginning with the wordless grief of an idiot son.

title: The Spirit of the Laws
author: Montesquieu
synopsis: A sweeping comparison of governments arguing that liberty depends on separating legislative, executive, and judicial power.

title: The Swiss Family Robinson
author: Johann David Wyss
synopsis: A shipwrecked pastor and his family build an ingenious island home from the salvage of their wrecked ship.

title: The Tattler
author: John Addison
synopsis: A gentleman's periodical of essays on manners, morals, and the coffeehouse life of early eighteenth-century London.

title: The Tour of Doctor Syntax in Search of the Picturesque
author: William Combe
synopsis: A bumbling clergyman-schoolmaster sets off on horseback in comic pursuit of picturesque scenery and easy fortune.

title: The Vanity of Human Wishes
author: Samuel Johnson
synopsis: A grave survey of humanity's futile ambitions, showing how wealth, power, and fame all end in ruin.

title: The Way of All Flesh
author: Samuel Butler
synopsis: Four generations of a Victorian family expose the quiet tyrannies of respectable religion and paternal love.

title: The Whole Duty of Man
author: Richard Allestree
synopsis: A devotional manual laying out the ordinary Christian's duties to God, to neighbor, and to himself.

title: The Wide, Wide World
author: Susan Warner
synopsis: An orphaned girl, sent from her dying mother into hard hands, learns to endure through piety and tears.

title: The Winter's Tale
author: William Shakespeare
synopsis: A king's sudden jealousy destroys his family, until time and a living statue restore what he had lost.

title: The Wizard of Oz
author: L Frank Baum
synopsis: A Kansas girl swept to a magic land follows a golden road, gathering odd companions on her way home.

title: Theory of the Leisure Class
author: Thorstein Veblen
synopsis: An economist dissects how the wealthy signal status through conspicuous consumption and deliberate waste rather than usefulness.

title: Things Fall Apart
author: Chinua Achebe
synopsis: Okonkwo, a proud Igbo warrior, watches his world and his standing unravel as British colonizers and missionaries arrive in his Nigerian village.

title: Toussant l'Ouverture
author: Wendel Phillips
synopsis: An abolitionist orator hails the self-freed slave who led Haiti's revolution as a soldier and statesman greater than Napoleon.

title: Treason: Liberal Treachery from the Cold War to the War on Terrorism
author: Ann Coulter
synopsis: A combative polemic arguing that American liberals have betrayed the nation from the Cold War through the war on terror.

title: Treatise on the Law of Corporations
author: Stewart Kyd
synopsis: The first English-language treatise on corporate law, defining what a corporation is and how one may be created, governed, and dissolved.

title: Undine and Sintram
author: Friedrich de la Motte Fouqué
synopsis: A water nymph marries a knight to win a human soul, only to be betrayed by his wandering heart.

title: Urn Burial
author: Thomas Browne
synopsis: Roman funeral urns unearthed in Norfolk prompt a sweeping meditation on death, memory, and the vanity of earthly monuments.

title: Valentine and Orson
author: Anonymous
synopsis: Twin brothers separated at birth — one raised at court, the other by a bear in the forest — reunite in chivalric adventure.

title: Valley of the Dolls
author: Jacqueline Susann
synopsis: Three ambitious women chase fame in New York and Hollywood, propped up by pills and worn down by the men around them.

title: Voyage autour de ma Chambre
author: Xavier de Maistre
synopsis: Confined to his room for forty-two days, an officer turns the journey from bed to armchair into a witty philosophical travelogue.

title: Walden
author: Henry David Thoreau
synopsis: Thoreau's account of two years living simply in a cabin by Walden Pond, and his argument for deliberate living and self-reliance. A founding text of American nature writing.

title: Waverley
author: Walter Scott
synopsis: A dreamy young English officer is swept into the doomed Jacobite rising of 1745 in the Scottish Highlands.

title: What's the Matter with Kansas
author: Thomas Frank
synopsis: An argument that working-class Americans have been lured into voting against their own economic interests by cultural grievances.

title: Wilhelm Meister's Apprenticeship
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
synopsis: A merchant's son abandons commerce for the theater, drifting through love and illusion toward a hard-won maturity.

title: Windsor Forest
author: Alexander Pope
synopsis: A pastoral poem celebrating the English countryside and the peace of Queen Anne's reign.

title: Wonderful Ethiopians of the Ancient Cushite Empire
author: Drusilla D Houston
synopsis: A sweeping history arguing that a great Cushite civilization of black Africa shaped the ancient world from Ethiopia to India.

title: the Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
synopsis: Evolution seen from the gene's point of view, where organisms are survival machines built to propagate their DNA.

title: À Rebours
author: Joris-Karl Huysmans
synopsis: A jaded aristocrat withdraws from society into a private world of rare perfumes, art, and exquisite artificial sensations.

title: Émaux et Camées
author: Théophile Gautier
synopsis: A collection of finely chiseled poems that treat verse as sculpture, pursuing beauty for its own sake.

title: Articles of Belief and Acts of Religion
author: Benjamin Franklin
synopsis: The young printer drafts a private liturgy and personal creed for his own solitary worship of a benevolent God.

title: Before an Audience
author: Nathan Sheppard
synopsis: Force of will, not gesture or elocution, is what truly commands a speaker's audience.

title: Belsham's Essays
author: William Belsham
synopsis: Essays ranging across philosophy, history, and letters, defending human liberty against the doctrine of necessity.

title: Brideshead
author: Evelyn Waugh
synopsis: Charles Ryder recalls his enchantment with an aristocratic Catholic family and its slow, grace-haunted decline.

title: Buy Low, Sell High, Collect Early, and Pay Late: The Manager's Guide to Financial Survival
author: Richard I. Levin
synopsis: A manager's plain-spoken guide to reading the numbers and surviving the game of corporate finance.

title: Caesar's Wars
author: Julius Caesar
synopsis: Caesar's own terse account of his conquest of Gaul and the civil war that made him Rome's master.

title: Children of the Sun
author: George Wells Parker
synopsis: Parker marshals early anthropology to argue that Greek and Mesopotamian civilization sprang from African roots.

title: Confession of General Bonaparte to he Abbe Maury
author: Jean Sarrazin
synopsis: An exiled general puts imagined confessions in Napoleon's mouth, a bitter satire on imperial ambition.

title: Contes de Daudet
author: Alphonse Daudet
synopsis: Tales of Provence and wartime France, tender, ironic, and lit with Southern sun.

title: Crowds
author: Gerald Stanley Lee
synopsis: Lee reads modern democracy as one vast, hopeful crowd and asks how to make it good and beautiful.

title: Crown of Wild Olive
author: John Ruskin
synopsis: Four Ruskin lectures on work, traffic, war, and what a nation owes to its own soul.

title: Dresden, History, Stage and Gallery
author: Mary Endell
synopsis: A cultured tour of Dresden's history, its opera and theater, and the treasures of its picture gallery.

title: Edda
author: Snorri Sturluson
synopsis: Snorri gathers the Norse myths and the craft of skaldic verse into the great handbook of the North.

title: Feudal and Modern Japan
author: Arthur May Knapp
synopsis: Knapp explains how Japan cast off feudalism and remade itself into a modern power within a single generation.

title: Friendship's Offering
author: Anonymous
synopsis: A Christmas gift-book annual gathering poems, songs, and prose legends from Byron, Southey, and other fashionable authors of the day.

title: History Philosophically Illustrated
author: George Miller
synopsis: Miller traces the philosophical causes behind European events from the fall of Rome to the French Revolution.

title: How Plants Grow
author: Asa Gray
synopsis: Asa Gray's simple introduction to botany for young students, long a standard American school text.

title: Japan in Transition
author: Stafford Ransome
synopsis: Ransome measures Japan's rapid progress, policy, and methods in the years after its victory over China.

title: Miscellaneous Essays
author: George Saintsbury
synopsis: Saintsbury's critical essays range over English and French prose style, Baudelaire, Renan, and the present state of the novel.

title: Poppies
author: Natsume Soseki
synopsis: Six young men and women navigate love and ambition in Meiji Japan's collision of tradition and modernity.

title: Princess Mary's Gift Book
author: various
synopsis: Stories and poems by Barrie, Kipling, and Doyle, sold to fund Christmas gifts for Britain's wartime soldiers and sailors.

title: Psychology
author: Daniel Putnam
synopsis: An elementary school textbook of mental and moral science, surveying perception, memory, emotion, and will.

title: Talk to Students on the Art of Study
author: Frank Cramer
synopsis: A biologist's practical counsel to students on how to concentrate, reason, and learn effectively.

title: The Gist of Japan
author: R. B. Peery
synopsis: A missionary surveys Japan's islands, people, and religions, and the progress of Christian missions among them.

title: The Infallibility of Human Judgment
author: Lyons
synopsis: Lyons proposes a new art of reasoning that reduces every dispute to self-evident propositions to settle truth.

title: The Midnight Bell
author: Francis Lathom
synopsis: Fleeing his father's murder, a young Saxon count is drawn back to a haunted castle whose bell tolls each midnight.

title: The Mysterious Warning
author: Eliza Parsons
synopsis: A spectral voice warns young Ferdinand against his brother, launching a Gothic tangle of disinheritance, murder, and buried family sin.

title: The Necromancer of the Black Forest
author: Ludwig Flammenberg
synopsis: Interlocking tales of banditry and seeming sorcery in the Black Forest circle a mysterious necromancer whose hauntings prove all too human.

title: The Orphan of the Rhine
author: Eleanor Sleath
synopsis: An orphaned girl and her protector flee across Italy through haunted castles as a hidden noble parentage and villainous schemes unravel.

title: The Principles of Ethics
author: Herbert Spencer
synopsis: Spencer grounds morality in evolution, deriving the laws of right conduct from biology, psychology, and the survival of society.

title: The Working Principles of Rhetoric
author: John Franklin Genung
synopsis: Genung's exhaustive college manual anatomizes effective writing into invention, style, and structure as teachable working principles.

title: Thinking and Learning to Think
author: Nathan C. Schaeffer
synopsis: Schaeffer examines attention, memory, and reasoning to argue that education's true aim is teaching pupils to think, not memorize.

title: Voyages
author: James Cook
synopsis: Captain Cook's own journals of Pacific exploration, charting Tahiti, New Zealand, and the search for a great southern continent.

title: Works and Days
author: Hesiod
synopsis: Hesiod counsels his idle brother on honest labor, the farmer's calendar, and the ages of man in a world where the gods watch over justice.

title: Writing and Speaking
author: Charles Sears Baldwin
synopsis: Baldwin's rhetoric text treats composition and oratory together, teaching how ideas become clear, persuasive writing and speech.

title: Time Traveler
author: Norman and Jeanne MacKenzie
synopsis: A full-length biography of H.G. Wells, tracing the draper's apprentice who became a prophet of science fiction and social reform.

title: College Friends
author: Edmondo De Amicis
synopsis: De Amicis's nostalgic reminiscence of his years as a cadet at the military college of Modena, recalling the friendships, hardships, and youthful ardor of army-school life.

title: Essays
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
synopsis: Emerson's First Series preaches individual conscience and the divinity within each soul, from Self-Reliance to The Over-Soul.

title: The Essays
author: Michel de Montaigne
synopsis: Montaigne turns his skeptical gaze on himself, wandering through fear, friendship, and death to ask, 'What do I know?'

title: The Time Machine
author: H. G. Wells
synopsis: A Victorian inventor travels to the year 802,701 and finds humanity split into the childlike Eloi and the subterranean Morlocks. The novel that launched modern time-travel fiction.

title: The Invisible Man
author: H. G. Wells
synopsis: A scientist discovers how to make himself invisible but cannot reverse it, and his isolation curdles into megalomania and terror. A parable of power without accountability.

title: The Island of Doctor Moreau
author: H. G. Wells
synopsis: A shipwrecked man is stranded on a remote island where a renegade physiologist surgically transforms animals into grotesque half-human 'Beast Folk,' who eventually revert to savagery.

title: The First Men in the Moon
author: H. G. Wells
synopsis: A businessman and an eccentric scientist travel to the Moon in a sphere coated with an antigravity material, where they are captured by the insect-like Selenites of a subterranean lunar civilization.

title: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea
author: Jules Verne
synopsis: A French marine biologist and his companions are taken captive aboard the Nautilus, a technologically marvelous submarine commanded by the enigmatic Captain Nemo, and journey through the world's oceans.

title: King Solomon's Mines
author: H. Rider Haggard
synopsis: Elephant hunter Allan Quatermain leads Sir Henry Curtis and Captain Good across the African wilderness in search of a lost brother and the fabled diamond mines of King Solomon.

title: She
author: H. Rider Haggard
synopsis: Cambridge scholar Horace Holly and his ward Leo Vincey follow an ancient family relic into the African interior, where they encounter Ayesha, the immortal white queen known as She-who-must-be-obeyed.

title: Allan Quatermain
author: H. Rider Haggard
synopsis: The aging hunter Allan Quatermain, restless in retirement after his son's death, sets out with Sir Henry Curtis and Captain Good on a final expedition that leads them to the hidden white civilization of Zu-Vendis.

title: The Prisoner of Zenda
author: Anthony Hope
synopsis: An English gentleman who is the double of a kidnapped king is persuaded to impersonate him and save the throne of Ruritania. The swashbuckling romance that named a whole genre.

title: The Call of the Wild
author: Jack London
synopsis: Buck, a domestic dog stolen into the brutal world of Klondike sled teams, sheds civilization and answers the wild's ancestral pull. A spare tale of instinct and survival.

title: The Jungle Book
author: Rudyard Kipling
synopsis: A collection of fables set mostly in the Indian jungle, following the man-cub Mowgli raised by wolves and taught the law of the jungle by the bear Baloo and the panther Bagheera, alongside tales of other animals.

title: The Wonderful Wizard of Oz
author: L. Frank Baum
synopsis: A Kansas girl swept by cyclone to the land of Oz travels the yellow brick road with a scarecrow, a tin man, and a lion to reach a humbug wizard. The first great American fairy tale.

title: Peter Pan
author: J. M. Barrie
synopsis: The boy who won't grow up flies the Darling children to Neverland, where they meet lost boys, mermaids, and Captain Hook. A bittersweet fantasy about childhood and its end.

title: The Secret Garden
author: Frances Hodgson Burnett
synopsis: Orphaned, sour-tempered Mary Lennox is sent to her uncle's bleak Yorkshire manor, where discovering and reviving a walled, neglected garden with the moor boy Dickon and her invalid cousin Colin heals both the children and the grieving household.

title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: Sherlock Holmes reveals that he survived the Reichenbach Falls and returns to Baker Street, reuniting with Dr. Watson to unravel a fresh run of baffling crimes in this collection of thirteen mystery stories.

title: Main Street
author: Sinclair Lewis
synopsis: Idealistic, city-bred Carol Kennicott marries a country doctor and moves to the stifling small town of Gopher Prairie, Minnesota, where her attempts to reform its smug provincialism meet resistance and quiet defeat.

title: Howards End
author: E. M. Forster
synopsis: Three families of differing classes in Edwardian England — the cultured Schlegels, the pragmatic business-minded Wilcoxes, and the struggling clerk Leonard Bast — collide over love, money, and the fate of a country house and who shall inherit it.

title: Mary Barton
author: Elizabeth Gaskell
synopsis: In the hungry 1840s of industrial Manchester, working-class seamstress Mary Barton is caught between a wealthy mill-owner's son and a poor young mechanic when a murder throws her family into crisis and lays bare the bitter divide between masters and workers.

title: McTeague
author: Frank Norris
synopsis: A slow-witted, unlicensed dentist on San Francisco's Polk Street marries the frugal Trina, whose lottery windfall and miserly hoarding corrode their marriage into jealousy, drink, and murder, driving him to a doomed flight across Death Valley.

title: The House of the Seven Gables
author: Nathaniel Hawthorne
synopsis: An old New England family lives under the shadow of a centuries-old curse laid upon their gabled mansion, as the impoverished spinster Hepzibah, her gentle broken brother Clifford, and their bright young cousin Phoebe confront a scheming judge and the inherited guilt of their Puritan ancestors.

title: Martin Eden
author: Jack London
synopsis: A rough young sailor educates himself into a successful writer to win a bourgeois woman's love, only to find disillusionment and despair once fame finally arrives.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
synopsis: An impoverished ex-student in St. Petersburg murders a pawnbroker to prove his theory that extraordinary men stand above moral law, then is slowly broken by guilt and paranoia until he confesses and turns toward redemption.

title: The Idiot
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
synopsis: The guileless, Christlike Prince Myshkin returns to Russian high society, where his radical innocence collides with greed, wounded pride, and destructive passion to tragic ends.

title: Notes from the Underground
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
synopsis: An embittered, isolated former civil servant delivers a caustic monologue attacking rationalism and human perfectibility, then recounts the humiliations that drove him into spiteful seclusion.

title: Father Goriot
author: Honoré de Balzac
synopsis: An ambitious young law student boards in a shabby Paris pension where he watches an old man beggar himself for two ungrateful, socially ascendant daughters.

title: Eugénie Grandet
author: Honoré de Balzac
synopsis: The sheltered daughter of a provincial miser gives her heart and her savings to her ruined Parisian cousin, only to be abandoned and left to a joyless spinsterhood.

title: The Man in the Iron Mask
author: Alexandre Dumas
synopsis: Aramis schemes to substitute Louis XIV's secret twin brother for the king while the aging musketeers face their final, fatal adventures.

title: Twenty Years After
author: Alexandre Dumas
synopsis: Two decades on, D'Artagnan reunites the four musketeers amid the French Fronde and the English Civil War in a doomed bid to save King Charles I.

title: The Black Tulip
author: Alexandre Dumas
synopsis: Amid Dutch political bloodshed, an imprisoned tulip-grower races a jealous rival to cultivate the fabled black tulip while falling in love with his jailer's daughter.

title: The Wind in the Willows
author: Kenneth Grahame
synopsis: Along an English riverbank, the gentle Mole, loyal Water Rat, wise Badger, and boastful Toad share adventures and rescue Toad's ancestral hall from weasels.

title: Black Beauty
author: Anna Sewell
synopsis: A well-bred horse narrates his life passing through many owners, kind and cruel, offering a plea for the humane treatment of animals.

title: Through the Looking-Glass
author: Lewis Carroll
synopsis: Alice steps through a mirror into a chessboard land of Tweedledum, the White Knight, and Humpty Dumpty. The inventive sequel to her Wonderland adventures.

title: The Princess and the Goblin
author: George MacDonald
synopsis: Young Princess Irene and the miner boy Curdie, guided by her mysterious great-great-grandmother, thwart a plot by the underground goblins to conquer the world above.

title: The Marvelous Land of Oz
author: L. Frank Baum
synopsis: The boy Tip flees the witch Mombi with his living creations Jack Pumpkinhead and the Saw-Horse, joining the Scarecrow and Tin Woodman to reclaim the Emerald City and discover his own true identity.

title: Heidi
author: Johanna Spyri
synopsis: An orphaned girl thrives in the care of her gruff grandfather high in the Swiss Alps, then pines for the mountains when she is sent away to the city.

title: Anne of Avonlea
author: L. M. Montgomery
synopsis: Now sixteen, Anne Shirley begins teaching at the Avonlea school and helps raise a pair of orphan twins while the little community goes on around her.

title: Little Lord Fauntleroy
author: Frances Hodgson Burnett
synopsis: A warm-hearted American boy discovers he is heir to an English earldom and gradually thaws his embittered grandfather.

title: Five Children and It
author: E. Nesbit
synopsis: Five siblings dig up an ancient, irritable sand-fairy that grants them one wish a day, each of which goes comically wrong by sundown.

title: A Princess of Mars
author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
synopsis: A Confederate veteran is mysteriously transported to Mars, where lighter gravity makes him a formidable warrior amid warring alien races and a captive princess.

title: The Lost World
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: The irascible Professor Challenger leads a small expedition to a remote South American plateau where prehistoric dinosaurs and ape-men have survived into the modern age.

title: The Thirty-Nine Steps
author: John Buchan
synopsis: Framed for murder and hunted by both police and foreign agents, Richard Hannay flees across the Scottish moors to unravel and foil a German espionage plot on the eve of war.

title: Tarzan of the Apes
author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
synopsis: Orphaned in the African jungle and raised by great apes, the infant Lord Greystoke grows into Tarzan, then confronts civilization and love when Jane Porter arrives on his shores.

title: The Phantom of the Opera
author: Gaston Leroux
synopsis: A disfigured musical genius haunting the cellars of the Paris Opera falls obsessively in love with the young soprano Christine Daae and terrorizes the theatre to possess her.

title: The White Company
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: In the fourteenth century, the abbey-raised youth Alleyne Edricson joins Sir Nigel Loring and a band of English archers, marching through France and Spain amid the campaigns of the Hundred Years' War.

title: White Fang
author: Jack London
synopsis: A wild wolf-dog is beaten and abused before human kindness slowly tames him — the mirror image of The Call of the Wild. A story of nature, cruelty, and redemption in the Yukon.

title: The Sea-Wolf
author: Jack London
synopsis: Rescued from a shipwreck by the brutal, brilliant sealing captain Wolf Larsen, the sheltered critic Humphrey Van Weyden is forced to toughen himself aboard the Ghost and ultimately fight for survival and love against the Nietzschean tyrant.

title: The Iron Heel
author: Jack London
synopsis: Framed as a rediscovered memoir of Avis Everhard, the novel recounts the rise of a tyrannical capitalist Oligarchy, the 'Iron Heel,' and the doomed socialist revolt against it in an early-twentieth-century America.

title: Tono-Bungay
author: H. G. Wells
synopsis: Narrator George Ponderevo chronicles how he and his uncle Edward built a vast, hollow commercial empire on a worthless patent tonic, Tono-Bungay, before it all collapses in fraud and ruin.

title: The Mysterious Island
author: Jules Verne
synopsis: Five Union prisoners escape the American Civil War by balloon and are marooned on an uncharted Pacific island, where their ingenuity and a hidden benefactor help them survive.

title: Typhoon
author: Joseph Conrad
synopsis: The stolidly unimaginative Captain MacWhirr drives his steamer straight into a monstrous China Sea typhoon, testing his crew and the Chinese coolies battened below deck.

title: The Light That Failed
author: Rudyard Kipling
synopsis: Dick Heldar, a war artist who wins fame after the Sudan campaigns, slowly goes blind while pining for the indifferent Maisie.

title: The Turn of the Screw
author: Henry James
synopsis: A young governess at a remote country house becomes convinced that two ghosts are corrupting the two children in her care.

title: The American
author: Henry James
synopsis: Christopher Newman, a self-made American businessman in Paris, courts an aristocratic widow only to be thwarted by her proud, secretive family.

title: The Old Curiosity Shop
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: Angelic Little Nell Trent and her ruined grandfather flee their London curiosity shop and wander the English countryside to escape the malignant dwarf moneylender Daniel Quilp.

title: The Mystery of Edwin Drood
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: In the cathedral town of Cloisterham, the opium-haunted choirmaster John Jasper nurses a jealous obsession bound up with the sudden disappearance of his nephew Edwin Drood, in Dickens's novel left unfinished at his death.

title: A Pair of Blue Eyes
author: Thomas Hardy
synopsis: Elfride Swancourt, a Cornish clergyman's daughter, is torn between the young architect Stephen Smith and his older mentor, the exacting reviewer Henry Knight, with tragic consequences.

title: The Woodlanders
author: Thomas Hardy
synopsis: In the woodland hamlet of Little Hintock, Grace Melbury is caught between the steadfast apple-grower Giles Winterborne and the restless incomer doctor Edred Fitzpiers, amid the villagers' intimate dependence on the surrounding trees.

title: Doctor Thorne
author: Anthony Trollope
synopsis: A penniless young Barsetshire squire loves the village doctor's illegitimate niece, whose hidden claim to a fortune may dissolve the barriers of money and rank between them.

title: Framley Parsonage
author: Anthony Trollope
synopsis: A young Barsetshire clergyman risks his living and his family's peace by recklessly backing a dissipated politician's debts, while his devoted sister resists a courtship above her station.

title: The Last Chronicle of Barset
author: Anthony Trollope
synopsis: An impoverished, proud curate stands accused of stealing a cheque, and the scandal grips all Barsetshire as his fate and his daughter's love hang in the balance.

title: Armadale
author: Wilkie Collins
synopsis: Two men who share the name Allan Armadale are drawn together under the shadow of an ancestral murder while a beautiful, scheming woman plots to seize a fortune.

title: No Name
author: Wilkie Collins
synopsis: When a legal technicality strips two sisters of their inheritance and legitimacy, the younger schemes through disguise and marriage to reclaim what was taken from them.

title: The Jungle
author: Upton Sinclair
synopsis: A Lithuanian immigrant family is ground down by the meatpacking industry of Chicago's stockyards. A muckraking novel whose scenes of filth and exploitation drove real food-safety reform.

title: The Rise of Silas Lapham
author: William Dean Howells
synopsis: A self-made Boston paint manufacturer faces financial ruin and the social snobbery of the Brahmin elite while striving to hold on to his moral integrity.

title: Babbitt
author: Sinclair Lewis
synopsis: A prosperous, conformist real-estate booster in the Midwestern city of Zenith grows restless with middle-class convention, briefly rebels, then returns to the fold.

title: Pudd'nhead Wilson
author: Mark Twain
synopsis: In an antebellum Missouri town a slave secretly switches her light-skinned infant with her master's son, and years later a fingerprint-collecting lawyer unravels the swap at a murder trial.

title: Daisy Miller
author: Henry James
synopsis: A spontaneous, unconventional young American woman traveling in Europe scandalizes expatriate society with her flirtatious independence, baffling the reserved admirer who cannot decide whether she is innocent or improper.

title: The Last of the Mohicans
author: James Fenimore Cooper
synopsis: During the French and Indian War, the scout Hawkeye and his Mohican companions guide two sisters through hostile wilderness. A foundational American frontier romance.

title: Candide
author: Voltaire
synopsis: A naive young man taught that this is 'the best of all possible worlds' is battered by disaster after disaster across the globe. A savage philosophical satire on optimism and human folly.

title: The Portrait of a Lady
author: Henry James
synopsis: Isabel Archer, a spirited young American who inherits a fortune, chooses a marriage that slowly reveals itself as a trap laid by scheming expatriates. A subtle drama of independence, illusion, and consequence.

title: Sister Carrie
author: Theodore Dreiser
synopsis: A country girl comes to Chicago and rises through the city on charm and circumstance while the man who helped her sinks into ruin. A naturalist chronicle of ambition, money, and drift.

title: The Hunchback of Notre-Dame
author: Victor Hugo
synopsis: In medieval Paris the deformed bell-ringer Quasimodo, the archdeacon Frollo, and the gypsy Esmeralda are bound in tragedy around the great cathedral. A gothic romance and a hymn to the city and its architecture.

title: Dubliners
author: James Joyce
synopsis: Fifteen linked stories of ordinary lives in early-1900s Dublin, each building to a quiet moment of paralysis or revelation. Joyce's precise portrait of a city and its spiritual stagnation.

title: A Portrait of the Artist as a Young Man
author: James Joyce
synopsis: Stephen Dedalus grows from a devout Catholic boy into a young man who rejects family, faith, and nation to become an artist. A semi-autobiographical account of an intellectual awakening.

title: Around the World in Eighty Days
author: Jules Verne
synopsis: The precise Englishman Phileas Fogg wagers he can circle the globe in eighty days and races on with his valet Passepartout, dogged by a detective. A breathless tour of trains, ships, and near misses.

title: The Strange Case of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
synopsis: A respectable London doctor concocts a potion that releases his monstrous alter ego, and the two selves war for control. The definitive story of the divided human nature.

title: Grimms' Fairy Tales
author: Jacob and Wilhelm Grimm
synopsis: The Brothers Grimm's collected German folk tales — Cinderella, Hansel and Gretel, Rapunzel, and dozens more — in their often dark early forms. The wellspring of the European fairy tale.

title: Pygmalion
author: George Bernard Shaw
synopsis: Phonetics professor Henry Higgins bets he can pass a Cockney flower girl off as a duchess by fixing her speech, and reaps unintended consequences. A witty attack on class and the English accent.

title: A Doll's House
author: Henrik Ibsen
synopsis: Nora Helmer, treated as a plaything by her husband, discovers her own mind and walks out of her marriage. The play whose slamming door helped launch modern drama and the woman question.

title: Beyond Good and Evil
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
synopsis: Nietzsche attacks traditional morality and the philosophers who built it, arguing for a revaluation of values beyond good and evil. An aphoristic prelude to a philosophy of the future.

title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
synopsis: The prophet Zarathustra descends from his mountain to teach the death of God, the overman, and eternal recurrence in poetic parables. Nietzsche's most ambitious and mythic work.

title: Meditations
author: Marcus Aurelius
synopsis: The private notebooks of a Roman emperor, reminding himself to live with reason, duty, and equanimity. The central practical text of Stoic philosophy.

title: The Prince
author: Niccolò Machiavelli
synopsis: A blunt handbook on how rulers gain and keep power, prizing effectiveness over conventional virtue. The book that made 'Machiavellian' a word.

title: Utopia
author: Thomas More
synopsis: A traveler describes an imaginary island commonwealth with no private property, gently satirizing the Europe of More's day. The work that named an entire genre of ideal societies.

title: The Communist Manifesto
author: Karl Marx and Friedrich Engels
synopsis: A short, incendiary pamphlet arguing that all history is class struggle and calling the workers of the world to unite. The founding document of modern communism.

title: The Souls of Black Folk
author: W. E. B. Du Bois
synopsis: Essays on race in post-emancipation America that introduce 'double consciousness' and 'the veil'. A landmark of sociology and African American letters.

title: Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass
author: Frederick Douglass
synopsis: Douglass's account of his birth into slavery, his secret path to literacy, and his escape to freedom. The most influential of the American slave narratives.

title: Tess of the d'Urbervilles
author: Thomas Hardy
synopsis: A poor country girl is seduced by a callous gentleman, then rejected by the man she loves when her past comes out. Hardy's tragedy of a pure woman crushed by Victorian double standards and fate.

title: Under the Greenwood Tree
author: Thomas Hardy
synopsis: A gentle pastoral comedy of courtship in a Wessex village, centered on a tranter's son wooing the new schoolmistress as the old church choir is disbanded. Hardy's sunniest novel.

title: The History of Henry Esmond
author: William Makepeace Thackeray
synopsis: A Jacobite colonel narrates his devotion to the woman he loves and the family he serves across the intrigues of Queen Anne's England. Thackeray's stately historical novel of unrequited love.

title: Romola
author: George Eliot
synopsis: In Renaissance Florence, a scholar's daughter watches her charming husband betray everyone around him while the reformer Savonarola rises and falls. Eliot's densely researched historical novel.

title: Cranford
author: Elizabeth Gaskell
synopsis: Gentle, comic sketches of the genteel widows and spinsters of a small English town, whose small economies and kindnesses fill their quiet lives. Gaskell's most beloved book.

title: Ruth
author: Elizabeth Gaskell
synopsis: A seamstress seduced and abandoned raises her son in secrecy as a respectable widow, until her past threatens to destroy them. Gaskell's compassionate plea against the ruin of the fallen woman.

title: Phineas Finn
author: Anthony Trollope
synopsis: An idealistic young Irishman enters Parliament and navigates love, money, and principle in Victorian political society. The second of Trollope's Palliser novels.

title: The Egoist
author: George Meredith
synopsis: A brilliant, self-regarding gentleman is dissected through his treatment of the women he courts, until his egoism undoes him. Meredith's sharp comic study of male vanity.

title: Erewhon
author: Samuel Butler
synopsis: A traveler discovers a hidden country where illness is a crime, machines are banned as future rivals, and all values are inverted. Butler's satirical utopia.

title: New Grub Street
author: George Gissing
synopsis: Two writers, one a mercenary hack and one an uncompromising artist, are ground against each other by the London literary marketplace. Gissing's bleak novel of art and money.

title: The Old Wives' Tale
author: Arnold Bennett
synopsis: The linked lives of two sisters from a Staffordshire draper's shop, one who stays home and one who elopes to Paris, followed across fifty years. Bennett's panoramic realist masterpiece.

title: Green Mansions
author: W. H. Hudson
synopsis: An exiled Venezuelan finds a mysterious bird-girl in the jungle and loves her, only to see paradise destroyed. Hudson's exotic romance of nature and loss.

title: The Riddle of the Sands
author: Erskine Childers
synopsis: Two young yachtsmen cruising the Frisian coast stumble on a secret German plan to invade England. An early and hugely influential spy thriller.

title: Greenmantle
author: John Buchan
synopsis: Richard Hannay goes undercover across wartime Europe to foil a German plot to raise a jihad in the East. Buchan's rollicking WWI spy adventure.

title: The Four Feathers
author: A. E. W. Mason
synopsis: Branded a coward for resigning his commission, a young officer redeems his honor through secret heroics in the Sudan campaign. A classic tale of courage and disgrace.

title: Rupert of Hentzau
author: Anthony Hope
synopsis: The swashbuckling sequel to The Prisoner of Zenda, as Rudolf Rassendyll returns to Ruritania to protect the queen and the throne. Cloak-and-sword romance.

title: The Scarlet Pimpernel
author: Baroness Orczy
synopsis: A foppish English aristocrat secretly rescues French nobles from the guillotine under a daring alias. Orczy's founding tale of the masked hero.

title: Lady Audley's Secret
author: Mary Elizabeth Braddon
synopsis: A beautiful, ambitious woman's respectable second marriage hides bigamy, arson, and attempted murder. A defining Victorian sensation novel.

title: Trilby
author: George du Maurier
synopsis: In bohemian Paris, an artists' model falls under the hypnotic control of the sinister musician Svengali. Du Maurier's romance that gave the language a byword for manipulation.

title: The Diary of a Nobody
author: George Grossmith
synopsis: The self-important diary of a suburban London clerk, chronicling his small mishaps and social anxieties with unwitting comedy. A gentle satire of middle-class respectability.

title: The Napoleon of Notting Hill
author: G. K. Chesterton
synopsis: In a future London chosen by lottery, a whimsical king and a fanatical patriot turn a borough boundary dispute into war. Chesterton's fable of romance versus modern uniformity.

title: The Man Who Was Thursday
author: G. K. Chesterton
synopsis: A poet-detective infiltrates a council of anarchists code-named for days of the week, only to find nothing is what it seems. Chesterton's metaphysical thriller subtitled 'A Nightmare.'

title: The Innocence of Father Brown
author: G. K. Chesterton
synopsis: The first cases of a mild Catholic priest who solves crimes through moral insight and shrewd understanding of human sin. Chesterton's beloved detective stories.

title: Orthodoxy
author: G. K. Chesterton
synopsis: Chesterton's witty spiritual autobiography, arguing his way from doubt to Christian orthodoxy through paradox and wonder. A cornerstone of modern apologetics.

title: The History of Mr Polly
author: H. G. Wells
synopsis: A dyspeptic, unhappy shopkeeper burns down his shop and runs away to find contentment as a country innkeeper. Wells's warm comic novel of escape from a dreary life.

title: Kipps
author: H. G. Wells
synopsis: A draper's assistant inherits a fortune, tries to climb into genteel society, and finds happiness only by returning to his own class. Wells's comedy of English snobbery.

title: Love and Mr Lewisham
author: H. G. Wells
synopsis: An ambitious young science teacher's grand plans for the world collide with love, marriage, and ordinary compromise. Wells's semi-autobiographical novel of thwarted idealism.

title: The Food of the Gods
author: H. G. Wells
synopsis: A miraculous growth compound escapes the laboratory and produces giant plants, vermin, and children who threaten the ordinary world. Wells's fable of change too big to contain.

title: The Bostonians
author: Henry James
synopsis: A Boston feminist and her conservative Southern cousin battle for the soul and love of a gifted young speaker. James's satirical novel of reform and possession.

title: The Wings of the Dove
author: Henry James
synopsis: A dying American heiress, her scheming friend, and the man they both love form a slow tragedy of money and betrayal. One of James's late masterpieces.

title: Washington Square
author: Henry James
synopsis: A plain, dutiful heiress is courted by a fortune-hunter and forbidden by her cold father, and quietly chooses her own dignity. James's spare, painful New York drama.

title: The Europeans
author: Henry James
synopsis: A pair of sophisticated European cousins descend on their staid New England relatives, unsettling the household. James's light comedy of transatlantic manners.

title: The Blithedale Romance
author: Nathaniel Hawthorne
synopsis: A disillusioned narrator recalls a failed utopian farming commune and the magnetic, doomed women at its center. Hawthorne's novel drawn from his Brook Farm days.

title: Billy Budd
author: Herman Melville
synopsis: A saintly young sailor, falsely accused by a malignant officer, strikes and kills him and is hanged under naval law. Melville's posthumous meditation on innocence and justice.

title: Typee
author: Herman Melville
synopsis: A sailor jumps ship in the Marquesas and lives among a Polynesian tribe rumored to be cannibals. Melville's romantic South Seas adventure drawn from his own escapade.

title: Bartleby, the Scrivener
author: Herman Melville
synopsis: A Wall Street lawyer is confounded by a copyist who answers every request with 'I would prefer not to,' and slowly withdraws from life. Melville's haunting tale of passive refusal.

title: The Pioneers
author: James Fenimore Cooper
synopsis: The aging frontiersman Natty Bumppo clashes with encroaching settlement on the New York frontier. The first-written of Cooper's Leatherstocking Tales.

title: The Prairie
author: James Fenimore Cooper
synopsis: The old trapper Natty Bumppo, now on the western plains, aids settlers among warring tribes in his final adventure. The last of the Leatherstocking Tales.

title: The Octopus
author: Frank Norris
synopsis: California wheat ranchers wage a doomed war against the railroad monopoly that controls their land and prices. Norris's epic of the octopus of corporate power.

title: Maggie: A Girl of the Streets
author: Stephen Crane
synopsis: A girl raised in the Bowery slums is driven to prostitution and ruin by poverty and hypocrisy. Crane's stark, pioneering work of American naturalism.

title: The Country of the Pointed Firs
author: Sarah Orne Jewett
synopsis: A summer visitor's quiet sketches of a declining Maine fishing village and its resilient women. Jewett's luminous classic of regional life.

title: Winesburg, Ohio
author: Sherwood Anderson
synopsis: Linked stories of lonely, thwarted lives in a small Ohio town, seen through a young reporter about to leave it. Anderson's influential portrait of American isolation.

title: O Pioneers!
author: Willa Cather
synopsis: A strong-willed Swedish immigrant woman turns a hard Nebraska homestead into a thriving farm while love passes her by. Cather's pioneering prairie novel.

title: The Song of the Lark
author: Willa Cather
synopsis: A gifted girl from a Colorado railroad town claws her way to become a great opera singer. Cather's portrait of the artist's ruthless ambition.

title: The Custom of the Country
author: Edith Wharton
synopsis: A ruthlessly beautiful social climber divorces and marries her way up through Gilded Age society. Wharton's biting satire of American money and ambition.

title: Looking Backward
author: Edward Bellamy
synopsis: A Bostonian wakes in the year 2000 to find a peaceful socialist utopia of plenty and equality. Bellamy's hugely influential utopian romance.

title: The Innocents Abroad
author: Mark Twain
synopsis: Twain's comic account of a shipload of American tourists blundering through Europe and the Holy Land. His irreverent best-selling travel book.

title: Life on the Mississippi
author: Mark Twain
synopsis: Twain's memoir of his years as a riverboat pilot and his later return to a changed Mississippi. Part nostalgia, part sharp social observation.

title: Roughing It
author: Mark Twain
synopsis: Twain's tall-tale memoir of stagecoach travel, silver fever, and frontier life in the American West and Hawaii. Comic autobiography at full gallop.

title: Uncle Remus
author: Joel Chandler Harris
synopsis: The trickster tales of Br'er Rabbit outwitting Br'er Fox, framed as told by an old plantation storyteller. Harris's influential, and now controversial, collection of African American folklore.

title: Little Men
author: Louisa May Alcott
synopsis: Jo March and her husband run a homey school for boys, whose scrapes and growth fill the sequel to Little Women. Alcott's warm domestic tale.

title: An Old-Fashioned Girl
author: Louisa May Alcott
synopsis: A simple country girl visits her rich, fashionable city relations and gently exposes their follies. Alcott's contrast of honest and frivolous values.

title: Eight Cousins
author: Louisa May Alcott
synopsis: An orphaned heiress is raised by her wise uncle and seven boisterous boy cousins, learning health and character. Alcott's cheerful domestic story.

title: Rebecca of Sunnybrook Farm
author: Kate Douglas Wiggin
synopsis: An imaginative, irrepressible girl is sent to live with her stern aunts in Maine and wins everyone over. A warm-hearted American children's classic.

title: Pollyanna
author: Eleanor H. Porter
synopsis: An orphan whose relentless 'glad game' transforms a gloomy New England town, until an accident tests her optimism. The novel that made 'Pollyanna' a word.

title: The Water-Babies
author: Charles Kingsley
synopsis: A mistreated chimney-sweep drowns and is reborn as a water-baby, undergoing a moral education among river and sea creatures. Kingsley's didactic Victorian fairy tale.

title: Westward Ho!
author: Charles Kingsley
synopsis: A young Devon man sails against the Spanish in the age of Elizabeth, from the Caribbean to the Armada. Kingsley's rousing patriotic sea adventure.

title: At the Back of the North Wind
author: George MacDonald
synopsis: A little boy is carried on magical journeys by the beautiful, terrible North Wind, who is also death. MacDonald's tender, mystical children's fantasy.

title: Phantastes
author: George MacDonald
synopsis: A young man wanders a dreamlike Fairy Land of shadows, knights, and temptations in search of an ideal. MacDonald's pioneering adult fantasy.

title: The Railway Children
author: E. Nesbit
synopsis: Three children whose father is wrongly imprisoned move to the country, where the railway becomes the center of their adventures. Nesbit's beloved family story.

title: The Story of the Treasure Seekers
author: E. Nesbit
synopsis: Six children try ingenious and disastrous schemes to restore their family's lost fortune. Nesbit's witty, warm-hearted tale narrated by one of them.

title: Leaves of Grass
author: Walt Whitman
synopsis: Whitman's sprawling free-verse celebration of the self, the body, democracy, and America. The revolutionary book of poems he revised all his life.

title: The Divine Comedy
author: Dante Alighieri
synopsis: Dante's journey through Hell, Purgatory, and Paradise, guided by Virgil and Beatrice toward the vision of God. The supreme poem of the Middle Ages.

title: The Faerie Queene
author: Edmund Spenser
synopsis: An allegorical epic of knights embodying the virtues, questing through a fairyland in praise of Elizabeth I. Spenser's vast unfinished masterpiece.

title: The Canterbury Tales
author: Geoffrey Chaucer
synopsis: A company of pilgrims riding to Canterbury trade tales bawdy, pious, and comic. Chaucer's founding masterpiece of English poetry.

title: The Song of Hiawatha
author: Henry Wadsworth Longfellow
synopsis: The legendary life of an Ojibwe hero told in chanting trochaic verse. Longfellow's once-ubiquitous American Indian epic.

title: Evangeline
author: Henry Wadsworth Longfellow
synopsis: Acadian lovers are separated by the expulsion from Nova Scotia and reunited only at death. Longfellow's tender narrative poem.

title: Goblin Market
author: Christina Rossetti
synopsis: Two sisters are tempted by the fruit of sinister goblin merchants, and one's love saves the other. Rossetti's rich, symbolic fairy-tale poem.

title: The Rime of the Ancient Mariner
author: Samuel Taylor Coleridge
synopsis: A mariner who kills an albatross endures supernatural punishment and learns to bless all living things. Coleridge's haunting ballad of guilt and penance.

title: Songs of Innocence and of Experience
author: William Blake
synopsis: Paired lyric poems contrasting childhood joy and adult corruption, illustrated by Blake himself. Includes 'The Tyger' and 'The Lamb.'

title: The Marriage of Heaven and Hell
author: William Blake
synopsis: Blake's visionary prose-poem overturning conventional morality, with its Proverbs of Hell and marriage of contraries. A radical Romantic manifesto.

title: Childe Harold's Pilgrimage
author: Byron
synopsis: A world-weary wanderer roams Europe brooding on history, ruins, and liberty. Byron's poem that made him famous and defined the Byronic hero.

title: Endymion
author: John Keats
synopsis: A shepherd-prince's mythic quest for the moon-goddess, opening 'A thing of beauty is a joy for ever.' Keats's lush early epic.

title: Gitanjali
author: Rabindranath Tagore
synopsis: Song-offerings of devotion in which the soul seeks the divine beloved. Tagore's mystical Bengali lyrics, which won him the Nobel Prize.

title: Spoon River Anthology
author: Edgar Lee Masters
synopsis: The dead of a Midwestern town speak their own honest epitaphs from the hill cemetery. Masters's landmark of free-verse American realism.

title: The Golden Ass
author: Apuleius
synopsis: A young man magically turned into a donkey passes through many masters and adventures before the goddess Isis restores him. Apuleius's bawdy, fantastical Latin novel.

title: Jerusalem Delivered
author: Torquato Tasso
synopsis: The First Crusade's siege of Jerusalem, woven with sorcery, love, and heroism. Tasso's great Italian Renaissance epic.

title: The Lusiads
author: Luís de Camões
synopsis: Vasco da Gama's voyage to India framed as a national epic of Portuguese discovery, with gods and storms. Camões's masterpiece.

title: Medea
author: Euripides
synopsis: Betrayed by Jason for another bride, the sorceress Medea takes a monstrous revenge by killing their children. Euripides's searing tragedy of a wronged woman.

title: The Bacchae
author: Euripides
synopsis: The god Dionysus punishes the king who denies his worship, driving the women of Thebes to tear him apart. Euripides's terrifying study of ecstasy and repression.

title: The Trojan Women
author: Euripides
synopsis: The captive women of fallen Troy await enslavement as their city burns and their children are killed. Euripides's devastating anti-war tragedy.

title: Prometheus Bound
author: Aeschylus
synopsis: Chained to a rock for giving fire to humankind, the defiant Titan endures Zeus's torments. The great tragedy of rebellion attributed to Aeschylus.

title: The Frogs
author: Aristophanes
synopsis: Dionysus descends to Hades to bring back a great tragedian and judges a contest between Aeschylus and Euripides. Aristophanes's comic masterpiece of literary criticism.

title: The Clouds
author: Aristophanes
synopsis: A farmer sends his son to Socrates's 'Thinkery' to learn how to argue his way out of debt, with disastrous results. Aristophanes's satire on the new philosophy.

title: Lysistrata
author: Aristophanes
synopsis: The women of Greece withhold sex from their husbands until the men end the war. Aristophanes's bawdy anti-war comedy.

title: The Birds
author: Aristophanes
synopsis: Two Athenians persuade the birds to build a city in the sky between men and gods. Aristophanes's soaring fantastical comedy.

title: Tamburlaine the Great
author: Christopher Marlowe
synopsis: A Scythian shepherd rises through ruthless conquest to become the scourge of Asia. Marlowe's mighty two-part tragedy of overreaching ambition.

title: The Jew of Malta
author: Christopher Marlowe
synopsis: The vengeful merchant Barabas schemes and murders his way through Malta after his wealth is seized. Marlowe's dark, ironic revenge play.

title: Volpone
author: Ben Jonson
synopsis: A rich Venetian feigns dying to extract gifts from greedy would-be heirs, aided by his parasite Mosca. Jonson's brilliant satirical comedy of greed.

title: The Alchemist
author: Ben Jonson
synopsis: Three con artists run a fraudulent alchemy racket out of a London house while the master is away. Jonson's fast, intricate comedy of gulls and swindlers.

title: The Duchess of Malfi
author: John Webster
synopsis: A widowed duchess secretly marries beneath her and is hunted to death by her monstrous brothers. Webster's blood-soaked Jacobean tragedy.

title: She Stoops to Conquer
author: Oliver Goldsmith
synopsis: A young man, tricked into thinking a country house is an inn, woos its daughter while mistaking her for a barmaid. Goldsmith's genial comedy of errors.

title: Tartuffe
author: Molière
synopsis: A pious fraud worms his way into a household and nearly ruins it before being exposed. Molière's great comedy of religious hypocrisy.

title: The Miser
author: Molière
synopsis: An obsessive miser's love of his money-box wrecks his children's happiness and his own. Molière's classic comedy of avarice.

title: The Wild Duck
author: Henrik Ibsen
synopsis: An idealist's insistence on absolute truth shatters a family living on comforting illusions. Ibsen's tragicomedy of the 'life-lie.'

title: Ghosts
author: Henrik Ibsen
synopsis: A widow's respectable façade collapses as inherited disease and past sins surface in her son. Ibsen's scandalous attack on Victorian hypocrisy.

title: Hedda Gabler
author: Henrik Ibsen
synopsis: A bored, manipulative woman destroys those around her and herself rather than face a life she despises. Ibsen's icy study of a trapped, dangerous mind.

title: An Enemy of the People
author: Henrik Ibsen
synopsis: A doctor who exposes his town's contaminated baths is branded a traitor for threatening its prosperity. Ibsen's drama of the individual against the majority.

title: Uncle Vanya
author: Anton Chekhov
synopsis: On a decaying estate, thwarted lives simmer with regret and unspoken love during a professor's disruptive visit. Chekhov's quietly devastating play.

title: The Playboy of the Western World
author: J. M. Synge
synopsis: A young man is lionized by an Irish village for boasting he killed his father, until the father turns up alive. Synge's poetic, riot-provoking comedy.

title: Salomé
author: Oscar Wilde
synopsis: The princess demands the head of the prophet who spurned her, dancing for it before her stepfather. Wilde's lush, decadent one-act tragedy.

title: Lady Windermere's Fan
author: Oscar Wilde
synopsis: A virtuous wife nearly ruins herself over a misunderstanding involving the woman who is secretly her mother. Wilde's sparkling society comedy.

title: An Ideal Husband
author: Oscar Wilde
synopsis: A blackmailer threatens to expose a statesman's past as his idealistic wife demands perfection. Wilde's witty comedy of politics and forgiveness.

title: Arms and the Man
author: George Bernard Shaw
synopsis: A fugitive soldier who prefers chocolate to bullets upends a romantic Balkan household. Shaw's anti-heroic comedy of war and love.

title: Man and Superman
author: George Bernard Shaw
synopsis: A confirmed bachelor is pursued by a determined woman embodying the Life Force, framed by a dream debate in Hell. Shaw's comedy of ideas about sex and evolution.

title: Major Barbara
author: George Bernard Shaw
synopsis: A Salvation Army idealist confronts her armaments-manufacturer father over money, poverty, and power. Shaw's provocative debate-comedy.

title: Mrs Warren's Profession
author: George Bernard Shaw
synopsis: A respectable young woman learns her mother's fortune comes from running brothels, forcing a reckoning with hypocrisy. Shaw's censored social drama.

title: Cyrano de Bergerac
author: Edmond Rostand
synopsis: A brilliant swordsman and poet with a grotesque nose woos his beloved on behalf of a handsome rival. Rostand's beloved verse drama of self-sacrificing love.

title: Fathers and Sons
author: Ivan Turgenev
synopsis: A young nihilist scorns the older generation's ideals until love and death humble him. Turgenev's landmark novel of Russia's generational conflict.

title: A Sportsman's Sketches
author: Ivan Turgenev
synopsis: A hunter's quiet sketches of serf and gentry life that exposed the cruelty of Russian serfdom. Turgenev's influential linked stories.

title: First Love
author: Ivan Turgenev
synopsis: A boy of sixteen falls hopelessly for an older girl, only to discover she is his own father's mistress. Turgenev's exquisite novella of first love.

title: Oblomov
author: Ivan Goncharov
synopsis: A kindly, paralyzingly lazy nobleman lets life, love, and his estate slip away rather than rise from his sofa. Goncharov's great comic-tragic portrait of inertia.

title: A Hero of Our Time
author: Mikhail Lermontov
synopsis: A bored, brilliant, cynical army officer wounds everyone around him in a set of linked Caucasus tales. Lermontov's foundational Russian novel of the 'superfluous man.'

title: Taras Bulba
author: Nikolai Gogol
synopsis: A fierce old Cossack and his two sons ride to war against the Poles, ending in betrayal and death. Gogol's romantic, brutal historical tale.

title: The Kreutzer Sonata
author: Leo Tolstoy
synopsis: A jealous husband confesses on a train to murdering his wife, in a fevered attack on marriage and desire. Tolstoy's controversial novella.

title: Resurrection
author: Leo Tolstoy
synopsis: A nobleman on a jury recognizes the prostitute he once ruined and devotes himself to saving her. Tolstoy's late novel of guilt and social protest.

title: The Cossacks
author: Leo Tolstoy
synopsis: A dissolute young Moscow aristocrat seeks meaning among the Cossacks of the Caucasus and loves a village girl. Tolstoy's vivid early novel.

title: The Kingdom of God Is Within You
author: Leo Tolstoy
synopsis: Tolstoy's argument for nonviolent resistance and Christian anarchism, which later inspired Gandhi. His radical case against the state and the church.

title: Germinal
author: Émile Zola
synopsis: A coal miners' strike in northern France builds to a doomed, violent uprising against the owners. Zola's towering novel of labor and capital.

title: L'Assommoir
author: Émile Zola
synopsis: A Paris laundress's modest hopes are slowly drowned by poverty and drink. Zola's harrowing study of working-class ruin.

title: Bel-Ami
author: Guy de Maupassant
synopsis: A handsome, unscrupulous journalist sleeps and schemes his way to the top of Paris society. Maupassant's cynical novel of ambition.

title: Une Vie
author: Guy de Maupassant
synopsis: A sheltered young woman's romantic hopes for marriage curdle into disappointment across a long life. Maupassant's tender, bleak first novel.

title: Sentimental Education
author: Gustave Flaubert
synopsis: A young man's passion for an older married woman drifts across the failures of a generation and the 1848 revolution. Flaubert's great novel of disillusion.

title: Salammbô
author: Gustave Flaubert
synopsis: Amid the wars of ancient Carthage, a mercenary revolt centers on a doomed love for the priestess Salammbô. Flaubert's lush, violent historical epic.

title: The Charterhouse of Parma
author: Stendhal
synopsis: A young Italian nobleman pursues love and intrigue through the petty courts of post-Napoleonic Italy. Stendhal's sparkling novel of romance and politics.

title: Lost Illusions
author: Honoré de Balzac
synopsis: A provincial poet is chewed up and corrupted by the literary and financial worlds of Paris. One of the central novels of Balzac's Human Comedy.

title: Carmen
author: Prosper Mérimée
synopsis: A soldier is destroyed by his obsessive love for the fierce, free Gypsy woman Carmen. Mérimée's novella that inspired the opera.

title: The Flowers of Evil
author: Charles Baudelaire
synopsis: Baudelaire's scandalous, revolutionary poems of beauty, vice, boredom, and the modern city. The founding book of modern French poetry.

title: Hunger
author: Knut Hamsun
synopsis: A starving young writer wanders Christiania half-mad with pride and deprivation. Hamsun's intense, pioneering psychological novel.

title: Growth of the Soil
author: Knut Hamsun
synopsis: A settler carves a farm and a life out of the Norwegian wilderness across the generations. Hamsun's epic of the soil, which won the Nobel Prize.

title: Quo Vadis
author: Henryk Sienkiewicz
synopsis: Under the tyranny of Nero, Christians are persecuted as a Roman patrician falls in love with a devout hostage. Sienkiewicz's epic of the early Church.

title: The Cloister and the Hearth
author: Charles Reade
synopsis: A young couple are separated across fifteenth-century Europe on the eve of the parents of Erasmus. Reade's richly researched historical novel.

title: The Valley of Fear
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: Holmes unravels a coded warning and a country-house murder that reaches back to a secret society in America. The last Sherlock Holmes novel.

title: Micah Clarke
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: A young Puritan marches with Monmouth's doomed rebellion against James II. Conan Doyle's vigorous historical adventure.

title: The Second Jungle Book
author: Rudyard Kipling
synopsis: More tales of Mowgli among the wolves, plus stories of the Indian wild. Kipling's second collection of Jungle Book fables.

title: Just So Stories
author: Rudyard Kipling
synopsis: Playful origin myths explaining how the leopard got its spots and the camel its hump, told to a child. Kipling's inventive fables for the young.

title: Puck of Pook's Hill
author: Rudyard Kipling
synopsis: Two children are shown England's deep history by the ancient spirit Puck, who conjures Romans, Normans, and smugglers. Kipling's historical fantasy.

title: Captains Courageous
author: Rudyard Kipling
synopsis: A spoiled millionaire's son is knocked overboard, rescued by cod fishermen, and made a man by hard labor at sea. Kipling's rousing tale of the Grand Banks.

title: Plain Tales from the Hills
author: Rudyard Kipling
synopsis: Sharp, ironic stories of British India, soldiers, and the intrigues of Anglo-Indian society. Kipling's early collection that made his name.

title: The Man Who Would Be King
author: Rudyard Kipling
synopsis: Two British adventurers set out to become kings of a remote Afghan kingdom, with tragic results. Kipling's dark, brilliant novella of imperial hubris.

title: From the Earth to the Moon
author: Jules Verne
synopsis: A Baltimore gun club builds a giant cannon to fire a manned projectile at the moon. Verne's prophetic, tongue-in-cheek space adventure.

title: Michael Strogoff
author: Jules Verne
synopsis: A courier races across rebellion-torn Siberia to deliver the Tsar's warning, enduring capture and near blindness. Verne's thrilling adventure.

title: Five Weeks in a Balloon
author: Jules Verne
synopsis: Explorers cross unmapped Africa by hydrogen balloon, surviving deserts, beasts, and storms. Verne's first great adventure novel.

title: The Coral Island
author: R. M. Ballantyne
synopsis: Three boys shipwrecked on a Pacific island build an idyllic life until pirates and cannibals intrude. Ballantyne's classic boys' adventure.

title: Two Years Before the Mast
author: Richard Henry Dana
synopsis: A Harvard student's vivid memoir of two years as a common sailor rounding Cape Horn. Dana's influential account of life before the mast.

title: The Castle of Otranto
author: Horace Walpole
synopsis: A tyrant's castle is haunted by a giant helmet, a bleeding statue, and an ancient curse. Walpole's founding Gothic novel.

title: Melmoth the Wanderer
author: Charles Maturin
synopsis: A damned wanderer roams the earth seeking someone to take on his bargain with the devil, in nested tales of suffering. Maturin's sprawling Gothic masterpiece.

title: Carmilla
author: Sheridan Le Fanu
synopsis: A lonely girl is befriended, and slowly preyed upon, by a beautiful female vampire. Le Fanu's influential tale that predates Dracula.

title: The King in Yellow
author: Robert W. Chambers
synopsis: Linked stories circling a forbidden play that drives its readers mad. Chambers's eerie collection that shaped weird fiction.

title: The Great God Pan
author: Arthur Machen
synopsis: A cruel experiment lets a woman glimpse the god Pan, unleashing a trail of madness and death. Machen's landmark of cosmic horror.

title: The Narrative of Arthur Gordon Pym
author: Edgar Allan Poe
synopsis: A stowaway endures mutiny, shipwreck, cannibalism, and stranger horrors on a voyage to the Antarctic. Poe's only novel, a nightmarish sea adventure.

title: The Mystery of a Hansom Cab
author: Fergus Hume
synopsis: A body is found in a Melbourne cab, launching a twisting investigation through the city's classes. A record-breaking early detective bestseller.

title: The Leavenworth Case
author: Anna Katharine Green
synopsis: A wealthy man is shot in his library and detective Ebenezer Gryce untangles the family's secrets. Green's pioneering American detective novel.

title: The Circular Staircase
author: Mary Roberts Rinehart
synopsis: A spinster renting a summer house is caught in a web of bank fraud and murder. Rinehart's hugely popular mystery that shaped the genre.

title: The Mysterious Affair at Styles
author: Agatha Christie
synopsis: A poisoning at an English country house is solved by a fastidious Belgian refugee named Hercule Poirot. Christie's debut, introducing her great detective.

title: The Autocrat of the Breakfast-Table
author: Oliver Wendell Holmes
synopsis: Witty, digressive table conversation on science, faith, and human nature at a Boston boarding house. Holmes's genial classic of the familiar essay.

title: The Compleat Angler
author: Izaak Walton
synopsis: A leisurely dialogue on the art and philosophy of angling, laced with songs and country wisdom. Walton's enduring celebration of the contemplative life.

title: Areopagitica
author: John Milton
synopsis: Milton's impassioned speech against government licensing of books and for the freedom of the press. A foundational defense of free expression.

title: Paradise Regained
author: John Milton
synopsis: Christ resists Satan's temptations in the wilderness, the brief epic sequel to Paradise Lost. Milton's austere poem of spiritual victory.

title: History of the Peloponnesian War
author: Thucydides
synopsis: The Athenian general's rigorous account of the long war between Athens and Sparta. Thucydides's founding work of scientific history.

title: The Nicomachean Ethics
author: Aristotle
synopsis: Aristotle's inquiry into happiness, virtue, and the good life as the fulfillment of human nature. The cornerstone of Western ethics.

title: Politics
author: Aristotle
synopsis: Aristotle's systematic study of constitutions and the state as natural to man. A founding text of political science.

title: On the Nature of Things
author: Lucretius
synopsis: A didactic poem expounding Epicurean atomism to free humanity from fear of gods and death. Lucretius's astonishing scientific epic.

title: The Consolation of Philosophy
author: Boethius
synopsis: Awaiting execution, Boethius is consoled by Lady Philosophy on fortune, happiness, and providence. The most influential philosophical work of the Middle Ages.

title: The Enchiridion
author: Epictetus
synopsis: A pocket handbook of Stoic wisdom on distinguishing what is and isn't in our power. Epictetus's practical guide to serenity.

title: The Confessions
author: Augustine of Hippo
synopsis: Augustine's intimate account of his sins, restless searching, and conversion, addressed to God. The first great Western autobiography.

title: The Praise of Folly
author: Erasmus
synopsis: The goddess Folly delivers a mock-oration praising human foolishness and skewering the powerful and the pious. Erasmus's witty humanist satire.

title: The Book of the Courtier
author: Baldassare Castiglione
synopsis: Elegant dialogues at the court of Urbino defining the ideal Renaissance gentleman and lady. Castiglione's hugely influential book of manners.

title: Novum Organum
author: Francis Bacon
synopsis: Bacon's manifesto for a new science based on observation and inductive method, sweeping away old dogma. A founding text of the scientific revolution.

title: Ethics
author: Baruch Spinoza
synopsis: A rigorous geometry of God, nature, mind, and freedom, arguing that God and Nature are one. Spinoza's austere rationalist masterpiece.

title: An Enquiry Concerning Human Understanding
author: David Hume
synopsis: Hume's skeptical inquiry into knowledge, causation, and miracles, arguing that reason rests on habit. A cornerstone of empiricism.

title: The Wealth of Nations
author: Adam Smith
synopsis: Smith's foundational analysis of markets, labor, and the 'invisible hand' of self-interest. The book that launched modern economics.

title: A Vindication of the Rights of Woman
author: Mary Wollstonecraft
synopsis: Wollstonecraft's bold argument that women are rational beings entitled to education and equality. A founding text of feminism.

title: Emile
author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau
synopsis: Rousseau's treatise on raising a child according to nature rather than corrupt society. His influential philosophy of education, told through a fictional pupil.

title: Perpetual Peace
author: Immanuel Kant
synopsis: Kant's blueprint for lasting peace through republics, federation, and cosmopolitan right. A founding text of liberal internationalism.

title: Utilitarianism
author: John Stuart Mill
synopsis: Mill's defense of the greatest-happiness principle as the basis of morality, refined against its critics. The classic statement of utilitarian ethics.

title: The Subjection of Women
author: John Stuart Mill
synopsis: Mill's argument that the legal subordination of women is wrong and a chief obstacle to human progress. A landmark of liberal feminism.

title: Representative Men
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
synopsis: Emerson's essays on six great men, from Plato to Napoleon, as embodiments of human possibility. A study of genius and its uses.

title: Democracy in America
author: Alexis de Tocqueville
synopsis: A French aristocrat's penetrating analysis of American equality, liberty, and its dangers. The classic outsider's study of democracy.

title: The Rights of Man
author: Thomas Paine
synopsis: Paine's ringing defense of the French Revolution and of universal human rights against Burke. A foundational radical democratic text.

title: The Age of Reason
author: Thomas Paine
synopsis: Paine's deist attack on organized religion and revealed scripture in favor of reason. A controversial Enlightenment tract.

title: The French Revolution: A History
author: Thomas Carlyle
synopsis: Carlyle's turbulent, vivid narrative history of the Revolution as a force of nature. A prose epic of the Terror.

title: The Oregon Trail
author: Francis Parkman
synopsis: The young historian's firsthand account of travel among the Plains tribes and emigrants on the frontier. Parkman's classic of the American West.

title: The Education of Henry Adams
author: Henry Adams
synopsis: Adams's ironic third-person memoir of an old-world education failing to fit the age of dynamos and science. A landmark of American autobiography.

title: Eminent Victorians
author: Lytton Strachey
synopsis: Four irreverent, debunking short biographies of Victorian icons like Florence Nightingale. Strachey's book that reinvented the art of biography.

title: The Twelve Caesars
author: Suetonius
synopsis: Gossipy, vivid lives of Julius Caesar and the first eleven emperors, virtues and vices alike. Suetonius's enduringly readable Roman history.

title: The Voyage of the Beagle
author: Charles Darwin
synopsis: Darwin's journal of the five-year survey voyage that took him to the Galápagos and shaped his theory. A classic of scientific travel.

title: The Varieties of Religious Experience
author: William James
synopsis: James's landmark study of conversion, mysticism, and faith as human experience rather than doctrine. A founding work of the psychology of religion.

title: Pragmatism
author: William James
synopsis: James's argument that the truth of an idea lies in its practical consequences. The classic statement of American pragmatism.

title: The Story of My Life
author: Helen Keller
synopsis: Keller's account of emerging from a silent, dark world into language and learning through her teacher Anne Sullivan. An inspiring autobiography.

title: Up from Slavery
author: Booker T. Washington
synopsis: Washington's memoir of rising from slavery to found Tuskegee and lead through self-improvement. A defining, and debated, African American autobiography.

title: Twelve Years a Slave
author: Solomon Northup
synopsis: A free Black New Yorker's account of being kidnapped and sold into twelve years of Louisiana slavery. One of the most powerful slave narratives.

title: Incidents in the Life of a Slave Girl
author: Harriet Jacobs
synopsis: A slave woman's account of sexual abuse, years hidden in a crawlspace, and escape to freedom. A rare female-authored slave narrative.

title: The Yellow Wallpaper
author: Charlotte Perkins Gilman
synopsis: A woman confined for a nervous condition descends into madness fixated on her room's wallpaper. Gilman's chilling feminist story of enforced idleness.

title: Herland
author: Charlotte Perkins Gilman
synopsis: Three male explorers discover an isolated society of women who reproduce without men and live in peace. Gilman's feminist utopian satire.

title: The Story of an African Farm
author: Olive Schreiner
synopsis: On a lonely Boer farm, orphaned children wrestle with faith, freedom, and a woman's ambitions. Schreiner's pioneering colonial and feminist novel.

title: Castle Rackrent
author: Maria Edgeworth
synopsis: An Irish servant narrates the decline of a family of reckless landlords across generations. Edgeworth's witty pioneering regional novel.

title: Evelina
author: Fanny Burney
synopsis: A sheltered young woman's comic and perilous entrance into London society, told in letters. Burney's sparkling novel of manners.

title: Clarissa
author: Samuel Richardson
synopsis: A virtuous young woman is abducted and destroyed by the rake Lovelace, told in a vast web of letters. Richardson's monumental tragic novel.

title: Joseph Andrews
author: Henry Fielding
synopsis: A handsome, virtuous footman fends off his mistress and rambles home with Parson Adams. Fielding's comic novel that began as a parody of Pamela.

title: The Expedition of Humphry Clinker
author: Tobias Smollett
synopsis: A cantankerous squire tours Britain with his household, whose letters give comic, clashing views. Smollett's genial epistolary travel novel.

title: The Adventures of Roderick Random
author: Tobias Smollett
synopsis: A hot-tempered Scot knocks about the navy, war, and love in a rough picaresque. Smollett's vigorous early novel.

title: A Sentimental Journey
author: Laurence Sterne
synopsis: A whimsical, digressive account of a clergyman's tour through France and Italy, alive to every small feeling. Sterne's influential sentimental travelogue.

title: A Journal of the Plague Year
author: Daniel Defoe
synopsis: A survivor's minutely detailed account of London during the plague of 1665. Defoe's vivid fictional chronicle disguised as memoir.

title: Travels with a Donkey in the Cévennes
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
synopsis: Stevenson's charming account of a solitary walking tour through the Cévennes with a stubborn donkey. A classic of travel writing.

title: The Master of Ballantrae
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
synopsis: A dark tale of two brothers locked in lifelong hatred across Scotland, America, and India. Stevenson's brooding novel of a family's ruin.

title: The Black Arrow
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
