synopsis: A young man seeks vengeance and love amid the Wars of the Roses, aided by a mysterious archers' band. Stevenson's medieval adventure.

title: Catriona
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
synopsis: The sequel to Kidnapped, as David Balfour pursues justice and romance in Jacobite intrigue. Stevenson's Scottish historical adventure.

title: A Christmas Carol
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: The miser Scrooge is redeemed in a single night by three spirits of Christmas. Dickens's immortal ghost story of second chances.

title: Personal Memoirs of U. S. Grant
author: Ulysses S. Grant
synopsis: The dying general's plain, masterful account of his Civil War campaigns, written to provide for his family. One of the finest military memoirs.

title: Nicholas Nickleby
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: A young man supports his mother and sister against a villainous uncle amid schools, actors, and swindlers. Dickens's sprawling early novel.

title: Barnaby Rudge
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: A simple-hearted youth and his raven are swept up in the anti-Catholic Gordon Riots. Dickens's historical novel of mob violence.

title: Hard Times
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: In a grim industrial town, a schoolmaster's fact-worship and a mill owner's greed crush the human spirit. Dickens's fierce short novel against utilitarianism.

title: The Small House at Allington
author: Anthony Trollope
synopsis: A gentle woman's long, thwarted love for a man who wavers between her and a worldlier match. The fifth Barsetshire novel by Trollope.

title: Can You Forgive Her?
author: Anthony Trollope
synopsis: A headstrong heiress must choose among suitors while her cousins' fortunes entwine. The first of Trollope's Palliser political novels.

title: The Eustace Diamonds
author: Anthony Trollope
synopsis: A beautiful widow schemes to keep a diamond necklace she may not own, testing law and society. One of Trollope's sharpest Palliser novels.

title: Orley Farm
author: Anthony Trollope
synopsis: A disputed will and a hidden crime drive a sprawling tale of English country life and law. One of Trollope's most admired standalone novels.

title: Old Mortality
author: Walter Scott
synopsis: Amid Scotland's Covenanter uprising, a moderate hero is caught between fanatics and tyrants. One of Scott's finest historical novels.

title: Kenilworth
author: Walter Scott
synopsis: Court intrigue and a secret marriage lead to tragedy in the reign of Elizabeth I. Scott's dramatic Elizabethan romance.

title: The Talisman
author: Walter Scott
synopsis: A Scottish crusader and Saladin cross paths in the Holy Land in a tale of chivalry and disguise. Scott's Crusades adventure.

title: Quentin Durward
author: Walter Scott
synopsis: A Scots archer in the guard of Louis XI navigates the treacherous French court. Scott's colorful medieval adventure.

title: Guy Mannering
author: Walter Scott
synopsis: A lost heir, a Gypsy's prophecy, and a smuggling plot unwind in the Scottish borders. Scott's second novel.

title: The Antiquary
author: Walter Scott
synopsis: A genial antiquary presides over a comic tangle of disguise, romance, and a lost inheritance. One of Scott's own favorites among his novels.

title: The Law and the Lady
author: Wilkie Collins
synopsis: A new bride investigates the mysterious first wife's death that shadows her marriage. Collins's mystery with a rare female detective.

title: Man and Wife
author: Wilkie Collins
synopsis: A rash promise and Scotland's marriage laws trap a woman with a brutal husband. Collins's sensation novel with a social-reform edge.

title: The Haunted Hotel
author: Wilkie Collins
synopsis: Newlyweds take a Venetian palace hotel where a nobleman's disappearance hides a murder. Collins's atmospheric ghostly mystery.

title: Scenes of Clerical Life
author: George Eliot
synopsis: Three tender, ironic tales of English clergymen and their parishioners. Eliot's first fiction, where her genius first appeared.

title: Felix Holt, the Radical
author: George Eliot
synopsis: An idealistic radical returns to a corrupt election town and loves above his station. Eliot's novel of politics and conscience.

title: The Lifted Veil
author: George Eliot
synopsis: A man cursed with the power to foresee the future and read minds is poisoned by the knowledge. Eliot's eerie supernatural novella.

title: The Princess Casamassima
author: Henry James
synopsis: A poor, high-strung young man is drawn into revolutionary conspiracy and torn between violence and beauty. James's novel of London anarchism.

title: The Tragic Muse
author: Henry James
synopsis: A brilliant actress and the men who love art, politics, and her collide over what to live for. James's novel of the theater and the artistic life.

title: The Spoils of Poynton
author: Henry James
synopsis: A widow and her son battle over the priceless furnishings of a country house, wrecking a marriage. James's taut drama of possession and taste.

title: What Maisie Knew
author: Henry James
synopsis: A small girl is shuttled between divorced and remarrying parents, absorbing the adults' betrayals. James's subtle novel seen through a child's eyes.

title: The Aspern Papers
author: Henry James
synopsis: An editor schemes to obtain a dead poet's love letters from his aged former mistress in Venice. James's celebrated novella of obsession and betrayal.

title: The Reef
author: Edith Wharton
synopsis: An engagement is poisoned by the fiancé's concealed affair with the young woman's governess. Wharton's spare novel of scruple and desire.

title: The Fruit of the Tree
author: Edith Wharton
synopsis: A mill manager's reforming ideals collide with love, money, and a fatal act of mercy. Wharton's novel of industry and conscience.

title: The Valley of Decision
author: Edith Wharton
synopsis: A young man's education and love amid the intrigues of eighteenth-century Italy on the eve of revolution. Wharton's ambitious historical novel.

title: The People of the Abyss
author: Jack London
synopsis: London went undercover to live among the destitute of the East End and expose their misery. Journalism of social outrage.

title: Before Adam
author: Jack London
synopsis: A modern man dreams the life of his primitive ancestor in a vivid tale of prehistoric survival. London's evolutionary fantasy.

title: John Barleycorn
author: Jack London
synopsis: London's candid memoir of a life shadowed by drink, both confession and temperance argument. His 'alcoholic memoirs.'

title: The Star Rover
author: Jack London
synopsis: A condemned prisoner escapes torture by projecting his mind into past lives. London's mystical prison novel.

title: Burning Daylight
author: Jack London
synopsis: A tough Klondike miner strikes it rich, conquers finance, and is redeemed by love of the land. London's novel of money and simplicity.

title: The Valley of the Moon
author: Jack London
synopsis: A working-class Oakland couple flee the corrupt city to seek a farming life in the California valleys. London's agrarian novel.

title: The Scarlet Plague
author: Jack London
synopsis: An old man tells his grandsons how a plague destroyed civilization sixty years before. London's post-apocalyptic novella.

title: The Cruise of the Snark
author: Jack London
synopsis: London's account of building a boat and sailing the South Pacific with his wife. A vivid, mishap-filled travelogue.

title: The Hand of Ethelberta
author: Thomas Hardy
synopsis: A stage performer passes as a lady and juggles suitors to secure her family's future. Hardy's atypical social comedy.

title: Two on a Tower
author: Thomas Hardy
synopsis: A young astronomer and an older noblewoman conduct a secret love beneath the stars. Hardy's romance set against the vast cosmos.

title: Desperate Remedies
author: Thomas Hardy
synopsis: A architect's daughter is caught in a web of mistaken identity and a sinister steward's secret. Hardy's first published novel, a sensation mystery.

title: Wessex Tales
author: Thomas Hardy
synopsis: Ironic, fateful tales of rural Wessex life, from a returning soldier to a hanging. Hardy's first collection of short stories.

title: Sylvia's Lovers
author: Elizabeth Gaskell
synopsis: In a Yorkshire whaling town, a passionate woman is torn between a steady cousin and a dashing sailor pressed into the navy. Gaskell's tragic historical novel.

title: The Pleasures of Hope
author: Thomas Campbell
synopsis: A didactic poem celebrating hope as the consolation that gilds human life and sustains the mind through suffering.

title: A Midsummer Night's Dream
author: William Shakespeare
synopsis: Four lovers, a troupe of bumbling actors, and quarreling fairies tangle in a moonlit wood outside Athens.

title: Ulysses
author: James Joyce
synopsis: A day in the life of Leopold Bloom wandering Dublin on 16 June 1904, retelling Homer's Odyssey through radically shifting modernist styles.

title: The Adventures of Pinocchio
author: Carlo Collodi
synopsis: A mischievous wooden puppet longs to become a real boy, learning hard moral lessons through a string of misadventures.

title: Journey to the Center of the Earth
author: Jules Verne
synopsis: A professor, his nephew, and their Icelandic guide descend an extinct volcano into a vast subterranean world of prehistoric seas and creatures.

title: A Little Princess
author: Frances Hodgson Burnett
synopsis: Sara Crewe, a wealthy captain's daughter at a London boarding school, is reduced to servitude when her father dies, but sustains herself through imagination and quiet dignity.

title: This Side of Paradise
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
synopsis: Amory Blaine, a handsome and self-absorbed Princeton student, drifts through love affairs and disillusionment into the uncertain dawn of the Jazz Age.

title: Anne of the Island
author: L. M. Montgomery
synopsis: Anne Shirley leaves Avonlea for Redmond College, navigating friendship, heartbreak, and self-discovery over four years away at university.

title: The Secret Adversary
author: Agatha Christie
synopsis: Young adventurers Tommy and Tuppence hunt for a missing woman and a secret treaty, tangling with a shadowy master criminal in post-war London.

title: Riders of the Purple Sage
author: Zane Grey
synopsis: In 1871 Utah, a mysterious gunman and a persecuted Mormon rancher woman are drawn together amid feuds, faith, and the wild canyon country.

title: The Virginian
author: Owen Wister
synopsis: A soft-spoken Wyoming cowboy courts an Eastern schoolteacher and faces down a rustler, in the novel that defined the Western hero.

title: My Man Jeeves
author: P. G. Wodehouse
synopsis: Eight comic stories in which the imperturbable valet Jeeves extricates Bertie Wooster and his hapless friends from social scrapes.

title: Anthem
author: Ayn Rand
synopsis: In a collectivist future that has erased the word 'I', a man rediscovers individuality, invention, and forbidden love, and reclaims the self.

title: Flatland
author: Edwin A. Abbott
synopsis: A square living in a two-dimensional world is shown higher dimensions, in a satire of Victorian social hierarchy and a witty primer on geometry.

title: The Enchanted April
author: Elizabeth von Arnim
synopsis: Four discontented Englishwomen rent an Italian castle for the month of April, where beauty and sunshine quietly transform their lives and marriages.

title: The Gods of Mars
author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
synopsis: John Carter returns to Barsoom, battling the deadly false religion of the Therns and racing to rescue his princess Dejah Thoris.

title: Ozma of Oz
author: L. Frank Baum
synopsis: Dorothy is swept to the fairy land of Ev and joins Ozma of Oz and her friends to free a royal family imprisoned by the Nome King.

title: The Magnificent Ambersons
author: Booth Tarkington
synopsis: The spoiled heir of a wealthy Midwestern family clings to old privilege as industrial modernity overtakes his town and erodes the family fortune.

title: The Good Soldier
author: Ford Madox Ford
synopsis: An unreliable narrator slowly unravels the adulteries and quiet deceptions behind two seemingly proper Edwardian couples.

title: The Four Million
author: O. Henry
synopsis: Short stories of ordinary New Yorkers — including 'The Gift of the Magi' — turning on irony, coincidence, and sharp twist endings.

title: The Happy Prince and Other Tales
author: Oscar Wilde
synopsis: Five literary fairy tales — including 'The Happy Prince' and 'The Selfish Giant' — blending beauty, sacrifice, and gentle moral fable.

title: The Blue Fairy Book
author: Andrew Lang
synopsis: The landmark Victorian anthology of classic fairy tales — Cinderella, Sleeping Beauty, Aladdin, Beauty and the Beast and more — retold for children.

title: The Sun Also Rises
author: Ernest Hemingway
synopsis: Disillusioned American and British expatriates drift from Paris to the bullfights of Pamplona in the aftermath of the Great War.

title: In Our Time
author: Ernest Hemingway
synopsis: Interlinked short stories and vignettes, many following Nick Adams, tracing violence, war, and coming of age in spare, understated prose.

title: Three Stories and Ten Poems
author: Ernest Hemingway
synopsis: Hemingway's first book: three early short stories and ten poems, an apprentice sketch of his terse mature style.

title: The Beautiful and Damned
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
synopsis: Anthony Patch and his wife Gloria squander their youth and beauty waiting on an inheritance, decaying amid Jazz Age dissipation.

title: Tales of the Jazz Age
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
synopsis: A story collection ranging from social satire to fantasy, including 'The Curious Case of Benjamin Button' and 'The Diamond as Big as the Ritz'.

title: Flappers and Philosophers
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
synopsis: Fitzgerald's first story collection: portraits of ambitious young men and modern flappers, including 'Bernice Bobs Her Hair'.

title: All the Sad Young Men
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
synopsis: Nine stories of love, class, and disillusionment, including 'The Rich Boy' and 'Winter Dreams'.

title: Lady Susan
author: Jane Austen
synopsis: In a witty epistolary novella, the beautiful, scheming widow Lady Susan Vernon manipulates suitors and her own daughter in pursuit of advantageous matches.

title: Love and Freindship
author: Jane Austen
synopsis: A teenage Austen's comic epistolary burlesque of sentimental fiction, chronicling absurd misadventures and fainting heroines.

title: Agnes Grey
author: Anne Brontë
synopsis: A clergyman's daughter endures the petty cruelties of employment as a governess, in a quietly realistic study of dignity and moral endurance.

title: Poems by Currer, Ellis, and Acton Bell
author: Charlotte, Emily, and Anne Brontë
synopsis: The Brontë sisters' first published book, a joint poetry collection issued under the pseudonyms Currer, Ellis, and Acton Bell.

title: His Last Bow
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: A collection of Sherlock Holmes cases, closing with the title story in which an aged Holmes outwits a German spy on the eve of the Great War.

title: The Poison Belt
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: Professor Challenger gathers his friends as Earth passes through a belt of poisonous ether that seems to end all human life.

title: The Exploits of Brigadier Gerard
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: The boastful, gallant Napoleonic cavalry officer Étienne Gerard recounts his daring and comic adventures across the wars of the Empire.

title: Rodney Stone
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: A Regency-era tale of prize-fighting, high society, and a country mystery, narrated by a young man drawn into the world of the Fancy.

title: The Refugees
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: A historical romance of Huguenot exiles fleeing the court of Louis XIV for the wilderness of New France.

title: Round the Red Lamp
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: Short 'facts and fancies of medical life' by the physician-author, ranging from grim realism to the uncanny.

title: Uncle Bernac
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: A young French émigré returns under Napoleon's shadow, caught among intrigue, loyalty, and the Emperor's camp at Boulogne.

title: The Firm of Girdlestone
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: An early Doyle novel of commercial villainy, as unscrupulous merchants scheme against a young heiress.

title: The Mystery of Cloomber
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
synopsis: A retired general is haunted by a doom pursued from India, in an early Doyle tale of vengeance and the occult.

title: The Moon and Sixpence
author: W. Somerset Maugham
synopsis: A conventional London stockbroker abandons his family to pursue painting in Paris and Tahiti, in a novel loosely inspired by Paul Gauguin.

title: Victory
author: Joseph Conrad
synopsis: A solitary Swede living on a remote island rescues a young woman, only to be menaced by three drifting villains, in a tragedy of detachment and love.

title: The Secret Agent
author: Joseph Conrad
synopsis: A London shopkeeper and spy is drawn into an anarchist plot to bomb the Greenwich Observatory, with devastating domestic consequences.

title: Nostromo
author: Joseph Conrad
synopsis: Revolution, silver, and corruption engulf a South American port, centered on the trusted, incorruptible dockworker Nostromo.

title: Jacob's Room
author: Virginia Woolf
synopsis: An elliptical, impressionistic portrait of young Jacob Flanders, assembled from fragments as he moves toward the Great War.

title: The Voyage Out
author: Virginia Woolf
synopsis: Woolf's first novel: a sheltered young woman sails to South America and awakens to love, ideas, and mortality.

title: Women in Love
author: D. H. Lawrence
synopsis: Two sisters pursue intense, contrasting relationships with two men, in Lawrence's searching study of love, will, and modernity.

title: The Rainbow
author: D. H. Lawrence
synopsis: Three generations of the Brangwen family seek fulfillment in love and marriage across a changing rural England.

title: Where Angels Fear to Tread
author: E. M. Forster
synopsis: The impulsive marriage of an English widow to a young Italian sparks a clash of cultures and a tragic struggle over a child.

title: One of Ours
author: Willa Cather
synopsis: A restless Nebraska farm boy finds purpose only when he goes to fight in the Great War, in Cather's Pulitzer-winning novel.

title: The Enormous Room
author: E. E. Cummings
synopsis: Cummings's experimental memoir of his wrongful WWI imprisonment by the French, told with exuberant, defiant wit.

title: Zuleika Dobson
author: Max Beerbohm
synopsis: A satirical fantasy in which a bewitching young woman visits Oxford and every besotted undergraduate resolves to die for her.

title: Alice Adams
author: Booth Tarkington
synopsis: A lower-middle-class girl strains to rise in society and win a suitor, in Tarkington's Pulitzer-winning study of class and pretension.

title: The Wisdom of Father Brown
author: G. K. Chesterton
synopsis: A second collection of mysteries solved by the unassuming priest-detective Father Brown through insight into the human soul.

title: Whose Body?
author: Dorothy L. Sayers
synopsis: The debut of aristocratic sleuth Lord Peter Wimsey, who investigates a stray corpse found in a bathtub wearing only a pince-nez.

title: Jurgen
author: James Branch Cabell
synopsis: A middle-aged pawnbroker-poet journeys through myth and the afterlife in a witty, once-banned fantasy of desire and disillusion.

title: A Voyage to Arcturus
author: David Lindsay
synopsis: A metaphysical fantasy in which a traveler crosses a strange planet, each landscape testing a different philosophy of existence.

title: The Sheik
author: E. M. Hull
synopsis: A hugely popular desert romance in which an independent Englishwoman is abducted by an Arab sheik — a landmark of the romance genre.

title: Daddy-Long-Legs
author: Jean Webster
synopsis: An orphan sent to college writes witty letters to the anonymous benefactor she has never met, in a beloved epistolary novel.

title: A Girl of the Limberlost
author: Gene Stratton-Porter
synopsis: A determined girl funds her education by collecting moths from an Indiana wetland, overcoming her embittered mother's neglect.

title: Marguerite de Valois
author: Alexandre Dumas
synopsis: In the bloody days of the St. Bartholomew's massacre, Marguerite de Valois navigates love and court intrigue at the French royal court.

title: The Vicomte de Bragelonne
author: Alexandre Dumas
synopsis: The aging Musketeers reunite amid the intrigues of Louis XIV's court in the opening of Dumas's epic conclusion to the d'Artagnan saga.

title: Chicot the Jester
author: Alexandre Dumas
synopsis: The witty court jester Chicot protects Henri III amid the plots of the Valois court and the ambitions of the Duke of Guise.

title: The Forty-Five Guardsmen
author: Alexandre Dumas
synopsis: Chicot and a band of forty-five Gascon guardsmen defend Henri III against conspiracies in the twilight of the Valois dynasty.

title: The Queen's Necklace
author: Alexandre Dumas
synopsis: The scandalous affair of a diamond necklace ensnares Marie Antoinette's court on the eve of the French Revolution.

title: The Companions of Jehu
author: Alexandre Dumas
synopsis: Royalist highwaymen wage a secret war against the Revolutionary Republic during the rise of Napoleon Bonaparte.

title: The Murder on the Links
author: Agatha Christie
synopsis: Hercule Poirot investigates a millionaire found stabbed on a French golf course, matching wits with a rival detective.

title: Poirot Investigates
author: Agatha Christie
synopsis: Eleven short cases in which Hercule Poirot applies his 'little grey cells' to jewel thefts, disappearances, and murder.

title: The Man in the Brown Suit
author: Agatha Christie
synopsis: A spirited young woman follows a trail of murder and diamonds from London to South Africa.

title: The Secret of Chimneys
author: Agatha Christie
synopsis: Blackmail, a lost royal, and murder converge at an English country house in a tale of international intrigue.

title: His Family
author: Ernest Poole
synopsis: A widowed New Yorker watches his three daughters embody divergent paths of a changing modern America.

title: The Able McLaughlins
author: Margaret Wilson
synopsis: A Scots-immigrant farming community on the Iowa frontier is tested by love, guilt, and frontier justice.

title: So Big
author: Edna Ferber
synopsis: A refined young woman becomes a widowed truck farmer near Chicago and pins her hopes on her son, in Ferber's Pulitzer-winning novel.

title: Jean-Christophe
author: Romain Rolland
synopsis: The sweeping life of a German-born musical genius, tracing his artistic struggles across a divided Europe.

title: Penguin Island
author: Anatole France
synopsis: A satirical mock-history of a nation of penguins accidentally baptized into humanity, skewering French society and history.

title: Thaïs
author: Anatole France
synopsis: A zealous monk sets out to convert a beautiful Alexandrian courtesan, only to be consumed by his own desire.

title: The Man of Property
author: John Galsworthy
synopsis: The propertied Forsyte family's values are shaken by Soames Forsyte's disastrous marriage, opening the Forsyte Saga.

title: With Fire and Sword
author: Henryk Sienkiewicz
synopsis: An epic of love and war set amid the 17th-century Cossack uprising against the Polish Commonwealth.

title: The Story of Gösta Berling
author: Selma Lagerlöf
synopsis: A defrocked priest and a band of cavaliers pursue romance and redemption across the manor houses of rural Sweden.

title: Pelle the Conqueror
author: Martin Andersen Nexø
synopsis: A poor Swedish boy and his father struggle as laborers on Bornholm, in a landmark novel of the working class.

title: Scaramouche
author: Rafael Sabatini
synopsis: A young lawyer takes up the mask of a commedia harlequin and the sword of a revolutionary in the France of 1789.

title: Captain Blood
author: Rafael Sabatini
synopsis: An Irish physician, wrongly sold into Caribbean slavery, escapes to become the most feared and gallant of pirates.

title: The Sea Hawk
author: Rafael Sabatini
synopsis: An Elizabethan gentleman betrayed and enslaved becomes a Barbary corsair bent on vengeance.

title: The Deluge
author: Henryk Sienkiewicz
synopsis: Poland fights for survival against the Swedish invasion of 1655, in the second novel of Sienkiewicz's Trilogy.

title: Pan Michael
author: Henryk Sienkiewicz
synopsis: The little knight Pan Michael Wolodyjowski defends the Commonwealth's southeastern frontier against the Turks, closing Sienkiewicz's Trilogy.

title: The Knights of the Cross
author: Henryk Sienkiewicz
synopsis: A sweeping medieval romance of Poland's struggle against the Teutonic Knights, culminating at the Battle of Grunwald.

title: Jerusalem
author: Selma Lagerlöf
synopsis: Swedish farmers from Dalarna, seized by religious fervor, sell their ancestral land to emigrate to a colony in Jerusalem.

title: The Miracles of Antichrist
author: Selma Lagerlöf
synopsis: In a Sicilian town, a false image of Christ works seeming miracles, blurring faith, socialism, and superstition.

title: The Girl from the Marsh Croft
author: Selma Lagerlöf
synopsis: A wronged servant girl's honesty in court sets in motion a quiet story of redemption in rural Sweden.

title: The Gods Are Athirst
author: Anatole France
synopsis: A fervent young painter becomes a Revolutionary juror during the Terror, and idealism curdles into fanaticism.

title: The Red Lily
author: Anatole France
synopsis: A Parisian society woman pursues a passionate, jealous love affair against the backdrop of Florence.

title: The Aspirations of Jean Servien
author: Anatole France
synopsis: A bookish young man of humble birth is consumed by a hopeless romantic and social longing in 19th-century Paris.

title: The Country House
author: John Galsworthy
synopsis: A satirical study of the English landed gentry, as the Pendyce family's rigid propriety is tested by scandal.

title: Fraternity
author: John Galsworthy
synopsis: London intellectuals wrestle with conscience and class as they try, and fail, to bridge the gulf between rich and poor.

title: The Island Pharisees
author: John Galsworthy
synopsis: A restless young Englishman travels among his own complacent class, questioning its hypocrisies.

title: Shallow Soil
author: Knut Hamsun
synopsis: In Kristiania's bohemian and mercantile circles, artists and businessmen vie for love and worldly success.

title: Wanderers
author: Knut Hamsun
synopsis: The vagabond Knut Pedersen drifts through the Norwegian countryside as a laborer, observing love and longing.

title: The Job
author: Sinclair Lewis
synopsis: A determined young woman builds a business career in New York, in an early realist novel of women and work.

title: Free Air
author: Sinclair Lewis
synopsis: A cross-country motoring adventure sparks romance across class lines between a society girl and a small-town mechanic.

title: Arne
author: Bjørnstjerne Bjørnson
synopsis: A brooding, dreamy youth grows up amid the songs and sorrows of a Norwegian mountain valley.

title: A Happy Boy
author: Bjørnstjerne Bjørnson
synopsis: A peasant boy's steady love and self-improvement win him education and the girl he adores in rural Norway.

title: The Home and the World
author: Rabindranath Tagore
synopsis: A Bengali wife is torn between her gentle husband and a charismatic nationalist agitator during the Swadeshi movement.

title: The Comedienne
author: Władysław Reymont
synopsis: A provincial girl flees to Warsaw to join a theatrical troupe, chasing freedom and disillusion on the stage.

title: The Secret City
author: Hugh Walpole
synopsis: In revolutionary Petrograd, an Englishman is drawn into the passions and disintegration of a Russian family as the old order collapses.

title: The Lost Girl
author: D. H. Lawrence
synopsis: A draper's daughter in a dull Midlands town breaks free by running off with an Italian performer to a hard life in the mountains.

title: Lady into Fox
author: David Garnett
synopsis: A gentleman's wife inexplicably turns into a fox, and he struggles to hold onto their marriage as her wild nature takes over.

title: Riceyman Steps
author: Arnold Bennett
synopsis: A miserly secondhand bookseller in a shabby London district marries, as his obsessive thrift corrodes love and life.

title: Under Fire
author: Henri Barbusse
synopsis: The daily horror of trench warfare seen through a French infantry squad, in the great realist novel of the First World War.

title: In Chancery
author: John Galsworthy
synopsis: The Forsyte family's feuds and divorces play out as Soames seeks an heir and old passions resurface.

title: To Let
author: John Galsworthy
synopsis: A love affair between the children of estranged Forsyte cousins reopens the family's old wounds.

title: The Patrician
author: John Galsworthy
synopsis: A young aristocratic politician's career collides with a forbidden love, testing duty against desire in the English governing class.

title: The Elm-Tree on the Mall
author: Anatole France
synopsis: In a French provincial town, clergy and academics intrigue over a vacant bishopric, in the first of France's satirical 'Contemporary History'.

title: The Wicker-Work Woman
author: Anatole France
synopsis: The mild scholar Monsieur Bergeret endures domestic betrayal and small-town politics, continuing the 'Contemporary History'.

title: The Amethyst Ring
author: Anatole France
synopsis: Provincial ambition and clerical scheming swirl around the contest for a bishop's ring, in the third 'Contemporary History' novel.

title: Monsieur Bergeret in Paris
author: Anatole France
synopsis: Monsieur Bergeret, now in Paris, reflects wryly on justice and politics amid the Dreyfus-era ferment, closing the 'Contemporary History'.

title: Pierre and Luce
author: Romain Rolland
synopsis: Two young lovers snatch fragile happiness in wartime Paris under the shadow of the bombardments of 1918.

title: Clérambault
author: Romain Rolland
synopsis: A French poet breaks with the war fever of his nation to follow his conscience, and pays the price of standing alone.

title: Look Back on Happiness
author: Knut Hamsun
synopsis: A solitary wanderer retreats to the northern wilderness, musing on love, nature, and the passing of youth.

title: Our Mr. Wrenn
author: Sinclair Lewis
synopsis: A timid New York clerk comes into a little money and sets out to see the world and find romance.

title: The Trail of the Hawk
author: Sinclair Lewis
synopsis: A restless young Midwesterner becomes a pioneering aviator, chasing adventure and love across a changing America.

title: In God's Way
author: Bjørnstjerne Bjørnson
synopsis: A clergyman and a freethinking doctor clash over faith and morality in a Norwegian town, in Bjørnson's realist novel.

title: The Well of Saint Clare
author: Anatole France
synopsis: Ironic, jewel-like tales of monks, poets, and devils set in Renaissance Italy, told with France's skeptical charm.

title: The Dark Flower
author: John Galsworthy
synopsis: A man's three great loves across the seasons of his life, in Galsworthy's study of passion and fidelity.

title: The World Set Free
author: H. G. Wells
synopsis: Prophesying atomic warfare, Wells imagines a world devastated and then remade after the discovery of nuclear energy.

title: When the Sleeper Wakes
author: H. G. Wells
synopsis: A man sleeps for two centuries and wakes to find himself the legal owner of the world, amid a nightmarish future megacity.

title: The War in the Air
author: H. G. Wells
synopsis: A hapless Everyman is swept into a global war fought by fleets of airships that shatter civilization.

title: The Last Man
author: Mary Shelley
synopsis: In the late 21st century, a plague annihilates humanity, leaving one survivor to wander an emptied world.

title: The Skylark of Space
author: E. E. Smith
synopsis: Scientist-adventurers build a faster-than-light ship and battle a rival across the galaxy, in the foundational space opera.

title: We
author: Yevgeny Zamyatin
synopsis: In a regimented future of glass cities and numbered citizens, an engineer's forbidden love awakens his suppressed soul.

title: The House on the Borderland
author: William Hope Hodgson
synopsis: A recluse's found manuscript recounts cosmic visions and a siege by monstrous swine-things at a house over an abyss.

title: The Return of Tarzan
author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
synopsis: Tarzan returns to Africa, uncovering the lost city of Opar and reclaiming his birthright and his love, Jane.

title: The Warlord of Mars
author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
synopsis: John Carter pursues his captive princess across Barsoom to the frozen pole, completing the first Martian trilogy.

title: At the Earth's Core
author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
synopsis: Two explorers bore into the Earth and emerge in Pellucidar, a savage inner world lit by a central sun.

title: Robur the Conqueror
author: Jules Verne
synopsis: A mysterious inventor astonishes the world with a heavier-than-air flying machine, defying the champions of the balloon.

title: The Blue Castle
author: L. M. Montgomery
synopsis: A repressed spinster, told she has a year to live, throws off convention and finds love and freedom in the Muskoka wilds.

title: Prester John
author: John Buchan
synopsis: A young Scot in South Africa is caught up in a native uprising led by a charismatic preacher claiming an ancient kingship.

title: Mr. Standfast
author: John Buchan
synopsis: Richard Hannay goes undercover as a pacifist to hunt a German master-spy across Britain and the Western Front.

title: The Amateur Cracksman
author: E. W. Hornung
synopsis: The gentleman-thief A.J. Raffles and his companion Bunny pull off daring jewel robberies in late-Victorian London.

title: Piccadilly Jim
author: P. G. Wodehouse
synopsis: A dissolute young American reinvents himself through a tangle of impersonations and transatlantic romance.

title: A Damsel in Distress
author: P. G. Wodehouse
synopsis: An American composer falls for an English lady and blunders into the comic intrigues of an aristocratic country house.

title: Cane
author: Jean Toomer
synopsis: A lyrical mosaic of stories, sketches, and poems on Black life in the rural South and the urban North.

title: Three Soldiers
author: John Dos Passos
synopsis: Three American soldiers are ground down by the machinery of army life in the First World War.

title: The Rainbow Trail
author: Zane Grey
synopsis: A young minister journeys into the canyon country to free those walled away at the end of Riders of the Purple Sage.

title: The Posthumous Memoirs of Brás Cubas
author: Machado de Assis
synopsis: Narrating from beyond the grave, the dead Brazilian gentleman Brás Cubas recounts his idle, self-deceiving life — a failed political career, a long affair with the married Virgília, and a crackpot friend's philosophy of 'Humanitism' — in 160 short, digressive, ironic chapters that made the novel a founding work of modern Latin American fiction.

title: The Magus
author: John Fowles
synopsis: Nicholas Urfe, a disaffected young Englishman, takes a teaching post on a remote Greek island and falls under the spell of Maurice Conchis, a wealthy recluse who ensnares him in an ever-shifting psychological 'godgame' of masques, staged illusions, and erotic deceptions that leave him unable to tell performance from reality.

title: Death and the Dervish
author: Meša Selimović
synopsis: A Sufi dervish in Ottoman Bosnia is drawn into a spiral of fear, bureaucracy, and moral compromise after his brother is arrested — a meditation on power and conscience.

title: Demian
author: Hermann Hesse
synopsis: A young man's coming-of-age under the spell of his enigmatic friend Max Demian, who guides him beyond conventional morality toward self-knowledge.

title: Ego Is the Enemy
author: Ryan Holiday
synopsis: A Stoic-inflected argument, told through historical case studies, that ego sabotages ambition, success, and resilience.

title: Flaubert's Parrot
author: Julian Barnes
synopsis: A retired English doctor obsessively researches Gustave Flaubert's life, turning literary biography into a meditation on art, truth, and grief.

title: Justice: What's the Right Thing to Do?
author: Michael J. Sandel
synopsis: Michael Sandel's tour of the major theories of justice — utilitarian, libertarian, Kantian, Aristotelian — tested against hard moral dilemmas.

title: Life: A User's Manual
author: Georges Perec
synopsis: An intricate puzzle-novel anatomizing a Paris apartment building and its inhabitants, room by room, around one man's lifelong artistic project.

title: Looking for Alaska
author: John Green
synopsis: At an Alabama boarding school, Miles Halter falls in with the magnetic, self-destructive Alaska Young and must reckon with grief and meaning after tragedy.

title: Men Without Women
author: Haruki Murakami
synopsis: Seven stories of solitary men severed from the women in their lives, in Murakami's register of loss and quiet surrealism.

title: Siddhartha
author: Hermann Hesse
synopsis: A young Brahmin's spiritual journey through asceticism, sensual excess, and worldly wealth toward enlightenment beside a river.

title: Steve Jobs
author: Walter Isaacson
synopsis: Walter Isaacson's authorized biography of Apple's co-founder, tracing his genius, cruelty, and reinvention of multiple industries.

title: The Dutch House
author: Ann Patchett
synopsis: Two siblings, exiled from their childhood mansion by their stepmother, remain bound to the house and to each other across five decades.

title: The Fortress
author: Meša Selimović
synopsis: Returning from war to Ottoman Sarajevo, a poor scholar navigates poverty, love, and the perils of speaking truth to power.

title: The Goldfinch
author: Donna Tartt
synopsis: After surviving a bombing that kills his mother, Theo Decker clings to a stolen Dutch painting through years of grief, crime, and obsession.

title: The Golem and the Jinni
author: Helene Wecker
synopsis: In 1899 New York, a masterless golem and a freed jinni — two supernatural immigrants — forge an unlikely friendship across the city's quarters.

title: The Innovator's Dilemma
author: Clayton M. Christensen
synopsis: Clayton Christensen's analysis of why well-run companies fail when confronted by disruptive technologies.

title: The Night Circus
author: Erin Morgenstern
synopsis: A wondrous circus that appears only at night is the arena for a magical duel between two young illusionists bound since childhood — who fall in love.

title: The Nightingale
author: Kristin Hannah
synopsis: Two sisters in Nazi-occupied France take divergent paths of resistance and survival during World War II.

title: The Noise of Time
author: Julian Barnes
synopsis: A novel of composer Dmitri Shostakovich's life under Stalinist terror, and the compromises art makes to survive tyranny.

title: The Secret History
author: Donna Tartt
synopsis: A clique of elite classics students at a Vermont college is drawn into murder, narrated by an outsider reconstructing how it happened.

title: The Siege of Krishnapur
author: J. G. Farrell
synopsis: English colonists besieged during the 1857 Indian Mutiny watch their Victorian certainties crumble along with their fortifications.

title: The Tyranny of Merit
author: Michael J. Sandel
synopsis: Michael Sandel's critique of meritocracy, arguing that the rhetoric of deserving success has corroded the common good.

title: The Wind-Up Bird Chronicle
author: Haruki Murakami
synopsis: An ordinary Tokyo man's search for his missing wife spirals into a surreal descent through wells, war memories, and the unconscious.

title: The Anxious Generation
author: Jonathan Haidt
synopsis: Jonathan Haidt's argument that smartphones and overprotective parenting rewired childhood and drove a youth mental-health crisis.

title: Wandering
author: Hermann Hesse
synopsis: A slim collection of Hermann Hesse's lyrical prose sketches and poems on travel, nature, and the homelessness of the spirit.

title: What I Talk About When I Talk About Running
author: Haruki Murakami
synopsis: Haruki Murakami's memoir braiding long-distance running with the discipline of the writing life.

author: Roberto Bolaño
synopsis: Published posthumously, 2666 is a sprawling five-part novel that circles the fictional Mexican border city of Santa Teresa, where hundreds of women are being murdered. Its interlocking books follow four European critics chasing a reclusive German novelist, a widowed philosophy professor, an American sports journalist, the unsolved femicides themselves, and finally the life of the vanished writer Benno von Archimboldi. Bolaño weaves war, scholarship, and violence into a vast meditation on evil and literature.

title: The Forty Rules of Love
author: Elif Shafak
synopsis: Ella Rubinstein, a dissatisfied Massachusetts housewife newly working as a reader for a literary agency, is assigned to review a manuscript called Sweet Blasphemy and begins an unexpected correspondence with its author. That manuscript retells the thirteenth-century story of the transformative spiritual bond between the poet Rumi and the wandering dervish Shams of Tabriz, whose forty rules of love reshape everyone around them. As the two narratives mirror each other, Ella's own life is drawn open by the same questions.

title: Catch-22
author: Joseph Heller
synopsis: During World War II, U.S. Army Air Forces bombardier John Yossarian is stationed on the Mediterranean island of Pianosa, desperate to escape the endless combat missions his ambitious commanders keep raising. Caught in a web of absurd military bureaucracy epitomized by the paradoxical rule of Catch-22, he tries every ruse to be declared insane and sent home, only to discover the rule guarantees he never can be. Heller's darkly comic satire skewers the insanity of war, profiteering, and institutional logic.

title: Destiny Disrupted
author: Tamim Ansary
synopsis: A sweeping narrative history that retells the story of the world through Islamic eyes, tracing the Muslim world from the era before Muhammad through the caliphates, the great empires, and the collision with rising European power up to the present day. Ansary argues that Islamic and Western civilizations grew as two overlapping but distinct world-historical narratives, and shows how their intersection shaped the modern Middle East.

title: Fahrenheit 451
author: Ray Bradbury
synopsis: In a future America where books are outlawed and "firemen" burn any that are found, Guy Montag begins to question the emptiness of his censored, screen-saturated society. A chance meeting with a curious young neighbor and the memory of a woman who chooses to die with her books drive Montag to secretly read and ultimately rebel against the machinery of forgetting.

title: Ficciones
author: Jorge Luis Borges
synopsis: Ficciones is a landmark collection of short stories by the Argentine writer Jorge Luis Borges, gathering his most celebrated experiments in metaphysical fiction. Its tales conjure labyrinths, an infinite library, invented encyclopedias, and impossible books, blurring the line between erudite essay and narrative. The stories probe infinity, memory, identity, and the nature of fiction itself.

title: Norwegian Wood
author: Haruki Murakami
synopsis: Prompted by a Beatles song heard on a landing plane, Toru Watanabe looks back on his student years in late-1960s Tokyo and the two women who shaped them: the fragile, grief-haunted Naoko, tied to him by the suicide of a mutual friend, and the lively, unpredictable Midori. A quiet, melancholy coming-of-age story about love, memory, mental illness, and loss.

title: On Beauty
author: Zadie Smith
synopsis: Set in the fictional New England college town of Wellington, the novel follows the interracial, transatlantic Belsey family - British art historian Howard, his African-American wife Kiki, and their three children - as their lives entangle with those of their conservative intellectual rivals, the Kipps family. A comic campus novel about academia, marriage, race, and aesthetics, it consciously echoes the structure of E. M. Forster's Howards End.

title: The Autobiography of Malcolm X
author: Malcolm X
synopsis: Told to journalist Alex Haley, this memoir traces Malcolm X's life from a troubled Midwestern childhood and his years as a Harlem street hustler through his imprisonment, self-education, and conversion to the Nation of Islam. It follows his rise as a fiery minister and national spokesman, his bitter break with Elijah Muhammad, his transformative pilgrimage to Mecca, and his evolving views on race in the months before his assassination.

title: The Bastard of Istanbul
author: Elif Shafak
synopsis: Two families, the Turkish Kazancis in Istanbul and the Armenian-American Tchakhmakhchians in San Francisco, are bound together by a buried, painful history. When Armanoush secretly travels to Istanbul to trace her grandmother's past, her search entwines with the life of rebellious young Asya Kazanci, drawing both families toward long-hidden truths about memory, identity, and the legacy of the Armenian genocide.

title: The Code Breaker
author: Walter Isaacson
synopsis: A biography of biochemist Jennifer Doudna, tracing her path from a childhood fascination with DNA to her Nobel Prize-winning co-invention of CRISPR gene-editing. Isaacson uses her story to explore the collaborative and competitive nature of modern science, the race to develop CRISPR, and the profound ethical questions raised by the power to rewrite the code of life.

title: The Crying of Lot 49
author: Thomas Pynchon
synopsis: The Crying of Lot 49 follows Oedipa Maas, a young California woman named executor of her former lover's tangled real-estate estate. Sorting through his holdings, she stumbles onto clues pointing to Trystero, a secret centuries-old underground postal network, and finds herself unable to tell whether she has uncovered a vast conspiracy, an elaborate hoax, or her own unraveling mind.

title: The Origin of Consciousness in the Breakdown of the Bicameral Mind
author: Julian Jaynes
synopsis: Julian Jaynes argues that subjective consciousness is a learned cultural construct that emerged only about three thousand years ago, when an earlier "bicameral" mentality broke down. In the bicameral mind, he contends, people obeyed hallucinated voices they experienced as gods rather than reflecting on an inner self. Drawing on Greek epic, Mesopotamian and Egyptian records, and the neurology of the divided brain, he traces this transition through prophecy, hypnosis, poetry, and schizophrenia.

title: The Savage Detectives
author: Roberto Bolaño
synopsis: Roberto Bolaño's sprawling novel follows Arturo Belano and Ulises Lima, founders of an avant-garde poetry movement called visceral realism in 1970s Mexico City, as they set out in search of a vanished poet from an earlier generation. Told through a young acolyte's diary and dozens of testimonies gathered across two decades and several continents, it traces the wandering, often unraveling lives of its bohemian poets. It is at once a comic elegy for youthful literary ambition and a mystery about art, exile, and disappearance.

title: The White Tiger
author: Aravind Adiga
synopsis: In a series of letters addressed to the Chinese Premier, Balram Halwai, a driver-turned-entrepreneur, recounts his rise from a poor village in "the Darkness" of rural India to a self-made businessman in Bangalore. His darkly comic confession lays bare the corruption, servitude, and class violence of a rapidly modernizing India, and the ruthless act that freed him from the "Rooster Coop."

title: White Teeth
author: Zadie Smith
synopsis: Spanning several decades in multicultural North London, White Teeth follows the tangled lives of two wartime friends—the hapless Englishman Archie Jones and the Bengali Muslim Samad Iqbal—along with their wives and children. As the families collide with the eccentric, intellectual Chalfens, the novel traces questions of immigration, identity, faith, and inheritance across generations. Zadie Smith's debut is a sprawling comic portrait of race, history, and belonging in late-twentieth-century Britain.

title: The Name of the Rose
author: Umberto Eco
synopsis: In 1327 the Franciscan friar William of Baskerville and his young novice Adso of Melk arrive at a wealthy Benedictine abbey in northern Italy, where a gathering meant to debate Church doctrine is disrupted by a series of mysterious deaths. Applying reason and deduction, William investigates killings that lead him into the abbey's labyrinthine library and its jealously guarded secrets. The murders prove bound up with a forbidden book and the deadly question of whether laughter has any place in Christian life.

title: Narcissus and Goldmund
author: Hermann Hesse
synopsis: In a medieval German monastery, the ascetic young scholar Narcissus recognizes that his gifted student Goldmund is not made for the cloistered life of the mind, and sets him free to wander. Goldmund roams the countryside as a lover, artist, and vagabond, pursuing sensual experience and the mystery of a maternal, life-giving world, before the two friends reunite. The novel counterpolarizes intellect and art, spirit and nature, in a meditation on the paths a soul may take toward meaning.

title: An Immense World
author: Ed Yong
synopsis: Science journalist Ed Yong explores the concept of the Umwelt—the unique sensory bubble each species inhabits—to reveal how animals perceive a world utterly unlike our own. From the electric fields sensed by fish to the magnetic navigation of birds and the seismic hearing of elephants, the book surveys the astonishing range of animal senses. Along the way it argues for humility about the limits of human perception and for protecting the sensory worlds we increasingly threaten.

title: The Hero with a Thousand Faces
author: Joseph Campbell
synopsis: Joseph Campbell's comparative study of world mythology argues that the hero-tales of every culture share a single underlying structure, which he calls the 'monomyth': a cycle of departure, initiation, and return. Drawing on sources ranging from Greek epic and Norse saga to Freudian and Jungian psychology, he traces the archetypal journey of the hero across religions and eras. First published in 1949, it became a foundational text on myth, narrative, and the psychology of the unconscious.

title: The Power of Myth
author: Joseph Campbell
synopsis: A wide-ranging conversation between mythologist Joseph Campbell and journalist Bill Moyers, recorded for the acclaimed PBS television series. Across eight thematic dialogues, Campbell draws on world mythology, religion, art, and literature to explore how myths give shape to human life, and how their recurring symbols and hero journeys still speak to the modern world.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
synopsis: In a genetically engineered World State kept docile with soma and conditioning, an outsider and a young man raised on Shakespeare and pueblo ritual collide with a regime built on comfort at the cost of freedom, art, and God.

title: The Shadow of the Wind
author: Carlos Ruiz Zafón
synopsis: In 1945 Barcelona, young Daniel Sempere discovers a haunting novel by the obscure Julián Carax in the secret Cemetery of Forgotten Books and sets out to learn the truth behind Carax's life and death, only to find someone determined to burn every remaining copy of his work. A gothic mystery of books, love, and vengeance unfolds against the ruins of postwar and Civil War-era Spain.

title: The Marriage Plot
author: Jeffrey Eugenides
synopsis: A semiotics-obsessed English major navigates a love triangle with a charismatic but manic-depressive biology major and a spiritually searching friend during and after their senior year at Brown University in the early 1980s.

title: The Year of Magical Thinking
author: Joan Didion
synopsis: A memoir of the year following the sudden death of Didion's husband, John Gregory Dunne, at their dinner table, written while their daughter Quintana lay gravely ill. Didion anatomizes grief, memory, and the mind's 'magical thinking' as it resists the fact of loss.

title: Why Read the Classics
author: Italo Calvino
synopsis: A posthumous collection of Italo Calvino's literary essays, each a personal appreciation of a classic author or work — from Homer, Ovid and Pliny through Defoe, Voltaire, Stendhal, Dickens and Tolstoy to Hemingway, Borges and Queneau. It opens with his famous meditation on what makes a book a 'classic'.

title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
author: Gabriel García Márquez
synopsis: A multigenerational saga of the Buendía family in the mythical town of Macondo, blending magical realism with the cyclical rise, glory, and eventual ruin of a dynasty and its world.

title: The Master and Margarita
author: Mikhail Bulgakov
synopsis: The Devil and his chaotic retinue descend on officially atheist Stalinist Moscow, wreaking supernatural havoc on the city's corrupt literary establishment, while a disgraced writer known only as 'the Master' and his devoted lover Margarita fight to reclaim his suppressed novel about Pontius Pilate. Bulgakov's satirical masterpiece interweaves this dark comedy with the Master's own retelling of Christ's trial and crucifixion.

title: Foucault's Pendulum
author: Umberto Eco
synopsis: Three Milanese book editors invent "the Plan," a combinatorial occult conspiracy linking the Knights Templar to centuries of esoteric secret societies, only to find their private game of connections taking on a menacing life of its own.

title: Pale Fire
author: Vladimir Nabokov
synopsis: A 999-line poem by the fictional poet John Shade is presented with an extensive commentary by his self-appointed editor Charles Kinbote, whose annotations spiral away from the poem into his own delusional secret history as the exiled king of Zembla.

title: Cat's Cradle
author: Kurt Vonnegut
synopsis: A writer researching the day of the Hiroshima bombing is drawn into the orbit of the Hoenikker family, inventors of ice-nine, a doomsday substance that instantly freezes any liquid it touches. His trail leads to the island nation of San Lorenzo, where the population secretly practices the outlawed, self-admittedly false religion of Bokononism.

title: 2BR02B
author: Kurt Vonnegut
synopsis: In a future where science has cured aging and death, population-control law requires a volunteer to die for every birth; a father awaiting triplets confronts the bureaucracy of the euthanasia service.

title: 84, Charing Cross Road
author: Helene Hanff
synopsis: A twenty-year correspondence between a witty, book-hungry New York writer and the staff of a London antiquarian bookshop, sparked by her orders for out-of-print English classics and warmed over the years by parcels of rationed food sent in return.

title: Nine Stories
author: J. D. Salinger
synopsis: J. D. Salinger's only short story collection, gathering nine tales—including "A Perfect Day for Bananafish" and "For Esmé—with Love and Squalor"—that probe innocence, war trauma, and spiritual yearning beneath the polish of postwar American life.

title: Ajax Penumbra 1969
author: Robin Sloan
synopsis: A prequel novella to Mr. Penumbra's 24-Hour Bookstore: a young Ajax Penumbra arrives in 1969 San Francisco and takes a job at a strange, half-empty bookshop, drawn into the secret society of the Unbroken Spine and its search for a cipher devised by the real Renaissance printer Aldus Manutius.

title: Necessity
author: Jo Walton
synopsis: The final book of the Thessaly trilogy: decades after The Just City and The Philosopher Kings, the community has relocated across the galaxy to the planet Plato, where first contact with an alien civilization and the sudden death of the immortal Athene force the philosophers and their children to confront necessity itself.

title: The Maidens
author: Alex Michaelides
synopsis: A grieving group therapist investigates a series of ritualistic murders of students at Cambridge University, drawn into the orbit of a charismatic Classics professor and the secretive undergraduate society, the Maidens, obsessed with Greek tragedy.

title: Mr. President
author: Miguel Ángel Asturias
synopsis: A phantasmagoric portrait of an unnamed Latin American dictatorship, tracing a single week of terror and paranoia through the intertwined fates of a beggar, a general's daughter, and the tyrant's favorite, Angel Face. Written by future Nobel laureate Miguel Ángel Asturias, it is considered a foundational work of the Latin American dictator novel and magical realism.

title: The Library of Babel and Other Stories
author: Jorge Luis Borges
synopsis: A collection of Borges's metaphysical short fiction and essays — including the title story's infinite library of every possible book, an essay on the nature of Hell, and the labyrinthine parables "The Circular Ruins" and "The Babylonian Lottery."

title: Blue Nights
author: Joan Didion
synopsis: A memoir of aging, memory, and parenthood written in the aftermath of the death of Didion's daughter Quintana Roo, moving between Quintana's adoption, childhood, and wedding and Didion's own reckoning with mortality and failure as a mother.

title: Let Me Tell You What I Mean
author: Joan Didion
synopsis: A collection of twelve early Didion essays and pieces of journalism from 1968 to 2000, ranging from literary criticism of Hemingway to reflections on writing, self-doubt, and the craft of nonfiction, with a foreword by Hilton Als.

title: Play It as It Lays
author: Joan Didion
synopsis: A spare, fragmented novel following Maria Wyeth, a failed actress and model adrift in the freeways and vacant lots of 1960s Hollywood, as her marriage, an illegal abortion, and a friend's suicide push her toward emotional collapse.

title: Slouching Towards Bethlehem
author: Joan Didion
synopsis: Didion's first essay collection, anatomizing the countercultural unraveling of 1960s California — from Haight-Ashbury hippies and John Wayne to Joan Baez and a squalid Los Angeles murder — alongside personal essays like 'On Keeping a Notebook' and 'Goodbye to All That.'

title: South and West
author: Joan Didion
synopsis: A posthumously assembled pair of Didion's working notebooks: a 1970 road-trip diary through the Deep South's small towns and heat, and a shorter, unfinished 1976 notebook on California reckoning with the Patty Hearst trial.

title: The White Album
author: Joan Didion
synopsis: An essay collection capturing the fraying of late-1960s and 1970s California — the Manson murders, the Black Panthers, the Doors, shopping malls, and Didion's own migraines and breakdowns — as a portrait of a society coming apart.

title: Babel Tower
author: A. S. Byatt
synopsis: Set in 1960s Britain, the third novel of Byatt's Frederica Potter quartet follows Frederica through a bitter custody battle over her son while an obscenity trial unfolds around 'Babbletower,' a violent underground novel that echoes and comments on her own story.

title: Possession
author: A. S. Byatt
synopsis: Two contemporary literary scholars, Roland Michell and Maud Bailey, uncover a hidden love affair between two invented Victorian poets, Randolph Henry Ash and Christabel LaMotte, in a novel that interweaves modern academic detective work with the poets' own letters and verse.

title: The Djinn in the Nightingale's Eye
author: A. S. Byatt
synopsis: A collection of five fairy stories steeped in the Arabian Nights tradition; in the title novella, a narratology scholar at a conference in Istanbul frees a djinn from a bottle and is granted three wishes.

title: The Matisse Stories
author: A. S. Byatt
synopsis: Three loosely linked novellas — about a hairdresser, an art historian, and a childminder — each orbiting a painting by Henri Matisse and the way art reshapes ordinary lives.

title: Among Others
author: Jo Walton
synopsis: Told as a teenage diary, the novel follows Mori, a Welsh girl who can see fairies and has just used magic to stop her mother, a witch, at a terrible cost — now exiled to an English boarding school, she survives on a voracious diet of science fiction and fantasy novels.

title: Lent
author: Jo Walton
synopsis: A historical fantasy reimagining the life of the real Renaissance friar Girolamo Savonarola, who can see and banish demons in 1490s Florence — until he discovers he is trapped in a time loop, reliving his rise and execution again and again.

title: Franny and Zooey
author: J. D. Salinger
synopsis: Two connected novellas about the youngest members of the Glass family: Franny, having a spiritual crisis over a small book of Russian pilgrim prayers, and her brother Zooey, who tries to talk her back from the edge in a single long afternoon at their parents' apartment.

title: Mr. Penumbra's 24-Hour Bookstore
author: Robin Sloan
synopsis: An unemployed web designer takes a night-shift job at a strange San Francisco bookstore and is drawn into a secret society, the Unbroken Spine, obsessed with a five-hundred-year-old cipher devised by the real Renaissance printer Aldus Manutius.

title: The Sirens of Titan
author: Kurt Vonnegut
synopsis: The richest man in America, Malachi Constant, is swept into a cosmic scheme by the space-and-time-displaced Winston Niles Rumfoord, who reveals that all of human history has been secretly manipulated by an alien robot stranded on Saturn's moon Titan, waiting for a spare part.

title: I Have Some Questions for You
author: Rebecca Makkai
synopsis: A true-crime podcaster returns to teach a course at the elite boarding school she attended, where her roommate was murdered decades earlier, and finds herself reopening the case — and the question of whether the man convicted of the killing was guilty at all.

title: The Borrower
author: Rebecca Makkai
synopsis: A children's librarian discovers her favorite young patron has run away from his parents' anti-gay-conversion efforts and stowed away in her car; the two end up on an improvised, book-fueled road trip across America.

title: The Great Believers
author: Rebecca Makkai
synopsis: A dual-timeline novel moving between Chicago's art world during the devastating first years of the 1980s AIDS crisis and a mother's 2015 search through Paris for her estranged daughter, tracing how loss echoes across a generation.

title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
synopsis: A psychotherapist becomes obsessed with treating Alicia Berenson, a painter who shot her husband five times and has not spoken a single word since, and sets out to unravel why — and what her cryptic self-portrait titled 'Alcestis' really means.

title: If on a Winter's Night a Traveler
author: Italo Calvino
synopsis: A metafictional novel addressed to 'you, the Reader,' who keeps buying a book called If on a Winter's Night a Traveler only to find it interrupted after its opening chapter — again and again, across ten different invented novels-within-the-novel.

title: Invisible Cities
author: Italo Calvino
synopsis: Marco Polo describes fifty-five fantastical, impossible cities to the aging emperor Kublai Khan in a series of prose poems that are as much a meditation on memory, desire, and language as on any real city.

title: Alexander Hamilton
author: Ron Chernow
synopsis: A sweeping biography of Alexander Hamilton — from his illegitimate birth in the Caribbean through the Revolutionary War, the drafting of the Constitution and The Federalist Papers, his founding of the American financial system as Treasury Secretary, and his fatal duel with Aaron Burr.

title: Grant
author: Ron Chernow
synopsis: A biography of Ulysses S. Grant tracing his rise from failure and alcoholism to Union military genius in the Civil War and a presidency defined by his fierce, embattled defense of Reconstruction and Black civil rights.

title: Washington: A Life
author: Ron Chernow
synopsis: A comprehensive biography of George Washington, following him from surveyor and young officer through the Revolutionary War command and two terms as the first president, portraying the private uncertainties behind the marble-statue myth.

title: Team of Rivals
author: Doris Kearns Goodwin
synopsis: A group biography of Abraham Lincoln and the three rivals he defeated for the 1860 Republican nomination — Seward, Chase, and Bates — whom he then brought into his cabinet, and how that unlikely coalition steered the Union through the Civil War.

title: The Bully Pulpit
author: Doris Kearns Goodwin
synopsis: A joint biography of Theodore Roosevelt and William Howard Taft set against the rise of muckraking journalism at McClure's Magazine, tracing their friendship, political rupture, and the reform-era alliance between the presidency and the press.

title: Soldier of Arete
author: Gene Wolfe
synopsis: The second novel in Gene Wolfe's Latro series: an amnesiac mercenary who forgets everything each day and records his life on a scroll continues his wanderings through ancient Greece in the aftermath of the Persian Wars, guided by gods only he can see.

title: Soldier of the Mist
author: Gene Wolfe
synopsis: A mercenary named Latro, struck on the head at the Battle of Plataea, wakes each day with no memory of the day before and must reconstruct his life from a scroll he keeps — while seeing gods and spirits invisible to everyone else as he wanders through post-war Greece.

title: The Historian
author: Elizabeth Kostova
synopsis: A young woman discovers her father's hidden trove of letters hinting that the historical Dracula, Vlad the Impaler, still exists, and follows a decades-spanning trail of scholars, archives, and vampire lore across Cold War-era Europe to find him.

title: The Thirteenth Tale
author: Diane Setterfield
synopsis: A reclusive, dying novelist famous for a lifetime of invented backstories finally summons a bookseller's daughter to transcribe her true life story — a Gothic tale of twins, a burned house, and a governess that echoes Jane Eyre and Wuthering Heights.

title: The Library at Mount Char
author: Scott Hawkins
synopsis: A group of adopted children, raised by a god-like being called Father to each master a lethal discipline drawn from his cosmic Library, must work together to survive his disappearance and the collapse of the order he imposed on reality.

title: The Sense of an Ending
author: Julian Barnes
synopsis: A retired, comfortably unreflective man receives an unexpected bequest that forces him to reexamine his memory of a brilliant school friend's suicide decades earlier, and discovers how unreliable and self-serving his own account of the past has been.

title: The Storied Life of A. J. Fikry
author: Gabrielle Zevin
synopsis: A widowed, curmudgeonly independent bookseller on a small island finds his life transformed after an abandoned toddler is left in his store, forcing him back into connection with his customers, his community, and eventually love.

title: The Handmaid's Tale
author: Margaret Atwood
synopsis: In the totalitarian theocracy of Gilead, fertile women are stripped of their names and enslaved as 'Handmaids' to bear children for the ruling class; Offred narrates her constrained daily life and the fragments of resistance and memory that survive it.

title: An Unnecessary Woman
author: Rabih Alameddine
synopsis: Aaliya, a reclusive, divorced 72-year-old former bookseller in Beirut, has spent decades secretly translating great works of world literature into Arabic for no one but herself; over one Beirut winter she reflects on her life, literature, and the war-scarred city around her.

title: Hitch-22
author: Christopher Hitchens
synopsis: The memoir of essayist and polemicist Christopher Hitchens, tracing his English boyhood, his years as a Trotskyist journalist, his famous friendships and public feuds, and his ideological journey from the socialist left to a fierce, contrarian internationalism.

title: How to Be a Heroine
author: Samantha Ellis
synopsis: A memoir structured as a chapter-by-chapter rereading of the literary heroines Ellis grew up idolizing — Cathy Earnshaw, Jane Eyre, Scarlett O'Hara, Lizzy Bennet, Franny Glass, and more — and what they reveal about the life she's actually lived.

title: Matilda
author: Roald Dahl
synopsis: A brilliant, telekinetic five-year-old neglected by her crass parents finds an ally in her kind teacher Miss Honey and uses her wits — and eventually her powers — to take down the tyrannical headmistress Miss Trunchbull.

title: My Salinger Year
author: Joanna Rakoff
synopsis: A memoir of the year a young aspiring writer spent working at J. D. Salinger's staid literary agency in the 1990s, typing responses to his fan mail on a Selectric typewriter and slowly discovering both his novels and her own ambitions.

title: Just Kids
author: Patti Smith
synopsis: Patti Smith's memoir of her formative years in late-1960s and 1970s New York and her deep, enduring relationship with photographer Robert Mapplethorpe, from their poverty at the Chelsea Hotel to their emergence as artists.

title: The Anthropocene Reviewed
author: John Green
synopsis: A collection of personal essays reviewing facets of the human-centered planet — from Diet Dr Pepper and sunsets to the QWERTY keyboard and Halley's Comet — each rated on a five-star scale, adapted from Green's podcast of the same name.

title: The House of the Spirits
author: Isabel Allende
synopsis: A multigenerational, magical-realist saga of the Trueba family in an unnamed Latin American country, spanning from the clairvoyant Clara's girlhood through her granddaughter Alba's survival of a military coup and its aftermath.

title: The Perks of Being a Wallflower
author: Stephen Chbosky
synopsis: Told through a series of letters to an anonymous 'friend,' introverted freshman Charlie navigates his first year of high school, new friendships with seniors Sam and Patrick, first love, mental health struggles, and a repressed childhood trauma.

title: Flights
author: Olga Tokarczuk
synopsis: A fragmented novel-essay hybrid weaving together short fictional vignettes and essayistic digressions on travel, anatomy, and the preservation of the body — from a modern flight attendant to the real 17th-century anatomist Filip Verheyen and his amputated leg.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
synopsis: Stephen Hawking surveys the origin and fate of the universe — the Big Bang, black holes, and the nature of time — for readers with no background in physics.
